## Agent 2 — continued attack: vanishing versus residual control for \(R_{\lab}\)

We decomposed the labelled-realization mismatch as

\[
R_{\lab}
=
R_{\mathrm{norm}}
+
R_{\mathrm{whit}}
+
R_{\mathrm{proj}}
+
R_{\mathrm{trunc}}
+
R_{\mathrm{chart}}
+
R_{\mathrm{tail}}.
\]

The current task is to decide which pieces should vanish structurally in the \(Y12\)-grade, and which pieces require estimates.

The main refinement is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Most of }R_{\lab}\text{ should be killed by origin/label functoriality, not by analysis.}
}
\]

Only truncation/tail/completion-type pieces should require analytic residual estimates.

---

# 1. Structural-vanishing class

These terms should vanish in the \(Y12\)-graded quotient if the labelled transition is correctly defined.

## 1.1 Normal-form mismatch

\[
R_{\mathrm{norm}}
\]

should vanish if the raw transition transports a formed canonical source rather than recomputing \(N_0\).

The structural condition is:

\[
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}N_0(\xi)
=
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}N_0(\xi)
\]

on actual formed sources, modulo non-\(Y12\) signatures.

Equivalently,

\[
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{norm}}=0.
\]

If this fails, there are two possibilities.

### Benign failure

Raw transition recomputes \(N_0\), but the induced \(D^2N_0\)-piece lies in a fixed/coherent finite-dimensional family. Then it joins

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}.
\]

### Fatal failure

Raw transition recomputes \(N_0\) with locally free source-input variations, giving arbitrary primitive \(Y12\)-amplitude. Then the labelled \(Y12\) channel fails.

So \(R_{\mathrm{norm}}\) is the first structural audit.

---

## 1.2 Whitening mismatch

\[
R_{\mathrm{whit}}
\]

should vanish in the primitive \(Y12\)-grade if the quotient remembers the origin pair

\[
(o_N,o_W).
\]

Terms of the form

\[
DW[\xi]\,E_2(\eta)
\]

or

\[
D^2W[\xi_1,\xi_2]N_0
\]

are not primitive

\[
(D^2N_0,DW_{\same})
\]

terms. Therefore

\[
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{whit}}=0
\]

provided \(\Pi_Y\) tracks

\[
(o_N,o_W)=(D^2N_0,DW_{\same}).
\]

Thus whitening mismatch is safe unless the quotient forgets origin.

If origin is forgotten, whitening mismatch can pollute the \(Y12\)-grade and the route becomes suspect.

---

## 1.3 Projection mismatch

\[
R_{\mathrm{proj}}
\]

should vanish if projection/quotient is applied after labelled realization and is homogeneous with respect to the \(Y12\) filtration.

The condition:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

must be filtered near

\[
\Sigma(Y12).
\]

Then

\[
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{proj}}=0.
\]

If projection is applied too early, or if it collapses all degree-two same-point whitening terms, then

\[
R_{\mathrm{proj}}
\]

may kill \(Y12\) or identify it with non-\(Y12\) nuisance. That is an algebraic no-go, not an analytic estimate.

---

## 1.4 Chart curvature mismatch

\[
R_{\mathrm{chart}}
\]

should vanish in \(Y12\)-grade if chart transition is source-linear and does not recompute \(N_0\).

Coordinate curvature can be second order in chart parameters, but its origin is chart/coordinate, not primitive \(D^2N_0\).

Thus the expected result is:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{chart}}=0.
\]

It is dangerous only if the chart transition includes a nonlinear normal-form recomputation.

So \(R_{\mathrm{chart}}\) is controlled by the same structural condition as \(R_{\mathrm{norm}}\).

---

# 2. Analytic-residual class

These terms may not vanish structurally and therefore need estimates.

## 2.1 Truncation mismatch

\[
R_{\mathrm{trunc}}
\]

comes from finite order or finite carrier truncation.

The required estimate is:

\[
\sum_p
\|\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{trunc},p}\|^2
\le
Q^{-B}\mu(\mathcal C).
\]

But because \(\Pi_Y\) selects the primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin sector, much of the ordinary truncation error may vanish structurally.

Thus the actual theorem should be targeted:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{TruncCompletion}_{Y12}:
\sum_p
\|\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{trunc},p}\|^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu.
}
\]

This is weaker than full high-order truncation control.

---

## 2.2 Tail/completion mismatch

\[
R_{\mathrm{tail}}
\]

is the \(E_2\)-type contribution.

Classify:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{tail}}
\]

as:

1. absent;
2. fixed basepoint;
3. finite-dimensional coherent;
4. residual-controlled;
5. locally free.

Only case 5 is fatal.

For cases 2 and 3, define a coherent amplitude space

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\tail}
\]

and include it in

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}.
\]

For case 4, prove

\[
\sum_p\|\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{tail},p}^{\small}\|^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

Thus the analytic work concentrates on:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{Y12}
}
\]

not full \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{K9}\).

---

# 3. Minimal theorem package

The labelled-realization consistency theorem can be split into four algebraic statements and two analytic estimates.

## Algebraic statements

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{norm}}
\in \mathcal C_Y^{\mathrm{norm}}
\text{ with }\dim\le Q^C
\text{ or }0.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{whit}}=0.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{proj}}=0.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{chart}}=0.
}
\]

The strongest expected outcome is all four are zero except possibly a fixed finite coherent \(R_{\mathrm{norm}}\).

## Analytic estimates

\[
\boxed{
\sum_p\|\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{trunc},p}\|^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\sum_p
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{tail},p},
\mathcal C_Y^{\tail}
\right)^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu.
}
\]

Then

\[
\Pi_YR_{\lab}
\in
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
+
\text{small},
\]

where

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
=
\mathcal C_Y^{\mathrm{norm}}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\tail}
+
\cdots
\]

is finite-dimensional/block-coherent and not locally free.

---

# 4. Local-free test for the analytic residuals

For any coherent space

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\bullet},
\]

we must verify it is not locally free.

The block criterion:

For each block \(\Omega\),

\[
\dim \mathcal C_Y^{\bullet}|_{\Omega}
\le Q^C
\]

while the number of atoms in \(\Omega\) may be much larger.

If instead

\[
\dim \mathcal C_Y^{\bullet}|_{\Omega}
\asymp |\Omega|,
\]

then it is extraction-local and fatal.

Thus analytic residuals are acceptable only if their coherent pieces have low complexity.

This applies equally to:

\[
E_2^Y,
\quad
R_{\lab}^Y,
\quad
\text{parameter graph shifts}.
\]

---

# 5. Consequence for the wall theorem

After the labelled-realization theorem, define

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}
=
\mathcal C_Y^{E_2}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\param}.
\]

The wall amplitude is

\[
F_Y
=
H\left((\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right).
\]

The usable wall is

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_{\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}})F_Y.
\]

The downstream lower requires:

\[
\|F_Y^{\eff}\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\|F_Y\|_{L^2(\nu)}^2.
\]

If this fails, the wall amplitude is absorbed by coherent actual-row effects, even if fiberwise \(Y12\) is normal.

This is the amplitude Schur problem.

---

# 6. Practical audit order

The fastest way to proceed is:

## Step 1: algebraic vanishings

Check:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{whit}}=0,
\quad
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{proj}}=0,
\quad
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{chart}}=0.
\]

If any fail due to origin overcollapse, refine labels or abandon \(Y12\).

## Step 2: normal-form factorization

Check whether raw transition transports formed source or recomputes \(N_0\).

If transports:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{norm}}=0.
\]

If recomputes:

classify \(\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{norm}}\) as finite coherent or locally free.

## Step 3: targeted residual estimates

Prove:

\[
\mathrm{TruncCompletion}_{Y12}
\]

and

\[
\mathrm{TailCompletion}_{Y12}.
\]

## Step 4: coherent-space dimension

For all coherent pieces, prove low complexity/block coherence.

## Step 5: amplitude Schur

Prove

\[
F_Y\notin\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}.
\]

---

# 7. Updated no-go conditions

The labelled-realization route fails if:

1. \(R_{\mathrm{norm}}\) is locally free in \(Y12\);
2. origin is not preserved through whitening/projection/chart realization;
3. truncation/tail residuals have uncontrolled \(Y12\)-norm;
4. coherent spaces are extraction-local;
5. amplitude Schur fails.

Only the first two are structural failures. The latter three are analytic/model-space failures.

---

# 8. Final theorem form

A mature theorem statement:

**LabelledRealizationConsistency\(_{Y12}\).** There exists a decomposition

\[
R_{\lab}
=
R_{\mathrm{alg}}

for \(R_{\mathrm{trunc}},R_{\mathrm{tail}}\).

## Agent 2 — continued attack: pre-extraction fixing of the \(Y12\) coherent amplitude space

The labelled-realization consistency package now has the form

R_{\lab}
=

+
R_{\mathrm{small}}

R_{\mathrm{coh}},

and

\Pi_YR_{\mathrm{coh}}\in\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}.

Together with the \(E_2^Y\) and actual-parameter coherent pieces, this gives a finite-dimensional coherent amplitude space

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}
=
\mathcal C_Y^{E_2}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\param}.
\]

The key issue now is **when** this space is chosen.

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}\text{ must be fixed under a smooth pre-extraction measure.}
}

If it is fitted after selecting a hard good subset, it can absorb the wall amplitude artificially.

---

# 1. Why post-extraction fitting is invalid

The effective wall amplitude is

\[
F_Y
=
H\left((\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right)
=
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

If, after extracting a set \(G\), we define

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}(G)
\]

using the behavior of \(F_Y\) on \(G\), a finite-dimensional space can become artificially strong.

For example, if \(G\) has only \(M\) atoms and we allow a dimension \(M\) coherent space on that set, then

\[
F_Y|_G\in \mathcal C_Y^{\actual}(G)
\]

trivially.

So the amplitude Schur statement

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
\left(F_Y,\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}\right)
\ge Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|_{L^2(\nu)}
\]

must be established **before** hard capping/extraction, under a smooth measure \(\nu_{\bd}\).

---

# 2. Correct order

The correct downstream order is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{labelled transition and }Y12\text{ quotient}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{define }\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}\text{ from actual-source/coherent data}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{prove amplitude Schur under smooth pre-extraction measure }\nu_{\bd}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{form }F_Y^{\eff}=(I-P_{\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}}^{\bd})F_Y
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{use }|F_Y^{\eff}|^2\text{ in the smooth O2/O3 aggregate}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{only then cap and extract pointwise-good centers}.
}
\]

This matches the broader order already forced by O2/O3:

\[
\text{smooth aggregate}
\to
\text{branch survival}
\to
\text{capped extraction}
\to
\text{atomic contradiction}.
\]

---

# 3. Define the projection under \(\nu_{\bd}\)

Let

\[
\nu_{\bd}
\]

be the smooth pre-extraction measure used for the joint aggregate. It should include smooth versions of Gate–1 and wall weights, not capped indicators.

Define the orthogonal projection

\[
P_Y^{\bd}
:
L^2(\nu_{\bd})\to \mathcal C_Y^{\actual}.
\]

Then

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_Y^{\bd})F_Y.
\]

The wall energy for aggregate purposes is

\[
E_{\wall}^{Y,\eff}
=
|F_Y^{\eff}|^2.
\]

Not

\[
|F_Y|^2
\]

if

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}\neq0.
\]

The amplitude Schur theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\|F_Y^{\eff}\|_{L^2(\nu_{\bd})}^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\|F_Y\|_{L^2(\nu_{\bd})}^2.
}
\]

This is the correct “no absorption by coherent amplitudes” statement.

---

# 4. Extraction after projection

Once

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
\]

is fixed, define a capped wall factor

\[
F_{\wall}^{Y,\eff}(m)
=
\min\left(
\frac{|F_Y^{\eff}(m)|^2}{\eta_{\wall}},
1
\right).
\]

Then extraction uses

\[
F_1F_{23}F_{\wall}^{Y,\eff}.
\]

This ensures that the extracted centers have local wall energy **after coherent amplitude subtraction**.

Without this, one could extract centers where

\[
F_Y
\]

is large but entirely coherent, which gives no contradiction against the actual-row upper.

---

# 5. Atomic upper after amplitude projection

The actual-row upper becomes

\[
\sum_p
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
\Pi_YJ_{\sing,p}(q''_{\can}),
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}
\right)^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu(\mathcal C).
\]

Equivalently, with the pre-fixed projection,

\[
\sum_p
\left|
(I-P_Y^{\bd})
\Pi_YJ_{\sing,p}(q''_{\can})
\right|^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu(\mathcal C),
\]

up to the bookkeeping needed when \(P_Y^{\bd}\) is global or block-global.

The D4 lower must be for the same projected channel:

\[
(I-P_Y^{\bd})
\Pi_YD_{\D4}.
\]

Thus the forbidden operator is effectively

\[
\Lambda_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_Y^{\bd})\Pi_YT_{\tr\to\sing}P_{\tr}.
\]

This is the true downstream \(\Lambda\) after coherent amplitude subtraction.

---

# 6. Locality regimes for \(\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}\)

There are three regimes.

## Regime A: \(\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}=0\)

Then

\[
F_Y^{\eff}=F_Y.
\]

The contradiction remains local/atomic.

This is the cleanest case.

## Regime B: block-local finite coherent space

Suppose

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}
=
\bigoplus_{\omega}\mathcal C_{Y,\omega}^{\actual}
\]

over blocks \(\omega\), with

\[

\]

\[

F_Y|_{\omega},

## Regime C: global finite coherent space

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}

is global finite-dimensional, the projection couples all atoms.

This can still work, but the final contradiction is a global Schur-energy contradiction, not a pointwise atomic contradiction.

Need a global upper and lower in the same \(L^2(\nu_{\bd})\) space.

## Regime D: extraction-local coherent space

If the coherent space can vary freely on each extraction atom, then

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}
\]

is locally free and fatal.

F_Y^{\eff},

not from

\[

If \(P_Y^{\bd}\) introduces high branch-charge complexity, the bounded-charge O2/O3 route may fail.

Thus amplitude projection has to be checked for branch-degree stability:

\[

P_Y^{\bd}
\text{ must not increase branch-charge bandwidth beyond }O(1).
}
\]

This is a new explicit condition.

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G.

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}\neq0,

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_Y^{\bd})
\left[
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)
\right].
\]

or

before extraction and prove

and

## Agent 2 — continued attack: avoiding circularity in the amplitude projection

The current downstream object is

\[
F_Y
=
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G),
\]

and if coherent \(Y12\)-amplitude spaces exist, the effective wall is

\[

=
(I-P_Y^{\bd})F_Y.

\[
\boxed{
P_Y^{\bd}\text{ cannot be defined using a measure that already depends on }F_Y^{\eff}.
}
\]

If it is, the wall projection is circular.

---

# 1. The circularity problem

The smooth pre-extraction weight was described schematically as

\[
W_{\bd}
=
N_{\sm}|H|^2|\ell_{\eff}(\Gamma)|^2.
\]

But

\[
\ell_{\eff}
\]

is defined by projecting

\[
F_Y
\]

away from

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}.
\]

If the projection

\[
P_Y^{\bd}
\]

is itself defined in

\[
L^2(W_{\bd}),
\]

then

\[
P_Y^{\bd}
\]

depends on

\[
F_Y^{\eff},
\]

which depends on

\[
P_Y^{\bd}.
\]

That is not a clean theorem. It risks defining the wall by a fixed point rather than by an independently specified forbidden channel.

So the amplitude projection must be defined using a **base smooth measure** that is independent of the projected wall.

---

# 2. Define a wall-independent base measure

Let

\[
d\nu_0
\]

be the smooth pre-wall measure built from the parts already secured before the \(Y12\) wall:

\[
d\nu_0
=
N_{\sm}\,d\rho
\]

or, after Gate/O2O3 smooth weights are available,

\[
d\nu_0
=
W_{1,23}^{\sm}\,N_{\sm}\,d\rho.

The key condition is:

\[
\boxed{
d\nu_0\text{ does not depend on }F_Y^{\eff}.
}
\]

Define the coherent projection

\[
P_Y^0:
L^2(\nu_0)\to \mathcal C_Y^{\actual}.
\]

Then define

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_Y^0)F_Y.
\]

Only after this is fixed do we form the wall weight

\[
W_{\wall}^{Y}
=
|F_Y^{\eff}|^2.
\]

Now the downstream weight can be

\[
W_{\bd}^{Y}
=
N_{\sm}|F_Y^{\eff}|^2
\]

or

\[
W_{\bd}^{Y}
=
W_{1,23}^{\sm}N_{\sm}|F_Y^{\eff}|^2,
\]

but the projection was already fixed.

This removes circularity.

---

# 3. Base-measure Schur theorem

The amplitude non-absorption theorem should be stated under \(d\nu_0\):

\[
\boxed{
\|(I-P_Y^0)F_Y\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2
\ge
Q^{-A}
\|F_Y\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2.
}
\]

This is the amplitude Schur lower bound.

It says the wall amplitude is not coherently absorbed **before** hard extraction and before using the wall as a weight.

Then the wall aggregate lower can use

\[
|F_Y^{\eff}|^2
\]

as an honest, fixed function.

---

# 4. Transfer from \(L^2(\nu_0)\) to wall-weighted extraction

After defining

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
\]

under \(L^2(\nu_0)\), the extraction stage needs a polynomial-mass set where

\[
|F_Y^{\eff}|^2
\]

is pointwise polynomially non-small.

This is standard capped extraction.

Define

\[
F_{\wall}^{Y}(m)
=
\min\left(
\frac{|F_Y^{\eff}(m)|^2}{\eta_Y},
1
\right).
\]

If

\[
\int F_{\wall}^{Y}\,d\nu_0
\ge Q^{-A}\nu_0(\mathcal C),
\]

then one extracts a subset where

\[
|F_Y^{\eff}(m)|^2\ge Q^{-A}.
\]

The amplitude Schur theorem gives an \(L^2\) lower bound, but to convert it into the capped lower bound we also need a polynomial upper bound:

\[
|F_Y^{\eff}(m)|^2\le Q^C
\]

on the routed set, or a dyadic truncation.

Thus add:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WallUpper}_{Y}:
|F_Y^{\eff}|^2\le Q^C
\text{ after discarding negligible mass.}
}
\]

Then

\[
\|F_Y^{\eff}\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2\ge Q^{-A}\nu_0(\mathcal C)
\]

implies a capped wall aggregate with polynomial loss.

---

# 5. Interaction with O2/O3

O2/O3 determinant survival uses a smooth bounded-degree pre-extraction weight. With the corrected order, that weight should be

\[
W_{\bd}^{Y}
=
N_{\sm}|F_Y^{\eff}|^2
\]

where

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
\]

has already been defined using

\[
P_Y^0.
\]

Thus the branch-charge bandwidth is computed from this fixed effective wall:

\[
d_0
=
d_N+2d_{Y,\eff},
\]

or in the previous notation,

\[
d_0=d_N+2d_H+2d_{\Gamma,\eff},
\]

where

\[
d_{Y,\eff}
\]

is the branch-charge degree of

\[
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G)
\]

after applying the fixed projection

\[
I-P_Y^0.
\]

If

\[
P_Y^0
\]

is global or high-complexity, it may destroy bounded branch-charge.

So we need:

\[
\boxed{
P_Y^0\text{ preserves bounded branch-charge complexity.}
}
\]

More concretely:

\[
(I-P_Y^0)F_Y
\]

must still lie in a branch-charge band of size

\[
O(1)
\]

or at worst within the conservative bound

\[
d_{\max}.
\]

If projection introduces high-frequency branch-charge components, the bounded-charge O2/O3 route fails.

---

# 6. Coherent amplitude spaces must be low-complexity

The projection

\[
P_Y^0
\]

is safe if

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}
\]

is spanned by low-complexity functions:

\[
\phi_j
\]

whose branch-charge degrees are bounded by \(O(1)\), or by a known finite \(d_{\max}\).

Then

\[
P_Y^0F_Y
\]

is a finite combination of low-complexity functions, and

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
\]

remains bounded-degree.

But if

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}
\]

contains high branch-charge or atom-local functions, then:

1. amplitude Schur may become false;
2. extraction may be fitted away;
3. O2/O3 branch survival loses bounded-charge structure.

Therefore the condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}
\text{ is finite-dimensional and branch-charge low-complexity under }d\nu_0.
}
\]

This is stronger than “finite-dimensional.” It must be low-complexity in the branch-charge algebra relevant to O2/O3.

---

# 7. Corrected downstream sequence

The mature sequence is now:

## Step 1 — labelled/fiber stage

Construct

\[
y_Y,\quad s_7,\quad s_9^Y,\quad \rho_Y.
\]

Obtain

\[
F_Y
=
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

## Step 2 — coherent amplitude model

Define

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}
\]

from \(E_2^Y\), labelled-realization residuals, and actual parameter amplitude families.

This must be done independently of extraction.

## Step 3 — base projection

Choose wall-independent

\[
d\nu_0.
\]

Define

\[
P_Y^0:L^2(\nu_0)\to\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}.
\]

Set

\[
F_Y^{\eff}=(I-P_Y^0)F_Y.
\]

## Step 4 — amplitude Schur

Prove

\[
\|F_Y^{\eff}\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2
\ge Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2.
\]

## Step 5 — bounded-degree audit

Prove

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
\]

has branch-charge bandwidth \(\le d_{\Gamma,\eff}\).

## Step 6 — O2/O3 smooth aggregate

Use

\[
|F_Y^{\eff}|^2
\]

as the wall part of the smooth O2/O3 weight.

## Step 7 — capped extraction

After aggregate Gate/O2/O3/wall survival, cap and extract.

This sequence avoids circularity.

---

# 8. New possible failure mode: projection destroys pointwise wall

Even if

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
\]

has \(L^2(\nu_0)\) mass, it might be highly oscillatory or concentrated. Extraction needs pointwise polynomial mass.

This is handled by:

1. dyadic upper truncation;
2. capped extraction;
3. local packing.

But if

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
\]

is supported on a tiny set of \(\nu_0\)-measure due to projection artifacts, then it may not give enough extracted centers.

So add a distribution condition:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{WallSpread}_{Y}:
F_Y^{\eff}\text{ is not concentrated on a super-polynomially tiny subset.}
}
\]

A polynomial \(L^\infty/L^2\) bound is sufficient.

---

# 9. Relation to atomic contradiction

If

\[
P_Y^0
\]

is global, the final forbidden upper/lower is not strictly atomwise. It is a projected global or block energy:

\[
\|(I-P_Y^0)\Pi_YJ_{\sing}(q''_{\can})\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2.
\]

The contradiction remains Type-I positive in the Hilbert-space sense, but not purely local atom-by-atom.

If this causes trouble, choose blocks \(\omega\) and define

\[
P_{Y,\omega}^0
\]

blockwise. Then the final energy is

\[
\sum_\omega
\|(I-P_{Y,\omega}^0)\Pi_YJ_{\sing}(q''_{\can})\|_{L^2(\nu_{0,\omega})}^2.
\]

This restores block-local atomicity.

Thus if coherent spaces are nonzero, a blockwise projection may be preferable.

---

# 10. Final Agent 2 update

The amplitude projection cannot be defined using the wall-weighted measure that it itself creates.

The correct object is:

\[
\boxed{
F_Y^{\eff}=(I-P_Y^0)F_Y
}
\]

where

\[
P_Y^0
\]

is fixed under a wall-independent smooth measure

\[
\nu_0.
\]

Then \(F_Y^{\eff}\) feeds:

1. the wall factor for extraction;
2. the bounded-degree O2/O3 weight;
3. the final projected forbidden upper/lower.

This prevents post-extraction fitting and removes a circular definition of the effective wall.

# 1. Labelled expression trees

(2,D^2N_0,DW_{\same},\mathrm{mixed}\ 12,\mathrm{same},1,\pi_Y,9,[\mathsf C_{Y12}]).

from terms such as

DW[\xi]\,E_2(\eta),

and

\partial_\alpha^2(\mathrm{EF\ atom}).

Those terms may be degree-two and whitening-bearing, but they are not primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin \(Y12\).

# 2. Decomposition of the realization mismatch

## 2.1 \(R_{\norm}\): normal-form mismatch

\[

measures the difference between:

\[
\text{transporting an already formed }N_0\text{-carrier}

\[
\text{recomputing }N_0\text{ after transition}.
\]

If raw transition is transport-of-formed-source, then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\norm}=0.
}
\]

If raw transition recomputes \(N_0\), then source-linear data can generate new

\[
D^2N_0[\delta\xi_1,\delta\xi_2]
\]

terms. Then one must classify

\[
\Pi_YR_{\norm}
\]

as finite coherent or locally free.

The fatal case is:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\norm,p}=a_p\,y_Y(p)
\]

with \(a_p\) arbitrary at extraction scale.

So the structural audit is:

\[
\boxed{
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}
\text{ transports formed canonical source expressions, not raw variables for recomputation.}
}
\]

---

## 2.2 \(R_{\whit}\): whitening mismatch

\[
R_{\whit}
\]

contains the difference between labelled same-point whitening and the raw singular whitening convention.

Terms like

\[
DW[\xi]\,E_2(\eta)
\]

or

\[
D^2W[\xi_1,\xi_2]N_0
\]

can occur, but these have origin pairs

\[
(\mathrm{EF\text{-}linear},DW)
\]

or

\[
(N_0,D^2W),
\]

not

\[
(D^2N_0,DW_{\same}).
\]

Therefore, if the singular quotient preserves the origin-pair grading,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\whit}=0.
}
\]

If the quotient forgets the origin-pair and only remembers “degree two with whitening,” this piece may contaminate the \(Y12\)-channel. That is quotient overcollapse, not an analytic tail problem.

---

## 2.3 \(R_{\proj}\): projection/quotient mismatch

\[
R_{\proj}
\]

is caused by applying projection or nuisance quotient before the labelled decomposition is realized.

The safe condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ is filtered near }\Sigma(Y12).
}
\]

Then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\proj}=0.
}
\]

If

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

kills or identifies all same-point degree-two whitening terms, then

\[
y_Y=0
\]

in the realized quotient. That is a rank-zero no-go for the current \(Y12\) channel.

---

## 2.4 \(R_{\chart}\): chart curvature mismatch

\[
R_{\chart}
\]

comes from coordinate nonlinearities and re-expansion curvature.

If chart transition is source-linear and does not recompute \(N_0\), chart curvature has origin

\[
\text{chart/coordinate},
\]

not

\[
D^2N_0.
\]

So the expected result is

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\chart}=0.
}
\]

It is dangerous only if chart transition includes a nonlinear normal-form recomputation.

Thus \(R_{\chart}\) is controlled by the same structural condition as \(R_{\norm}\).

---

## 2.5 \(R_{\trunc}\): truncation mismatch

\[
R_{\trunc}
\]

is a finite-order truncation error. It need not vanish structurally.

Required estimate:

\[
\boxed{
\sum_p\|\Pi_YR_{\trunc,p}\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu.
}
\]

This is a targeted \(Y12\)-sector truncation theorem, not full \(K_9\)-completion.

---

## 2.6 \(R_{\tail}\): tail/completion mismatch

\[
R_{\tail}
\]

is the \(E_2\)-type completion/tail component.

It must be classified as:

\[
\text{absent/fixed/finite-coherent/residual-controlled/locally free}.
\]

Acceptable:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\tail}\in \mathcal C_Y^{\tail}+\text{small},
\]

with

\[
\dim \mathcal C_Y^{\tail}|_{\Omega}\le Q^C
\]

on relevant blocks.

Fatal:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\tail}
\]

is extraction-locally free in the primitive \(Y12\) direction.

---

# 3. Algebraic versus analytic parts

The theorem should separate:

## Algebraic/origin-functorial part

Expected vanishings:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\whit}=0,
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\proj}=0,
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\chart}=0,
}
\]

and, in the best case,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\norm}=0.
}
\]

If \(R_{\norm}\neq0\), it must be finite coherent, not locally free.

## Analytic residual part

Need:

\[
\boxed{
\sum_p\|\Pi_YR_{\trunc,p}\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu,
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\sum_p
\operatorname{dist}
\left(
\Pi_YR_{\tail,p},
\mathcal C_Y^{\tail}
\right)^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu.
}
\]

Then

\[
\Pi_YR_{\lab}
\in
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
+
\text{small},
\]

with

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
=
\mathcal C_Y^{\norm}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\tail}
+
\cdots.
\]

---

# 4. Central transition modes

Central O2/O3 routing needs source-linear modes

\[
\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y]
\]

in the transition chart, with \(d_{\max}\) currently used as a conservative cap.

The labelled theorem must guarantee:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
(\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y]\text{-source-linear modes})
=0.
}
\]

And the raw/labelled mismatch on these modes must satisfy:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y
\left(
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
-
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}
\right)
(\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y]\text{-source-linear modes})
}
\]

is zero, residual-small, or finite coherent, but not locally free.

This theorem is the compatibility bridge between central O2/O3 enrichment and \(Y12\)-normality.

---

# 5. Local-free criterion

For any coherent labelled-realization space

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab},
\]

use the block criterion:

\[
\boxed{
\dim \mathcal C_Y^{\lab}|_{\Omega}\le Q^C
}
\]

for each relevant block \(\Omega\).

If

\[
\dim \mathcal C_Y^{\lab}|_{\Omega}\asymp |\Omega|,
\]

then \(\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}\) is extraction-local and fatal.

This criterion should also be used for

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{E_2}
\]

and parameter-amplitude coherent spaces.

---

# 6. Consequence for the effective wall

After LabelledRealizationConsistency\(_{Y12}\), define

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}
=
\mathcal C_Y^{E_2}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\param}
+\cdots.
\]

The fiber-level graph gives

\[
F_Y
=
H\left((\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right)
=
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}=0,
\]

this is the wall amplitude.

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}\neq0,
\]

the usable wall is the Schur residual

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu)}
(F_Y,\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}).
\]

Thus labelled-realization coherent mismatch becomes part of the amplitude Schur layer, not the fiber layer.

---

# 7. Mature theorem statement

**LabelledRealizationConsistency\(_{Y12}\).** There exists a decomposition

\[
R_{\lab}=R_{\alg}+R_{\small}+R_{\coh}
\]

such that:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\alg}=0,
\]

\[
\sum_p\|\Pi_YR_{\small,p}\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu,
\]

and

\[
\Pi_YR_{\coh}\in\mathcal C_Y^{\lab},
\]

where

\[
\dim\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}|_{\Omega}\le Q^C
\]

on all relevant blocks.

The same statement holds for source-linear central modes up to degree \(d_{\max}\).

Then labelled transition is compatible with the actual singular row and with central O2/O3 routing.

---

# 8. No-go cases

The labelled route fails if:

1. \(R_{\norm}\) recomputes \(N_0\) with locally free primitive \(Y12\)-amplitude;

2. \(R_{\whit}\) or \(R_{\proj}\) shows the quotient forgets the origin-pair;

3. \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\) kills the \(Y12\)-sector;

4. central source-linear modes become \(Y12\)-visible;

5. \(R_{\trunc}\) or \(R_{\tail}\) has uncontrolled \(Y12\)-norm;

6. \(\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}\) is extraction-local.

---

# Final Agent 2 conclusion

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{LabelledRealizationConsistency}_{Y12}
\text{ is mostly algebraic, not mostly analytic.}
}
\]

The algebraic part is origin/label functoriality:

\[
R_{\norm},R_{\whit},R_{\proj},R_{\chart}
\]

should vanish or be finite coherent in the \(Y12\)-grade.

The analytic part is targeted:

\[
R_{\trunc},R_{\tail}
\]

require only \(Y12\)-sector residual control.

If this package holds, then the labelled \(Y12\)-quotient applies to the actual singular row and remains compatible with the transition basis needed for bounded-charge O2/O3.

## Agent 2 — continued attack: contradiction-ready \(Y12\) operator

The labelled-realization package now says:

\[
R_{\lab}=R_{\alg}+R_{\small}+R_{\coh},
\]

with

\[
\Pi_YR_{\alg}=0,
\]

\[
\sum_p\|\Pi_YR_{\small,p}\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu,
\]

and

\[
\Pi_YR_{\coh}\in \mathcal C_Y^{\lab}.
\]

The next step is to define the actual forbidden operator used in the final upper/lower comparison. The key is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{The upper and lower must be measured after the same }Y12\text{-projection and coherent-amplitude subtraction.}
}
\]

Otherwise we risk proving a lower bound in a labelled channel while the upper applies to a different raw singular channel.

---

# 1. Define the raw \(Y12\)-projected operator

For each patch \(p\), define

\[
\Lambda_{Y,p}^{0}
=
\Pi_{Y,p}
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing,p}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing,p}^{\lab}
P_{\tr,p}.
\]

This maps transition carrier/source data into the primitive \(Y12\)-graded singular sector.

The actual raw singular row satisfies

\[
\Lambda_{Y,p}^{0}J_{\tr,p}(q''_{\can})
=
\Pi_{Y,p}T_{\tr\to\sing,p}^{\raw}J_{\tr,p}(q''_{\can})
+
\Pi_YR_{\lab,p}.
\]

Using labelled-realization consistency,

\[
\Pi_YR_{\lab,p}
=
c_p+s_p,
\]

where

\[
c=(c_p)_p\in \mathcal C_Y^{\lab},
\]

and

\[
\sum_p\|s_p\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

Thus the raw labelled operator upper is not

\[
\sum_p\|\Lambda_{Y,p}^{0}J_{\tr,p}(q''_{\can})\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu
\]

unless \(\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}=0\). In general, it is an upper **modulo** coherent amplitudes.

---

# 2. Define the coherent amplitude space once

Let

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}
=
\mathcal C_Y^{E_2}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\param}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\mathrm{graph}}
\]

be the full coherent \(Y12\)-amplitude space.

Here:

* \(\mathcal C_Y^{E_2}\) comes from fixed/finite coherent \(E_2^Y\);
* \(\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}\) comes from labelled-realization mismatch;
* \(\mathcal C_Y^{\param}\) comes from allowed actual-source amplitude families;
* \(\mathcal C_Y^{\mathrm{graph}}\) includes fixed basepoint or graph offsets such as \(\eta_{\fixed}\).

This space must be chosen before hard extraction, under a wall-independent smooth measure

\[
\nu_0.
\]

Define the projection

\[
P_Y^0:L^2(\nu_0)\to \mathcal C_Y^{\actual}.
\]

Then define the contradiction-ready operator

\[
\boxed{
\Lambda_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_Y^0)\Lambda_Y^0.
}
\]

This is the actual finite \(\Lambda\) produced by the current architecture.

---

# 3. Actual-row upper

The actual-row upper is now:

\[
\boxed{
\|\Lambda_Y^{\eff}J_{\tr}(q''_{\can})\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu(\mathcal C).
}
\]

This follows from:

1. source-linear invisibility:

   \[
   \Pi_YT_{\tr\to\sing}J_{\tr}(B_2+P_2+D_2)=0
   \]

   in the primitive \(Y12\)-grade;

2. fixed/coherent \(E_2^Y\), labelled mismatch, and parameter effects being absorbed by

   \[
   P_Y^0;
   \]

3. targeted residual control:

   \[
   \sum_p\|\Pi_YR_{\small,p}\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu.
   \]

So the upper is now in the same channel that will be used for the D4 lower.

---

# 4. D4 lower in the same channel

The D4 live carrier is

\[
D_{\D4}
=
H\,E_{\car}^{\tr}L_{\clean}^{\tr}\Gamma.
\]

In the \(Y12\) fiber,

\[
s_7^{D4}=Hc_7\Gamma_G,
\]

\[
s_9^{Y,D4}
=
H(\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

Subtracting the graph relation

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\fixed}
\]

gives

\[
F_Y
=
H\left((\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right)
=
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

After coherent amplitude projection, the lower channel is

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_Y^0)F_Y.
\]

Thus

\[
\boxed{
\Lambda_Y^{\eff}D_{\D4}
=
F_Y^{\eff}\,y_Y
}
\]

up to a fixed nonzero normalization of the \(Y12\)-graded carrier.

The local D4 lower is therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\|\Lambda_Y^{\eff}D_{\D4}\|^2
\asymp
|F_Y^{\eff}|^2\|y_Y\|^2.
}
\]

This is exactly the same channel as the actual-row upper.

---

# 5. Amplitude Schur is now part of \(\Lambda_Y^{\eff}\)

The amplitude Schur theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\|F_Y^{\eff}\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2
\ge Q^{-A}
\|F_Y\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2.
}
\]

This says that the coherent amplitude space does not absorb the wall.

If this fails, then \(\Lambda_Y^{\eff}\) may annihilate the D4 lower even though the fiber \(Y12\) carrier survives.

So the final \(\Lambda_Y^{\eff}\) channel exists only after both:

\[
\boxed{
\text{fiber Schur}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\text{amplitude Schur}
}
\]

hold.

---

# 6. Local versus block/global contradiction

If

\[
P_Y^0
\]

is local or block-local, then

\[
\Lambda_Y^{\eff}
\]

is still a Type-I positive atomic or block-atomic channel:

\[
\sum_{\omega}
\|\Lambda_{Y,\omega}^{\eff}V_{\glob}\|^2.
\]

If

\[
P_Y^0
\]

is global finite-dimensional, the channel is still positive but global:

\[
\|\Lambda_Y^{\eff}V_{\glob}\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2.
\]

This can still contradict the upper, but the extraction must produce a global \(L^2\)-lower, not merely pointwise atom lower.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\text{local }P_Y^0\Rightarrow\text{atomic contradiction;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{block }P_Y^0\Rightarrow\text{block-Schur contradiction;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{global }P_Y^0\Rightarrow\text{global Schur contradiction.}
}
\]

All are usable if stated correctly. Only extraction-local \(P_Y^0\) is fatal.

---

# 7. Interaction with O2/O3

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}=0
\]

or \(P_Y^0\) is low branch-charge, one may use

\[
|F_Y^{\eff}|^2
\]

inside the O2/O3 smooth weight.

If \(P_Y^0\) is nonlocal/global, safer order is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{use raw }|F_Y|^2\text{ for O2/O3;}
}
\]

then prove amplitude Schur separately:

\[
F_Y\notin\mathcal C_Y^{\actual};
\]

then extract using the projected lower.

So the actual O2/O3 weight should be chosen by case:

## Case A: no coherent amplitude

\[
W_{\bd}=N_{\sm}|F_Y|^2.
\]

## Case B: low-complexity projection

\[
W_{\bd}=N_{\sm}|F_Y^{\eff}|^2.
\]

## Case C: global/nonlocal projection

\[
W_{\bd}=N_{\sm}|F_Y|^2,
\]

then apply amplitude Schur after O2/O3.

This avoids branch-charge contamination.

---

# 8. Final contradiction theorem

Assume:

1. **Labelled realization consistency**

   \[
   \Lambda_Y^0J_{\tr}(q''_{\can})
   =
   \Pi_YT_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}J_{\tr}(q''_{\can})
   +
   c+s,
   \]

   with \(c\in\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}\) and \(s\) small.

2. **Fiber graph nonverticality**

   yielding

   \[
   F_Y=H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
   \]

3. **Amplitude Schur**

   \[
   \|(I-P_Y^0)F_Y\|^2
   \ge Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|^2.
   \]

4. **Wall mass/extraction**

   \(F_Y^{\eff}\) has polynomial mass on a good set.

5. **Atomic/block/global lower mobility**

   local D4 certificates contribute positively to

   \[
   \|\Lambda_Y^{\eff}V_{\glob}\|^2.
   \]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\|\Lambda_Y^{\eff}V_{\glob}\|^2
\ge Q^{-A}\mu(\mathcal C)
}
\]

but the actual-row upper gives

\[
\boxed{
\|\Lambda_Y^{\eff}V_{\glob}\|^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu(\mathcal C).
}
\]

Contradiction for \(B\) large.

---

# 9. Remaining no-go cases

The \(Y12\) channel fails if:

1. \(y_Y=0\) in the labelled singular quotient;

2. actual \(Y12\)-fiber is vertically free;

3. \(\Pi_YR_{\lab}\) is locally free;

4. \(E_2^Y\) is locally free;

5. amplitude Schur fails:

   \[
   F_Y\in\mathcal C_Y^{\actual};
   \]

6. \(P_Y^0\) is extraction-local or too high-complexity;

7. wall finite type/trap disposal fails after amplitude projection.

These are now sharply separated.

---

# 10. Final Agent 2 update

The labelled \(Y12\) route now yields an explicit contradiction-ready operator:

\[
\boxed{
\Lambda_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_Y^0)
\Pi_Y
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
P_{\tr}.
}
\]

This is the actual downstream \(\Lambda\), with coherent amplitudes removed before extraction.

The upper and lower are now measured in the same channel:

\[
\Lambda_Y^{\eff}J_{\tr}(q''_{\can})
\]

for actual rows, and

\[
\Lambda_Y^{\eff}D_{\D4}
=
F_Y^{\eff}y_Y
\]

for D4.

This resolves the earlier mismatch between formal labelled D4 visibility and actual singular-row coherence, provided the labelled-realization residual is not locally free.

## Agent 2 — continued attack: projection-safe D4 lower

We have the contradiction-ready operator

\[
\Lambda_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_Y^0)\Pi_Y
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
P_{\tr}.
\]

The actual-row upper and D4 lower are now measured in the same channel. That is good.

But there is one more obstruction:

\[
\boxed{
\text{The projection }I-P_Y^0\text{ can kill the local D4 lower even if raw }Y12\text{ survives.}
}
\]

So the local lower theorem must be projection-safe.

---

# 1. Raw D4 lower versus effective D4 lower

The raw \(Y12\) D4 amplitude is

\[
F_Y
=
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

The effective wall is

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_Y^0)F_Y.
\]

The raw fiber survival theorem only gives

\[
y_Y\neq0
\]

and hence

\[
\Pi_YD_{\D4}
=
F_Y\,y_Y.
\]

But the actual contradiction uses

\[
\Lambda_Y^{\eff}D_{\D4}
=
F_Y^{\eff}y_Y.
\]

Therefore the relevant lower is:

\[
\boxed{
\|F_Y^{\eff}\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2
\ge Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2.
}
\]

This is the amplitude Schur theorem. Without it, \(\Lambda_Y^{\eff}\) may remove precisely the D4 wall.

---

# 2. Projection-safe extraction

Once

\[
F_Y^{\eff}
\]

is fixed, extraction must use the capped factor

\[
F_{\wall}^{Y,\eff}(m)
=
\min\left(
\frac{|F_Y^{\eff}(m)|^2}{\eta_Y},
1
\right).
\]

Not

\[
\min\left(
\frac{|F_Y(m)|^2}{\eta_Y},
1
\right).
\]

If extraction uses the raw wall \(F_Y\), it may select centers where the wall is large but coherent, i.e.

\[
F_Y\approx P_Y^0F_Y.
\]

Such centers do not contribute to the forbidden channel.

So the extraction theorem must be updated:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{GoodSubsetExtraction}^{Y}
\text{ uses }F_{\wall}^{Y,\eff},\text{ not the raw wall.}
}
\]

---

# 3. Tension with O2/O3

There is a tension:

* O2/O3 prefers a smooth bounded-degree weight.
* If \(P_Y^0\) is global, then \(F_Y^{\eff}\) may not have bounded branch-charge.
* But extraction must use \(F_Y^{\eff}\), not \(F_Y\).

The safe order is therefore:

1. Use raw wall \(F_Y\) in the smooth O2/O3 aggregate if \(F_Y^{\eff}\) is nonlocal.
2. Prove O2/O3 determinant survival under that raw bounded-degree weight.
3. Prove amplitude Schur:

   \[
   \|F_Y^{\eff}\|^2\ge Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|^2.
   \]

4. Transfer the aggregate mass from raw wall to effective wall.
5. Only then cap/extract using \(F_Y^{\eff}\).

So the corrected aggregate implication is:

\[
\mathrm{Agg}_{1,23}^{F_Y}
+
\mathrm{AmplitudeSchur}_{Y}
\Longrightarrow
\mathrm{Agg}_{1,23}^{F_Y^{\eff}}.
\]

This is the clean bridge.

---

# 4. The bridge lemma

Let

\[
d\nu_{1,23}
=
F_1F_{23}N\,d\rho
\]

be the smooth post-Gate/O2O3 measure before wall capping.

Suppose raw wall aggregate is

\[
\int |F_Y|^2\,d\nu_{1,23}
\ge Q^{-A}\int d\nu_{1,23}.
\]

Suppose amplitude Schur under the same measure gives

\[
\int |F_Y^{\eff}|^2\,d\nu_{1,23}
\ge Q^{-A'}
\int |F_Y|^2\,d\nu_{1,23}.
\]

Then

\[
\boxed{
\int |F_Y^{\eff}|^2\,d\nu_{1,23}
\ge Q^{-A-A'}
\int d\nu_{1,23}.
}
\]

With a polynomial upper bound for \(F_Y^{\eff}\), this gives the capped effective-wall aggregate.

Thus the wall logic becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\text{raw wall for O2/O3}
+
\text{amplitude Schur}
\Rightarrow
\text{effective wall for extraction}.
}
\]

---

# 5. What if raw wall aggregate itself is not proved?

If O2/O3 already incorporates the wall into the determinant survival weight, the raw wall aggregate may be entangled with \(E_{23}\).

Then we need the joint form:

\[
\int F_1F_{23}|F_Y|^2N\,d\rho
\ge Q^{-A}\int N\,d\rho.
\]

Amplitude Schur must hold under the measure

\[
d\nu_{\bd}
=
F_1F_{23}N\,d\rho
\]

or an equivalent smooth measure.

If amplitude Schur is only known under a different reference measure, it may not transfer. Thus the projection measure must be chosen to match the actual aggregate measure as closely as possible, but without depending on \(F_Y^{\eff}\).

The natural choice is:

\[
\boxed{
d\nu_0=d\nu_{1,23}=F_1F_{23}N\,d\rho
}
\]

where \(F_1,F_{23}\) are smooth/capped pre-wall factors, but not \(F_{\wall}\).

This avoids circularity and aligns upper/lower.

---

# 6. Nonlocal projection and local D4 atoms

If

\[
P_Y^0
\]

is global, then

\[
F_Y^{\eff}(m)
\]

is not purely local in \(m\). This affects the local D4 theorem.

The local D4 lower originally produces a local amplitude

\[
F_Y(m)y_Y(m).
\]

After global projection,

\[
F_Y^{\eff}(m)
=
F_Y(m)-\sum_j a_j\phi_j(m),
\]

where coefficients \(a_j\) depend globally.

This is still a valid Hilbert-space lower, but not a pointwise local lower until the projection is fixed.

Thus the local theorem should be stated in Hilbert form:

\[
\boxed{
\|\Lambda_Y^{\eff}D_{\D4}\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2
=
\|F_Y^{\eff}y_Y\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2.
}
\]

Then extraction is performed after the global \(F_Y^{\eff}\) has been computed. Pointwise locality is recovered only at that stage.

So a nonlocal \(P_Y^0\) is not fatal, but it shifts the proof from atomwise lower-first to global-Schur-lower-first.

---

# 7. Block projection as compromise

To preserve locality, choose a block decomposition

\[
\mathcal C=\bigsqcup_\omega \mathcal C_\omega.
\]

Define

\[
P_{Y,\omega}^0:
L^2(\nu_{0,\omega})\to\mathcal C_{Y,\omega}^{\actual}.
\]

Then

\[
F_{Y,\omega}^{\eff}
=
(I-P_{Y,\omega}^0)F_Y|_{\omega}.
\]

The forbidden energy is

\[
\sum_\omega
\|F_{Y,\omega}^{\eff}\|_{L^2(\nu_{0,\omega})}^2.
\]

This keeps the contradiction Type-I at block level and prevents global fitting.

The block size should be chosen so that:

1. coherent spaces have low dimension per block;
2. branch-charge/O2O3 structure remains smooth inside blocks;
3. enough centers remain per block for extraction.

Thus a good theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{BlockAmplitudeSchur}_{Y}.
}
\]

This may be the best practical formulation if \(\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}\neq0\).

---

# 8. New failure mode: projection creates high oscillation

Even if \(F_Y^{\eff}\) has mass, it may have high oscillation due to subtracting a coherent model. This can affect:

1. branch-charge bandwidth;
2. local packing/extraction;
3. numerical stability of the wall factor.

If O2/O3 uses raw wall and projection happens after O2/O3, high oscillation does not affect determinant survival. It only affects extraction.

For extraction, high oscillation is acceptable if there is an \(L^\infty/L^2\) control:

\[
\|F_Y^{\eff}\|_{L^\infty}^2
\le Q^C
\|F_Y^{\eff}\|_{L^2(\nu_0)}^2/\nu_0(\mathcal C).
\]

Or use dyadic level-set extraction. Thus high oscillation is not fatal, but it requires a distributional bound.

Add:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EffectiveWallSpread}_{Y}.
}
\]

---

# 9. Updated downstream package

The mature rank-one \(Y12\) downstream package is now:

## Local labelled/fiber stage

\[
\mathrm{LiveY12DualPair}
+
\mathrm{LabelledRealizationConsistency}_{Y12}
+
\mathrm{Y12FiberGraphNonverticality}.
\]

This gives raw wall

\[
F_Y=H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

## Amplitude stage

Define \(\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}\) and prove either:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual}=0
\]

or

\[
\mathrm{AmplitudeSchur}_{Y}.
\]

This gives

\[
F_Y^{\eff}.
\]

## Aggregate stage

Prove raw wall aggregate/O2O3, then transfer via amplitude Schur to effective wall aggregate.

## Extraction stage

Extract using

\[
F_{\wall}^{Y,\eff}.
\]

## Contradiction stage

Use

\[
\Lambda_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_Y^0)\Pi_Y\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}P_{\tr}
\]

for both upper and lower.

---

# 10. Final conclusion

The key update is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Effective wall extraction must use the projected wall }F_Y^{\eff}.
}
\]

But O2/O3 may use the raw wall \(F_Y\) first if projection is nonlocal or high-complexity.

The bridge is the amplitude Schur lemma:

\[
\|F_Y^{\eff}\|^2\ge Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|^2.
\]

Thus the rank-one \(Y12\) route now has a clean non-circular order:

\[
\text{raw wall aggregate}
\to
\text{amplitude Schur}
\to
\text{effective wall extraction}
\to
\text{projected forbidden contradiction}.
\]

# Agent 2 — LabelledRealizationConsistency\(_{Y12}\)

## Verdict

\text{small},

with all algebraic mismatch terms either zero in the primitive \(Y12\)-grade or finite coherent, and only truncation/tail terms requiring analytic residual estimates.

\[
R_{\lab}
=

where

\[

\[
\sum_p\|\Pi_YR_{\small,p}\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu,

and

\[
\Pi_YR_{\coh}\in\mathcal C_Y^{\lab},

with \(\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}\) finite/block-coherent and not extraction-locally free.

If any part of \(\Pi_YR_{\lab}\) is locally free in the primitive \(Y12\)-direction, the labelled \(Y12\) route fails.

---

# 1. Labelled expression trees and \(\Sigma(Y12)\)

A labelled carrier is not just a scalar jet. It must carry:

\[
(\deg_{\src},o_N,o_W,\mathrm{branch},\mathrm{point},w,\pi,\rho,\mathrm{carrier}).
\]

For the \(Y12\) carrier,

\[
\Sigma(Y12)
=
(2,D^2N_0,DW_{\same},\mathrm{mixed}\ 12,\mathrm{same},1,\pi_Y,9,[\mathsf C_{Y12}]).
\]

The origin pair

\[
(o_N,o_W)=(D^2N_0,DW_{\same})
\]

is the key invariant.

It separates the actual \(Y12\) carrier

\[
DW_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]]
\]

from superficially similar terms such as

\[
DW[\xi]E_2(\eta),
\]

\[
D^2W[\xi_1,\xi_2]N_0,
\]

and

\[
\partial_\alpha^2(\mathrm{EF\ atom}).
\]

Those may be degree-two or whitening-bearing, but they are not primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin \(Y12\).

---

# 2. Decomposition of labelled-realization mismatch

We need

\[
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}J_{\tr}(q''_{\can})
=
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}J_{\tr}(q''_{\can})
+
R_{\lab}.

\[
R_{\lab}
=
R_{\norm}
+
R_{\whit}
+
R_{\proj}
+
R_{\trunc}
+
R_{\chart}
+
R_{\tail}.

\[
\boxed{
R_{\norm},R_{\whit},R_{\proj},R_{\chart}

\[
\boxed{
R_{\trunc},R_{\tail}
\text{ are analytic residual terms.}
}
\]

---

# 3. Algebraic terms

## 3.1 \(R_{\norm}\)

This term measures whether raw transition transports an already formed source or recomputes \(N_0\).

If raw transition transports a formed source, then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\norm}=0.
}
\]

If raw transition recomputes \(N_0\), it can create

\[
D^2N_0[\delta\xi_1,\delta\xi_2]
\]

from source-linear transition inputs.

Then \(\Pi_YR_{\norm}\) must be classified:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\norm}\in\mathcal C_Y^{\norm}+\text{small}
\]

is usable if \(\mathcal C_Y^{\norm}\) is finite coherent.

It is fatal if

\[
\Pi_YR_{\norm,p}=a_p y_Y(p)
\]

with \(a_p\) locally free at extraction scale.

So the structural test is:

\[
\boxed{
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}
\text{ transports formed canonical expressions, not raw variables for recomputation.}
}
\]

---

## 3.2 \(R_{\whit}\)

Whitening mismatch is safe if the quotient remembers the origin pair.

Terms like

\[
DW[\xi]E_2(\eta)
\]

or

\[
D^2W[\xi_1,\xi_2]N_0
\]

should not enter the primitive \(Y12\)-sector because their origin pairs differ from

\[
(D^2N_0,DW_{\same}).
\]

Thus the desired algebraic vanishing is

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\whit}=0.
}
\]

If this fails, it means the singular quotient is forgetting the origin-pair label and collapsing all degree-two whitening terms together. That is quotient overcollapse.

---

## 3.3 \(R_{\proj}\)

Projection mismatch should vanish if the singular nuisance quotient is homogeneous/filtered near \(\Sigma(Y12)\).

Desired:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\proj}=0.
}
\]

Failure means projection or quotienting is happening before the labelled origin decomposition is realized.

If \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\) kills the primitive \(Y12\)-sector, then \(e_Y\) dies and the \(Y12\) route is rank-zero.

---

## 3.4 \(R_{\chart}\)

Chart curvature is safe if it is merely coordinate/re-expansion curvature.

It has chart origin, not primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin.

Desired:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\chart}=0.
}
\]

It becomes dangerous only if the chart transition recomputes the nonlinear normal form \(N_0\). Then this belongs with \(R_{\norm}\).

---

# 4. Analytic residual terms

## 4.1 \(R_{\trunc}\)

Finite-order truncation mismatch requires a targeted estimate:

\[
\boxed{
\sum_p\|\Pi_YR_{\trunc,p}\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu.
}
\]

This is not full \(K_9\)-completion. It is only completion in the primitive \(Y12\)-graded sector.

---

## 4.2 \(R_{\tail}\)

Tail/completion mismatch is the \(E_2\)-type contribution.

It must be decomposed as:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\tail}
=
c_{\tail}
+
s_{\tail},
\]

where

\[
c_{\tail}\in\mathcal C_Y^{\tail}
\]

is finite/block-coherent, and

\[
\sum_p\|s_{\tail,p}\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

Fatal case:

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\tail}
\]

is extraction-locally free.

This is the same classification as \(E_2^Y\):

\[
\text{absent/fixed/finite coherent/residual-controlled/local-free}.
\]

---

# 5. Central source-linear modes

The theorem must also apply to transition central modes:

\[
\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y],
\]

with provisional conservative choice

\[
d_{\max}=14
\]

unless a sharper \(d_0\) is known.

For every source-linear central mode \(P(y)\) with

\[
\deg P\le d_{\max},
\]

we need

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
(P(y)\text{-source-linear mode})=0.
}
\]

And for raw/labelled mismatch,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y
\left(
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
-
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}
\right)
(P(y)\text{-source-linear mode})
}
\]

must be zero, small, or finite coherent, not locally free.

This is the compatibility bridge:

\[
\boxed{
\text{central O2/O3 enrichment does not pollute }Y12\text{ if labels are preserved.}
}
\]

---

# 6. Coherent labelled amplitude space

Collect all coherent labelled-realization contributions into

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
=
\mathcal C_Y^{\norm}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\tail}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\chart}
+\cdots.
\]

The no-local-free condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\dim \mathcal C_Y^{\lab}|_{\Omega}\le Q^C
}
\]

for every relevant block \(\Omega\).

If instead

\[
\dim \mathcal C_Y^{\lab}|_{\Omega}\asymp |\Omega|,
\]

then \(\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}\) is extraction-local and fatal.

This same condition will later be imposed on

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{E_2},\quad
\mathcal C_Y^{\param},\quad
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}.
\]

---

# 7. The theorem

A precise statement is:

**LabelledRealizationConsistency\(_{Y12}\).** There exists a decomposition

\[
R_{\lab}=R_{\alg}+R_{\small}+R_{\coh}
\]

such that:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\alg}=0,
\]

\[
\sum_p\|\Pi_YR_{\small,p}\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu,
\]

and

\[
\Pi_YR_{\coh}\in\mathcal C_Y^{\lab},
\]

where

\[
\dim\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}|_{\Omega}\le Q^C
\]

on all relevant blocks.

The same statement holds for source-linear transition modes

\[
\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y].
\]

Then the labelled \(Y12\)-projection applies to the actual singular row and is compatible with central O2/O3 routing.

---

# 8. Consequence for downstream wall

Under this theorem, the actual coherent amplitude space becomes

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
=
\mathcal C_Y^{E_2}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
+
\mathcal C_Y^{\param}
+\cdots.
\]

The fiber stage gives

\[
F_Y
=
H\left((\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right)
=
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}=0
\]

or only fixed basepoint, then \(F_Y\) is the pointwise wall.

If

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}
\]

is finite coherent, the downstream theorem uses amplitude Schur:

\[
\operatorname{dist}_{L^2(\nu_{\bd})}(F_Y,\mathcal C_Y^{\arith})^2
\ge
Q^{-A}\|F_Y\|_{L^2(\nu_{\bd})}^2.
\]

If \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) is locally free, the rank-one \(Y12\) route dies.

---

# 9. No-go cases

The labelled realization route fails if:

1. \(R_{\norm}\) recomputes \(N_0\) with locally free primitive \(Y12\)-amplitude;

2. \(R_{\whit}\) shows the quotient forgets origin-pair \((D^2N_0,DW_{\same})\);

3. \(R_{\proj}\) shows \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\) kills the \(Y12\)-sector;

4. \(R_{\chart}\) recomputes nonlinear source data;

5. \(R_{\trunc}\) or \(R_{\tail}\) has uncontrolled \(Y12\)-norm;

6. \(\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}\) is extraction-local;

7. central source-linear modes become \(Y12\)-visible.

---

# Final Agent 2 conclusion

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{LabelledRealizationConsistency}_{Y12}
\text{ is mostly an algebraic origin-functoriality theorem, plus a targeted }Y12\text{-completion estimate.}
}
\]

The algebraic terms

\[
R_{\norm},R_{\whit},R_{\proj},R_{\chart}
\]

should vanish or be finite coherent in the \(Y12\)-grade.

The analytic terms

\[
R_{\trunc},R_{\tail}
\]

need only \(Y12\)-sector residual estimates.

If this holds, the labelled transition really represents the actual singular row in the primitive \(Y12\) channel, and central O2/O3 transition modes remain compatible with \(Y12\)-normality.

## Agent 2 — continued attack: functoriality identities for \(R_{\lab}\)

We have the decomposition

\[
R_{\lab}
=
R_{\norm}
+
R_{\whit}
+
R_{\proj}
+
R_{\trunc}
+
R_{\chart}
+
R_{\tail}.
\]

The previous step classified which pieces should be algebraic-zero and which require estimates. I now turn that into proof criteria: exact functoriality identities whose failure produces the obstruction.

The goal is to replace vague statements like “transition preserves labels” by finite identities that can be checked.

---

# 1. Normal-form functoriality

The desired identity is:

\[
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}\bigl(N_0(\xi)\bigr)
=
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
\bigl(N_0(\xi)\bigr)
\]

in the \(Y12\)-graded quotient.

Equivalently,

\[
\Pi_Y
\left[
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}N_0(\xi)
-
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}N_0(\xi)
\right]
=0.
\]

This is the exact condition

\[
\boxed{\Pi_YR_{\norm}=0.}
\]

A sufficient structural version is:

\[
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}
\]

commutes with applying \(N_0\) to the already-formed source up to re-expansion:

\[
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw}\circ N_0
=
N_0^{\lab}\circ T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\src}
\]

where

\[
N_0^{\lab}
\]

does not re-polarize source-linear terms into new primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin carriers.

If instead raw transition uses a fresh normal-form solve in the singular chart, then the commutator

\[
\left[
T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw},N_0
\right]
\]

can contain

\[
D^2N_0[\delta\xi_1,\delta\xi_2],
\]

and \(R_{\norm}\) becomes dangerous.

So the finite audit is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y [T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw},N_0]=0?
}
\]

If yes, normal-form mismatch is harmless.

If no, compute whether the image is finite coherent or locally free.

---

# 2. Whitening functoriality

Let

\[
W_{\tr}^{-1/2}
\]

and

\[
W_{\sing}^{-1/2}
\]

be the whitening maps in transition and singular charts.

The labelled map keeps track of the whitening origin

\[
o_W=DW_{\same}.
\]

The desired identity is not that whitening maps commute exactly. The desired identity is weaker:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y
\left(
W_{\sing}^{-1/2}T_{\tr\to\sing}
-
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}W_{\tr}^{-1/2}
\right)=0.
}
\]

Expanded to first and second whitening variations, the possible contaminants are:

\[
DW[\xi]\,Z,
\]

\[
D^2W[\xi_1,\xi_2]\,Z,
\]

and transported versions.

These vanish under \(\Pi_Y\) unless

\[
Z
\]

has primitive

\[
D^2N_0
\]

origin and the whitening derivative is the same-point \(DW_{\same}\) slot.

Thus the finite criterion is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{(D^2N_0,DW_{\same})}
\left(
DW\cdot \mathrm{EF\text{-}linear}
+
D^2W\cdot N_0
+
DW\cdot DN_0
\right)=0.
}
\]

If the quotient remembers the origin pair, this is automatic.

If it forgets origin, this is exactly where false \(Y12\)-pollution can enter.

---

# 3. Projection functoriality

Let

\[
Q_{\sing}
\]

be the singular quotient/projection. Labelled transition wants:

\[
Q_{\sing}\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
=
\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
Q_{\tr}
\]

on the \(Y12\)-graded sector, modulo lower signatures.

The relevant condition is:

\[
\boxed{
Q_{\sing}
\text{ is filtered near }\Sigma(Y12).
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\cap
\mathcal F^{\le Y}
\]

must split as

\[
(\mathcal N_{\sing}\cap \mathcal F^{<Y})
\oplus
(\mathcal N_{\sing}\cap \mathcal Y_{\Sigma(Y12)}).
\]

Then projection cannot kill \(Y12\) by mixing it with lower-origin terms.

The finite audit is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{gr}_{\Sigma(Y12)}(\mathcal N_{\sing})=0
\quad\text{or at least does not contain }y_Y.
}
\]

If

\[
\operatorname{gr}_{\Sigma(Y12)}(\mathcal N_{\sing})
\]

contains

\[
y_Y,
\]

then \(R_{\proj}\) is not the only problem; the \(Y12\)-carrier itself is quotient-nuisance and the route dies.

---

# 4. Chart functoriality

Chart transition may be nonlinear in coordinates, but it should be source-linear:

\[
T_{\chart}(aZ_1+bZ_2)=aT_{\chart}Z_1+bT_{\chart}Z_2.
\]

The required identity is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y
\left(
T_{\chart}(\text{source-linear or coordinate-curvature terms})
\right)=0.
}
\]

This holds if chart operations do not apply \(D^2N_0\) and do not create a same-point \(DW_{\same}\) slot attached to a primitive \(D^2N_0\) source-Hessian.

A precise criterion:

\[
\operatorname{Origin}_N(T_{\chart}Z)
=
\operatorname{Origin}_N(Z),
\]

and

\[
\operatorname{Origin}_W(T_{\chart}Z)
=
\operatorname{Origin}_W(Z)
\]

unless \(Z\) already contains a whitening label.

Then chart curvature cannot create \(\Sigma(Y12)\) from source-linear data.

If chart transition recomputes the normal form, this falls back into the \(R_{\norm}\) obstruction.

---

# 5. Truncation functoriality

Truncation does not generally vanish by label. It gives:

\[
R_{\trunc}
=
(1-P_{\le 9})\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}J_{\tr}(q''_{\can})
\]

or the appropriate finite-order residue.

The targeted theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\sum_p\|\Pi_YR_{\trunc,p}\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu.
}
\]

But there is one structural simplification:

\[
\Pi_YR_{\trunc}
\]

only sees the primitive \(Y12\)-sector. So truncation of source-linear EF tails should not contribute.

The estimate should therefore be posed only for the primitive-source tail:

\[
R_{\trunc}^{Y}
=
\Pi_YR_{\trunc}.
\]

This is generally much smaller than full singular truncation error.

---

# 6. Tail/completion functoriality

For

\[
R_{\tail},
\]

the key decomposition is:

\[
R_{\tail}^{Y}
=
\Pi_YR_{\tail}
=
R_{\tail}^{Y,\fixed}
+
R_{\tail}^{Y,\coh}
+
R_{\tail}^{Y,\small}
+
R_{\tail}^{Y,\free?}.
\]

Acceptable:

\[
R_{\tail}^{Y,\fixed}
\]

shifts the basepoint,

\[
R_{\tail}^{Y,\coh}
\]

joins

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\actual},
\]

and

\[
R_{\tail}^{Y,\small}
\]

satisfies

\[
\sum_p\|R_{\tail,p}^{Y,\small}\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu.
\]

Fatal:

\[
R_{\tail}^{Y,\free?}
\]

contains extraction-local arbitrary amplitudes in the primitive \(Y12\)-direction.

Thus the tail theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
R_{\tail}^{Y,\free?}=0.
}
\]

or more precisely,

\[
\boxed{
\dim R_{\tail}^{Y}|_\Omega\le Q^C
\quad\text{on all blocks }\Omega.
}
\]

---

# 7. Consolidated finite checklist

The labelled-realization theorem reduces to the following finite list:

## Algebraic identities

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y[T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw},N_0]=0.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y\bigl(DW\cdot \mathrm{EF\text{-}linear}
+
D^2W\cdot N_0
+
DW\cdot DN_0\bigr)=0.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ is filtered near }\Sigma(Y12).
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
T_{\chart}\text{ preserves }(o_N,o_W)\text{ near }\Sigma(Y12).
}
\]

## Analytic estimates

\[
\boxed{
\sum_p\|\Pi_YR_{\trunc,p}\|^2\le Q^{-B}\mu.
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\sum_p
\operatorname{dist}
(R_{\tail,p}^{Y},\mathcal C_Y^{\tail})^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu.
}
\]

## Coherence restrictions

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal C_Y^{\lab}
\text{ has block dimension }\le Q^C
\text{ and no atom-local leverage.}
}
\]

These are exactly the conditions needed to prove

\[
\Pi_YR_{\lab}\in \mathcal C_Y^{\lab}+\text{small}.
\]

---

# 8. What this buys

If all identities/estimates hold, then

\[
\Lambda_Y^0
=
\Pi_Y\operatorname{Real}_{\sing}
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}P_{\tr}
\]

is a legitimate raw \(Y12\)-channel for the actual singular row.

After coherent amplitude projection,

\[
\Lambda_Y^{\eff}
=
(I-P_Y^0)\Lambda_Y^0
\]

is the final contradiction-ready operator.

This operator has matching upper and lower:

\[
\|\Lambda_Y^{\eff}J_{\tr}(q''_{\can})\|^2
\le Q^{-B}\mu
\]

and

\[
\|\Lambda_Y^{\eff}D_{\D4}\|^2
\asymp
\|F_Y^{\eff}y_Y\|^2.
\]

Thus labelled-realization consistency is precisely the bridge from formal \(Y12\) carrier algebra to actual source contradiction.

---

# 9. Failure classification

If an algebraic identity fails, the fix depends on which one.

## Failure of \(\Pi_Y[T,N_0]=0\)

Raw transition recomputes nonlinear source. Need either labelled-realization residual coherence or abandon labelled proof.

## Failure of whitening origin split

The quotient forgets origin pair. Need refine quotient or use full Schur test.

## Failure of filtered \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\)

Nuisance overcollapse. Need smaller nuisance or direct separating covector.

## Failure of truncation/tail estimate

Need stronger \(\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{Y12}\), not necessarily full \(K_9\).

## Failure of coherence dimension

Labelled residual is locally free. Rank-one \(Y12\) route dies.

---

# 10. Final Agent 2 status

The labelled-realization consistency theorem is now reduced to a finite set of functoriality identities plus targeted \(Y12\)-sector residual estimates.

The most important algebraic identity is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y[T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw},N_0]=0.
}
\]

The most important quotient guardrail is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ must be filtered near }\Sigma(Y12).
}
\]

The most important analytic input is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{EFCompletion}_{Y12}
}
\]

for truncation and tail terms only.

If these pass, the labelled \(Y12\) operator is valid for the actual singular row and can be used in the final projected upper/lower contradiction.

## Agent 2 — continued attack: normal-form functoriality in the \(Y12\)-sector

The labelled-realization package now hinges on the algebraic identity

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_Y[T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw},N_0]=0.
}
\]

This is the core condition behind

\[
\Pi_YR_{\norm}=0.
\]

I now push that identity directly.

The issue is whether transition-to-singular merely transports a formed nonlinear source carrier, or whether it recomputes the nonlinear normal form after changing chart. Only the second case can create new primitive \(Y12\)-origin terms.

---

# 1. Abstract transition diagram

Let

\[
U_{\tr}
\]

be the transition source-input space and

\[
U_{\sing}
\]

the singular source-input space.

Let

\[
L:U_{\tr}\to U_{\sing}
\]

be the source-input transition map, and let

\[
S:\mathcal H_{\tr}\to\mathcal H_{\sing}
\]

be the output carrier transition.

The naturality condition for the nonlinear normal form is

\[
\boxed{
N_{0,\sing}\circ L
=
S\circ N_{0,\tr}
}
\]

on the actual source-input family, at least modulo non-\(Y12\) signatures.

If this holds, then labelled transition and raw transition agree on formed \(N_0\)-carriers.

Differentiating twice gives

\[
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
+
DN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)]
=
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\]

if \(L\) is nonlinear in the source variables.

If \(L\) is linear or affine in the source slots, then

\[
D^2L=0,
\]

and the condition reduces to

\[
\boxed{
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
=
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2].
}
\]

This is exactly the origin-preserving labelled transition identity.

---

# 2. The \(Y12\) commutator

Define the \(Y12\)-sector commutator

\[
\mathfrak C_Y(\xi_1,\xi_2)
:=
\Pi_Y\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S\,D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right),
\]

plus, if \(L\) is nonlinear,

\[
\Pi_YDN_{0,\sing}[D^2L(\xi_1,\xi_2)].
\]

Then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YR_{\norm}=0
}
\]

is equivalent to

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y(\xi_1,\xi_2)=0
}
\]

for the relevant \(Y12\) source slots.

So the finite audit becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y=0,\quad
\mathfrak C_Y\in\mathcal C_Y^{\norm},\quad
\text{or }\mathfrak C_Y\text{ locally free?}
}
\]

This is sharper than saying “transition recomputes \(N_0\).”

---

# 3. Three cases

## Case A: strict naturality

\[
N_{0,\sing}\circ L=S\circ N_{0,\tr}
\]

as labelled maps.

Then

\[
\mathfrak C_Y=0.
\]

This is the ideal case:

\[
\boxed{\Pi_YR_{\norm}=0.}
\]

The labelled transition exactly matches raw transition in the primitive \(Y12\) sector.

## Case B: finite coherent curvature of transition

Suppose

\[
\mathfrak C_Y(\xi_1,\xi_2)
\]

is nonzero but depends only on fixed chart parameters or finitely many coherent modes.

Then

\[
\mathfrak C_Y\in \mathcal C_Y^{\norm}.
\]

This is usable if

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\norm}
\]

is finite/block-coherent and not extraction-local.

The wall theorem is then amplitude Schur modulo

\[
\mathcal C_Y^{\norm}.
\]

## Case C: locally free recomputation

If

\[
\mathfrak C_Y(\xi_1,\xi_2)
\]

can vary independently at the extraction atom scale, then raw transition can manufacture arbitrary primitive \(Y12\)-amplitude.

Then the labelled \(Y12\) channel is not forbidden.

This is fatal for the rank-one \(Y12\) route.

---

# 4. When is \(L\) linear enough?

The transition map \(L\) is safe if it is built from:

* rescaling;
* translation;
* fixed chart multiplication;
* fixed basis conversion;
* finite-dimensional Riesz projection independent of the source input.

These are source-linear. Then

\[
D^2L=0.
\]

They preserve the primitive Hessian origin:

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\mapsto
D^2N_0[L\xi_1,L\xi_2].
\]

They do not create \(D^2N_0\)-origin from source-linear terms.

The dangerous operations are:

* solving a chart-dependent nonlinear normalization from the source;
* rewhitening using a source-dependent Gram map before labels are formed;
* projecting using a data-dependent subspace;
* recomputing \(N_0\) from newly mixed source variables instead of transporting formed \(N_0\)-carriers.

These make \(L\) source-dependent or nonlinear, so \(D^2L\) terms appear.

---

# 5. The source-linear central modes test

For central O2/O3 modes

\[
P(y)\in\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y],
\]

they are source-linear inputs. Therefore the safe condition is:

\[
\Pi_Y
D^2N_{0,\sing}[LP_1,LP_2]=0
\]

unless both \(P_1,P_2\) occupy the specific mixed \(12\) source-input slots and the same-point whitening slot.

But central polynomial modes are not primitive mixed \(12\) nonlinear source-Hessian carriers. They are transition-coordinate source-linear basis modes.

Thus under labelled transition,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_YT_{\tr\to\sing}(\mathbb C_{d_{\max}}[y]\text{-source-linear modes})=0.
}
\]

If raw transition recomputes \(N_0\) using pairs of such modes, it may produce

\[
D^2N_0[LP_i,LP_j],
\]

which can pollute \(Y12\).

So central compatibility is exactly another instance of

\[
\mathfrak C_Y=0.
\]

---

# 6. Relation to \(E_2\)

The same commutator test handles \(E_2\).

If \(E_2\) enters only as a source-linear tail

\[
\eta\in U_{\tr}
\]

and transition is linear,

\[
T_{\tr\to\sing}E_2(\eta)
\]

remains source-linear and is killed by \(\Pi_Y\).

If raw transition recomputes

\[
N_0(\xi+\eta)
\]

and expands it, then cross terms appear:

\[
D^2N_0[\xi,\eta].
\]

These may have primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin.

Then the \(E_2^Y\) classification is precisely the classification of such cross terms:

* absent;
* fixed;
* finite coherent;
* residual-small;
* locally free.

So \(E_2\)-honesty is also a normal-form commutator question.

---

# 7. Proposed finite theorem

A useful theorem is:

**NormalFormNaturality\(_Y\).** Let

\[
L:U_{\tr}\to U_{\sing}
\]

and

\[
S:\mathcal H_{\tr}\to\mathcal H_{\sing}
\]

be the labelled source and carrier transition maps. Assume:

1. \(L\) is source-linear in the relevant slots;

2. \(S\) realizes the induced carrier transition;

3. the singular quotient preserves the origin pair \((D^2N_0,DW_{\same})\);

4. for the relevant mixed \(12\) slots,

   \[
   \Pi_Y\left(
   D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
   -
   S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
   \right)=0.
   \]

Then

\[
\Pi_YR_{\norm}=0.
\]

If (4) fails but the image has finite coherent dimension, then \(R_{\norm}\) is admissible as part of \(\mathcal C_Y^{\norm}\). If it is locally free, the \(Y12\) route fails.

---

# 8. How to test condition (4)

Pick bases

\[
\{u_i\}
\]

for \(U_1^{\mix}\) and

\[
\{v_j\}
\]

for \(U_2^{\mix}\).

Compute finite matrices:

\[
M_{\sing}^{ij}
=
\Pi_YD^2N_{0,\sing}[Lu_i,Lv_j],
\]

\[
M_{\tr}^{ij}
=
\Pi_YS D^2N_{0,\tr}[u_i,v_j].
\]

Then

\[
\mathfrak C_Y=0
\]

iff

\[
M_{\sing}^{ij}=M_{\tr}^{ij}
\]

for all relevant pairs \((i,j)\), modulo \(\mathcal C_Y^{\norm}\).

For \(Y12\) specifically, check the pair

\[
(\xi_1,\xi_2).
\]

If

\[
M_{\sing}^{12}-M_{\tr}^{12}
\]

is proportional to \(y_Y\) with free coefficient, fatal.

If it is fixed/coherent, admissible.

---

# 9. Consequence for labelled transition

The labelled transition can now be specified constructively:

\[
\widetilde T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\lab}
\left(D^2N_{0,\tr}[u_i,v_j]\right)
:=
D^2N_{0,\sing}[Lu_i,Lv_j],
\]

with the same labels carried forward.

Then realization consistency is exactly the equality of this labelled expression with raw transition.

This makes the labelled construction canonical.

---

# 10. Final Agent 2 update

The normal-form part of labelled-realization consistency reduces to the finite commutator

\[
\boxed{
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\Pi_Y\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\cdot,L\cdot]
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\cdot,\cdot]
\right).
}
\]

If

\[
\mathfrak C_Y=0,
\]

then

\[
\Pi_YR_{\norm}=0.
\]

If

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
\]

is finite coherent, it joins the amplitude model.

If it is locally free, the labelled \(Y12\) route fails.

This is now a concrete finite audit rather than an informal “does transition preserve labels?” question.

for the lower \(Y12\)-fiber graph slope,

s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\mathrm{fixed}}.

for the effective wall ratio,

\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.

for the O2/O3 fold ratio,

\[
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

I will not use \(r\) for any of these.

The instruction asks Agent 2 to audit the parts of the local \(Y12\)-fiber model \(B_Y\) expected to vanish by origin/label filtering:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal T_{\par}),
\qquad
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}),
\qquad
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal N_{\sing}).

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{\par}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}+\mathcal N_{\sing})
=0.
