This is one of the decisive finite audits in the current nested Schur architecture. The batch also confirms that the key downstream decisions are \(B_Y=0\), \(B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\), vertical/rank-two actual model, and \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\) zero/fixed, finite coherent, or locally free. 

---

# 1. The \(Y12\)-fiber projection

Work in the local primitive \(Y12\)-fiber

\[
(s_7,s_9^Y).
\]

Here:

\[
s_7
\]

is the order-seven coherent carrier coordinate, and

\[
s_9^Y
\]

is the primitive \(Y12\)-signature order-nine coordinate.

The projection

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\]

keeps only these two coordinates after:

1. labelled transition;
2. singular realization;
3. singular nuisance quotient;
4. projection to the primitive \(Y12\) fiber.

The \(Y12\)-signature is

\[
\Sigma(Y12)=
(2,D^2N_0,DW_{\same},\mathrm{mixed}\ 12,\mathrm{same},1,\pi_Y,9,[\mathsf C_{Y12}]).
\]

Thus \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}\) should kill every term that lacks the primitive origin pair

\[
(o_N,o_W)=(D^2N_0,DW_{\same}).
\]

---

# 2. Audit of \(\mathcal T_{\par}\)

The legitimate first-order actual-source parameters are:

\[
m,\quad \lambda,\quad \alpha\ \text{if admitted},\quad
\text{phase/normalization/chart parameters},\quad
\text{background/gamma parameters}.
\]

Their first-order variations are source-linear:

\[
\partial_\theta u_{\sing}
\sim
\text{linear deformation of EF atoms, phase, scale, weight, background, or chart}.
\]

They do not create a primitive source Hessian

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

Therefore, under the labelled origin-pair filtration,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\par}=0.
}
\]

This remains true for \(\alpha\) if \(\alpha\) is only an AFE phase/weight/log-frequency deformation and does not deform \(N_0\) or the same-point whitening mechanism in the primitive \(D^2N_0\)-coupled sector.

The failure cases are precise:

\[
\boxed{
\partial_\alpha N_0\neq0
}
\]

or

\[
\boxed{
\partial_\alpha(DW_{\same})\text{ couples to }D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]\text{ in mixed branch }12.
}
\]

If either happens, \(\alpha\) contributes to \(\mathcal T_{\par}\) in the \(Y12\)-fiber and must be included in \(B_Y\). Otherwise \(\mathcal T_{\par}\) vanishes in the fiber.

---

# 3. Audit of \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\)

The actual EF second curvature is generated by

\[
\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}
T_{\tr\to\sing}J_{\tr}(F_X^\theta).
\]

The dangerous question is whether such a second derivative can produce

\[
DW_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]]
\]

with mixed branch \(12\).

There are two types of second curvature.

## 3.1 EF-linear second curvature

Second derivatives such as

\[
\partial_m^2,\quad
\partial_m\partial_\lambda,\quad
\partial_\lambda^2,\quad
\partial_\alpha^2,
\]

applied to EF atoms produce log weights, scale derivatives, phase derivatives, and background variations. These remain EF-linear in origin.

They may be second-order in parameters, but they are not primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin terms.

Thus they vanish:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\text{EF-linear second parameter curvature})=0.
}
\]

## 3.2 Nonlinear-source second curvature

If an actual parameter changes the source input before applying \(N_0\), then

\[
\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}N_0(\xi(\theta))
=
D^2N_0[\partial_{\theta_i}\xi,\partial_{\theta_j}\xi]
+
DN_0[\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}\xi].
\]

This can enter the primitive \(Y12\)-sector only if the allowed first-variation slot space spans the \(Y12\) inputs:

\[
(\xi_1,\xi_2)
\in
V_{\arith,1}\times V_{\arith,2}
\]

in the relevant mixed branch and same-point whitening placement.

Therefore the exact criterion is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0
}
\]

provided

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\notin
D^2N_0[V_{\arith},V_{\arith}]
\]

in the primitive \(Y12\)-fiber.

If this membership fails, then \(Y12\) is legitimate actual-source second curvature. That is an arithmetic absorption failure, not a nuisance-quotient issue.

So the theorem is conditional on a finite slot-rank fact:

\[
\boxed{
(\xi_1,\xi_2)\notin V_{\arith,1}\times V_{\arith,2}
\quad
\text{or same-point }DW_{\same}\text{ slot unavailable}.
}
\]

Under that condition:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
}
\]

---

# 4. Audit of \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\)

The singular nuisance module must not kill or identify the primitive \(Y12\) fiber.

The desired condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0.
}
\]

More explicitly, \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\) must be filtered/homogeneous near

\[
\Sigma(Y12).
\]

If

\[
v_Y+v_{<Y}\in\mathcal N_{\sing},
\]

where \(v_Y\) is in the primitive \(Y12\)-sector and \(v_{<Y}\) is in lower/wrong origin sectors, then the quotient must imply

\[
v_Y\in\mathcal N_{\sing}
\quad\text{and}\quad
v_{<Y}\in\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

separately.

The \(Y12\)-route requires

\[
e_Y\notin \mathcal N_{\sing}.
\]

If

\[
e_Y\in\mathcal N_{\sing},
\]

then the singular quotient kills the \(Y12\) carrier and the current channel is rank-zero.

This is not the same as arithmetic absorption. It means the quotient/projection is too coarse or the chosen singular nuisance convention discards the obstruction.

Thus there are two outcomes:

## Safe

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0
\quad\text{and}\quad
e_Y\neq0.
}
\]

## Nuisance overcollapse

\[
\boxed{
e_Y\in\mathcal N_{\sing}
\quad\text{or}\quad
\mathcal N_{\sing}\text{ identifies }Y12\text{ with wrong-origin terms}.
}
\]

In the latter case, the route might be repairable by refining the quotient, unlike arithmetic absorption.

---

# 5. Combined theorem

The current Agent 2 theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{\par}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}+\mathcal N_{\sing})=0
}
\]

if the following four conditions hold.

## Condition 1: first-order source-linearity

All first-order actual-source parameters are source-linear or background/chart variations in the \(Y12\)-origin filtration:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\par}=0.
\]

## Condition 2: no primitive second-curvature slot absorption

Allowed actual-source first variations do not span both primitive \(Y12\) input slots with same-point whitening:

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\notin
D^2N_0[V_{\arith},V_{\arith}]
\quad
\text{in the }Y12\text{ fiber}.
\]

Then

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
\]

## Condition 3: singular nuisance faithfulness

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

is filtered near

\[
\Sigma(Y12)
\]

and does not kill \(e_Y\):

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0.
\]

## Condition 4: labelled realization consistency

The actual raw transition does not create locally free primitive \(Y12\)-amplitude through normal-form recomputation:

\[
\Pi_Y[T_{\tr\to\sing}^{\raw},N_0]
\]

is zero, fixed, residual-controlled, or finite coherent; not locally free.

This last point is logically adjacent to the \(\mathcal T_{\par}\) and \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) audit, because a nonzero normal-form commutator can manufacture apparent primitive \(Y12\)-directions from source-linear inputs.

---

# 6. Failure classification

If the target theorem fails, classify the failure precisely.

## Failure A: parameter tangent produces \(Y12\)

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\par}\neq0.
\]

Then \(Y12\) is first-order actual-source tangent. The local \(Y12\) channel dies.

## Failure B: second curvature produces \(Y12\)

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\neq0.
\]

Then \(Y12\) is legitimate actual-source curvature if this component is vertical or produces rank-two local freedom. The route may die by arithmetic absorption.

If it is graph-like

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

it may be safe and just modifies \(\rho_Y\).

## Failure C: singular nuisance kills \(Y12\)

\[
e_Y\in\mathcal N_{\sing}.
\]

Then this is quotient overcollapse. The route may be repairable by refining \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\).

## Failure D: quotient collapses origin-pair labels

The quotient identifies

\[
(D^2N_0,DW_{\same})
\]

with

\[
(\mathrm{EF\text{-}linear},DW)
\]

or

\[
(N_0,D^2W).
\]

Then \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}\) is not well-defined as a primitive-origin projection.

---

# 7. Consequence for \(B_Y\)

If Agent 2’s theorem holds, then the only remaining contributors to

\[
B_Y
\]

are the lower connection and the local part of \(E_2^Y\):

\[
B_Y
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_7+\mathcal T_{<7}+\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}).
\]

Then the next finite classification becomes simpler:

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

\[
B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

\[
(0,1)\in B_Y,
\]

or

\[
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=2.
\]

This is exactly the branch fork in the current architecture.

---

# Final verdict

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
(\mathcal T_{\par}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}+\mathcal N_{\sing})=0
}
\]

is plausible and should be true if the labelled origin-pair quotient is faithful and actual EF parameter variations do not span the primitive \(Y12\) source-Hessian slots.

The main live risks are:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\text{ contains }D^2N_0[V_{\arith},V_{\arith}]
\text{ in the }Y12\text{ slots,}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\text{ over-collapses the primitive }Y12\text{ sector.}
}
\]

If both are ruled out, the \(B_Y\)-classification reduces to the lower-connection and \(E_2^Y\)-local audits.

## Agent 2 — continued attack: reducing \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) to a slot-rank audit

The remaining nontrivial part of the Agent 2 vanishing theorem is:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
\]

The first-order tangent term \(\mathcal T_{\par}\) should vanish by source-linearity, and the nuisance term \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\) is a quotient-faithfulness audit. But \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) is subtler because \(Y12\) itself is quadratic.

The correct question is no longer:

\[
\text{Can actual parameters have second derivatives?}
\]

Of course they can. The correct question is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Can actual parameter second derivatives generate the primitive }Y12
\text{ input-slot polarization?}
}
\]

---

# 1. Actual second curvature decomposes into two parts

Let the actual EF source be represented as

\[
u(\theta)=N_0(\xi(\theta))
\]

before whitening and singular realization.

Then

\[
\partial_i\partial_j u
=
D^2N_0[\partial_i\xi,\partial_j\xi]
+
DN_0[\partial_i\partial_j\xi].
\]

Only the first term can have primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin.

The second term,

\[
DN_0[\partial_i\partial_j\xi],
\]

has \(DN_0\)-origin, not \(D^2N_0\)-origin, and should vanish in the primitive \(Y12\) quotient:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}DN_0[\partial_i\partial_j\xi]=0.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\text{ is controlled entirely by }
D^2N_0[V_{\arith},V_{\arith}]
\text{ in the }Y12\text{ slots.}
}
\]

---

# 2. Define the actual first-variation slot spaces

Let

\[
V_{\arith}
=
\operatorname{span}
\{\partial_\theta\xi:\theta\in\Theta_{\arith}\}.
\]

Project to the two \(Y12\) source-input slots:

\[
V_1=P_1V_{\arith}\subset U_1^{\mix},
\]

\[
V_2=P_2V_{\arith}\subset U_2^{\mix}.
\]

Also project to the same-point whitening slot:

\[
V_W=P_WV_{\arith}\subset W_{\same}.
\]

The \(Y12\) carrier is represented by a primitive tensor

\[
y_Y
=
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

Actual second curvature can hit the same primitive \(Y12\)-fiber only through

\[
DW_{\same}[a]D^2N_0[v_1,v_2],
\]

with

\[
a\in V_W,\qquad v_1\in V_1,\qquad v_2\in V_2.
\]

So the finite slot-rank obstruction is:

\[
\boxed{
a_Y\otimes \xi_1\otimes \xi_2
\in
V_W\otimes V_1\otimes V_2
\quad ?
}
\]

modulo kernels of \(DW_{\same}\) and \(D^2N_0\).

If the answer is no, then:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
}
\]

---

# 3. Three sufficient vanishings

Any one of the following is sufficient.

## Missing first source slot

\[
\xi_1\notin V_1.
\]

Then no actual second parameter curvature can produce

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

## Missing second source slot

\[
\xi_2\notin V_2.
\]

Same conclusion.

## Missing same-point whitening slot

\[
a_Y\notin V_W.
\]

Then even if the source Hessian appears, it is not coupled through the same \(DW_{\same}\)-slot as \(Y12\).

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\xi_1\notin V_1
\quad\text{or}\quad
\xi_2\notin V_2
\quad\text{or}\quad
a_Y\notin V_W
\Longrightarrow
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
}
\]

This is the finite rank test to perform before any coefficient expansion.

---

# 4. Why \(m,\lambda\) are probably safe

For the ordinary parameters \(m\) and \(\lambda\),

\[
\partial_m\xi,\quad \partial_\lambda\xi
\]

are source-linear deformations of the same EF packet. They act by:

* log-frequency multiplication;
* physical rescaling;
* translation of the local variable;
* derivatives of the same scalar packet.

These are diagonal/coherent deformations of the existing source, not independent mixed-branch \(12\) slot generators.

So the expected slot ranks are small:

\[
\dim P_1\operatorname{span}\{\partial_m\xi,\partial_\lambda\xi\}\le 1,
\]

\[
\dim P_2\operatorname{span}\{\partial_m\xi,\partial_\lambda\xi\}\le 1,
\]

and the paired polarization

\[
D^2N_0[\partial_m\xi,\partial_\lambda\xi]
\]

should have a source-linear/transported signature, not the primitive same-point mixed \(12\) \(Y12\)-signature.

Thus \(m,\lambda\) should not generate \(Y12\), unless the transition formalism defines \(\xi_1,\xi_2\) precisely as \(m,\lambda\) variations. That would be a serious no-go.

---

# 5. The real danger is \(\alpha\)

The \(\alpha\)-direction is the dangerous one because it may change plus/dual branch weights.

There are two cases.

## Safe \(\alpha\)

If \(\alpha\) only changes AFE weights/phases after the source map is formed, then

\[
\partial_\alpha\xi
\]

is source-linear and does not vary the nonlinear input slots of \(N_0\).

Then

\[
P_1\partial_\alpha\xi
\]

and

\[
P_2\partial_\alpha\xi
\]

do not span both primitive \(Y12\) source slots, and

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\partial_\alpha^2u=0.
\]

## Dangerous \(\alpha\)

If \(\alpha\) changes the source-input variables inside \(N_0\), then

\[
D^2N_0[\partial_\alpha\xi,\partial_\alpha\xi]
\]

may appear.

Even then, \(Y12\) is absorbed only if

\[
\partial_\alpha\xi
\]

has enough components to polarize both slots:

\[
(\xi_1,\xi_2)
\in
\operatorname{span}\{P_1\partial_\alpha\xi\}
\times
\operatorname{span}\{P_2\partial_\alpha\xi\}
\]

or, with \(m,\lambda\),

\[
(\xi_1,\xi_2)
\in
P_1\operatorname{span}\{\partial_m,\partial_\lambda,\partial_\alpha\}\xi
\times
P_2\operatorname{span}\{\partial_m,\partial_\lambda,\partial_\alpha\}\xi.
\]

So the \(\alpha\)-compatibility theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
(\xi_1,\xi_2)
\notin
P_1V_{\arith}^{(\alpha)}
\times
P_2V_{\arith}^{(\alpha)}
\quad
\Longrightarrow
\quad
\alpha\text{ does not kill }Y12.
}
\]

---

# 6. Same-point whitening makes the test stricter

Even if

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\]

can be generated by actual parameter curvature, the \(Y12\) carrier also requires a same-point whitening slot:

\[
DW_{\same}[a_Y].
\]

Actual second curvature contributes to the fiber only if the same-point whitening input is also available:

\[
a_Y\in V_W.
\]

Thus the real dangerous condition is not merely

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]\in D^2N_0[V_{\arith},V_{\arith}].
\]

It is:

\[
\boxed{
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\in
DW_{\same}[V_W]D^2N_0[V_1,V_2].
}
\]

This stricter condition should make absorption less likely.

---

# 7. What if \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) is graph-like?

If actual second curvature contributes a vector in the fiber

\[
(s_7,s_9^Y),
\]

it may not be fatal.

There are three possibilities.

## Zero

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
\]

Safe.

## Graph-like

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\subset
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Still safe. It modifies or contributes to the same coherent graph slope \(\rho_Y\).

## Vertical or second slope

If

\[
(0,1)\in \Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9},
\]

or if

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\]

contains two independent graph slopes, then \(Y12\) is locally coherent/absorbed.

Fatal for rank-one \(Y12\).

Thus Agent 2’s target theorem can be weakened:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0
}
\]

is best, but

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\subseteq \mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
}
\]

is still acceptable.

This matters because some actual second curvature may be graph-linked to \(s_7\).

---

# 8. Updated theorem

A more flexible theorem is:

**ActualSecondCurvatureY12Control.**

Let

\[
V_1,V_2,V_W
\]

be the projections of actual first-variation directions to the two \(Y12\) source slots and same-point whitening slot.

If

\[
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\notin
DW_{\same}[V_W]D^2N_0[V_1,V_2]
+
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

in the fiber quotient, then

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\]

does not kill the vertical \(Y12\) channel.

If the image is zero, even better.

If the image lies in

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

it is graph coherence.

If it contains

\[
(0,1)
\]

or a second independent graph slope, the local \(Y12\) channel dies.

---

# 9. Practical audit matrix

Construct matrices:

\[
M_1=[P_1\partial_{\theta_j}\xi]_j,
\]

\[
M_2=[P_2\partial_{\theta_j}\xi]_j,
\]

\[
M_W=[P_W\partial_{\theta_j}\xi]_j.
\]

Then check rank increases:

\[
\operatorname{rank}[M_1\ \xi_1]>\operatorname{rank}M_1,
\]

\[
\operatorname{rank}[M_2\ \xi_2]>\operatorname{rank}M_2,
\]

\[
\operatorname{rank}[M_W\ a_Y]>\operatorname{rank}M_W.
\]

If any rank increases, actual second curvature cannot generate the \(Y12\) carrier.

If none increase, perform the coupled bilinear membership test:

\[
a_Y\otimes\xi_1\otimes\xi_2
\in
V_W\otimes V_1\otimes V_2
\]

modulo the kernels of the relevant maps and modulo the graph line

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

---

# 10. Final Agent 2 update

The \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) audit reduces to a finite slot-rank/polarization test.

The main result:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0
}
\]

is plausible if actual parameter variations are source-linear and do not span both \(Y12\) source slots plus the same-point whitening slot.

Even if actual second curvature contributes, the route survives if it is graph-like:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\subseteq \mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

The fatal cases are:

\[
(0,1)\in\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\]

or a second graph slope independent of

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

So the next finite step is to compute the three slot projections

\[
V_1,V_2,V_W
\]

for the allowed actual parameters.

## Agent 2 — continued attack: parameter-by-parameter slot-rank audit

We reduced

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\]

to a finite slot-rank test. The dangerous object is

\[
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2].
\]

Actual second curvature can absorb \(Y12\) only if the allowed first-variation directions span the three relevant slots:

\[
a_Y\in V_W,\qquad \xi_1\in V_1,\qquad \xi_2\in V_2,
\]

modulo the kernels of \(DW_{\same}\) and \(D^2N_0\), and modulo any safe graph line

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

I now audit the main parameter classes.

---

# 1. Translation parameter \(m\)

For the local source packet,

\[
F_m(x)=\lambda^3q''_{\can}(m+\lambda x),
\]

we have

\[
\partial_mF_m(x)=\lambda^3q'''_{\can}(m+\lambda x).
\]

This is a derivative of the same scalar source. It is source-linear and diagonal with respect to the branch packet. It does not introduce a new independent mixed-branch pair.

Thus the \(m\)-variation contributes a single coherent source direction

\[
v_m:=\partial_m\xi.
\]

Its possible Hessian contribution is

\[
D^2N_0[v_m,v_m]
\]

or, with another parameter,

\[
D^2N_0[v_m,v_\theta].
\]

For \(m\) alone to absorb \(Y12\), one would need both

\[
\xi_1\in \mathbf C P_1v_m,
\qquad
\xi_2\in \mathbf C P_2v_m.
\]

That would mean the two \(Y12\) mixed slots are both generated by translating the same source packet.

This is unlikely unless the \(Y12\) slots are not genuinely independent mixed-block slots.

So the finite test is:

\[
\operatorname{rank}[P_1v_m\ \xi_1]
=
\operatorname{rank}[P_1v_m]
\]

and

\[
\operatorname{rank}[P_2v_m\ \xi_2]
=
\operatorname{rank}[P_2v_m].
\]

If either rank increases, \(m\)-curvature cannot absorb \(Y12\).

Expected result:

\[
\boxed{
m\text{-variation alone is safe.}
}
\]

---

# 2. Scale parameter \(\lambda\)

We have

\[
\partial_\lambda F_m(x)
=
3\lambda^2q''_{\can}(m+\lambda x)
+
\lambda^3xq'''_{\can}(m+\lambda x).
\]

Again, this is a coherent source-linear deformation of the same packet. It combines the original source with \(x\)-weighted translation derivative.

Let

\[
v_\lambda:=\partial_\lambda\xi.
\]

Scale and translation together span at most a small source-linear space

\[
V_{m,\lambda}=\operatorname{span}\{v_m,v_\lambda\}.
\]

Actual second curvature from \(m,\lambda\) lies inside

\[
D^2N_0[V_{m,\lambda},V_{m,\lambda}].
\]

So \(m,\lambda\) absorb \(Y12\) only if

\[
(\xi_1,\xi_2)
\in
P_1V_{m,\lambda}\times P_2V_{m,\lambda}
\]

and

\[
a_Y\in P_WV_{m,\lambda}.
\]

Expected obstruction: \(V_{m,\lambda}\) is too diagonal/coherent to span the primitive mixed-branch \(12\) pair with same-point whitening.

Thus the concrete condition to prove is:

\[
\boxed{
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\notin
DW_{\same}[P_WV_{m,\lambda}]
D^2N_0[P_1V_{m,\lambda},P_2V_{m,\lambda}].
}
\]

If this holds, then

\[
\partial_m^2,\quad \partial_m\partial_\lambda,\quad \partial_\lambda^2
\]

do not contribute vertical \(Y12\).

---

# 3. Phase / normalization / chart parameters

Phase and normalization parameters usually act by scalar multiplication, phase rotation, or gauge normalization of already formed source atoms.

Their first variations are of the form

\[
v_{\mathrm{phase}}=\chi_{\mathrm{phase}}\xi,
\]

\[
v_{\mathrm{norm}}=\chi_{\mathrm{norm}}\xi,
\]

or chart-linear combinations of existing source-linear directions.

They can enlarge

\[
V_{\arith}
\]

but normally do not create new mixed source slots.

They are dangerous only if a chart or normalization parameter changes the same-point whitening input:

\[
a_Y\in V_W.
\]

Even then, to absorb \(Y12\), they must also supply both source slots:

\[
\xi_1\in V_1,\qquad \xi_2\in V_2.
\]

So the audit splits:

## Source-slot test

Do these parameters enlarge

\[
V_1,V_2
\]

to include

\[
\xi_1,\xi_2?
\]

Usually no.

## Whitening-slot test

Do they include

\[
a_Y
\]

in

\[
V_W?
\]

Possibly, if the whitening geometry is varied as an actual-source parameter.

But a whitening-only variation without both source slots cannot produce the full primitive \(Y12\) tensor.

Thus chart/normalization parameters are safe if either:

\[
a_Y\notin V_W
\]

or

\[
(\xi_1,\xi_2)\notin V_1\times V_2.
\]

---

# 4. \(\alpha\)-parameter

This is the main danger.

Let

\[
v_\alpha:=\partial_\alpha\xi.
\]

There are two structurally different versions of \(\alpha\).

## 4.1 \(\alpha\) as proof-thickening / AFE weight tilt

If \(\alpha\) changes only the weights or phases of already formed AFE atoms, then

\[
v_\alpha
\]

is source-linear. Its second derivative

\[
\partial_\alpha^2\xi
\]

is also source-linear.

Then

\[
\partial_\alpha^2u
=
D^2N_0[v_\alpha,v_\alpha]
+
DN_0[\partial_\alpha^2\xi]
\]

could still have a Hessian term. But it absorbs \(Y12\) only if

\[
(P_1v_\alpha,P_2v_\alpha)
\]

spans

\[
(\xi_1,\xi_2).
\]

A single \(\alpha\)-direction generally produces only one branch-contrast vector, not two independent mixed slots. Therefore it is safe if

\[
\dim P_1\operatorname{span}\{v_\alpha\}<\dim\operatorname{span}\{\xi_1\}
\]

or

\[
\dim P_2\operatorname{span}\{v_\alpha\}<\dim\operatorname{span}\{\xi_2\}
\]

in the relevant labelled subspaces.

More concretely:

\[
\boxed{
(\xi_1,\xi_2)
\notin
P_1\mathbf C v_\alpha
\times
P_2\mathbf C v_\alpha
\Longrightarrow
\alpha^2\text{-curvature does not absorb }Y12.
}
\]

## 4.2 \(\alpha\) as genuine source deformation

If \(\alpha\) changes the nonlinear source map or the source variables inside \(N_0\), then \(v_\alpha\) may be a true source-slot direction.

Then \(\alpha\) is dangerous if

\[
P_1v_\alpha\parallel \xi_1
\]

and

\[
P_2v_\alpha\parallel \xi_2.
\]

Even if a single \(\alpha\)-direction cannot span both independently, mixed derivatives such as

\[
\partial_m\partial_\alpha u
\]

and

\[
\partial_\lambda\partial_\alpha u
\]

produce

\[
D^2N_0[v_m,v_\alpha],
\qquad
D^2N_0[v_\lambda,v_\alpha].
\]

Thus the true \(\alpha\)-danger condition is:

\[
\boxed{
(\xi_1,\xi_2)
\in
P_1\operatorname{span}\{v_m,v_\lambda,v_\alpha\}
\times
P_2\operatorname{span}\{v_m,v_\lambda,v_\alpha\}.
}
\]

And also

\[
a_Y\in P_W\operatorname{span}\{v_m,v_\lambda,v_\alpha\}
\]

if whitening variation is needed.

If this membership holds, then \(\alpha\)-admission may turn \(Y12\) into actual-source second curvature.

If it fails, \(\alpha\) is compatible with \(Y12\)-normality.

---

# 5. Background/gamma parameters

Background/gamma parameters such as \(B_2\)-type variations are coherent background, not primitive mixed branch \(12\) source-Hessian terms.

They should have origin labels:

\[
\gamma/\mathrm{background}
\]

or

\[
\mathrm{EF\text{-}linear}.
\]

Thus

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\text{background/gamma first and second variations})=0
}
\]

unless the background variation enters \(N_0\) as a nonlinear source slot, which would be a definitional problem.

This should be safe.

---

# 6. Summary matrix of parameter danger

Let

\[
V_{\safe}=\operatorname{span}\{v_m,v_\lambda,v_{\phase},v_{\norm},v_{\chart},v_{\bg}\}.
\]

Let

\[
V_{\alpha}=V_{\safe}+\mathbf C v_\alpha
\]

if \(\alpha\) is admitted.

Then:

## Fixed-\(\alpha\) downstream

Need

\[
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\notin
DW_{\same}[P_WV_{\safe}]
D^2N_0[P_1V_{\safe},P_2V_{\safe}].
\]

## \(\alpha\)-family downstream

Need the same condition with \(V_{\alpha}\):

\[
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\notin
DW_{\same}[P_WV_{\alpha}]
D^2N_0[P_1V_{\alpha},P_2V_{\alpha}].
\]

If true, \(\alpha\)-family use is compatible with \(Y12\)-normality.

If false only after adding \(v_\alpha\), then \(\alpha\)-admission kills \(Y12\), and \(\alpha\) can only be used as a proof-thickening parameter if one has a specialization/persistence theorem back to fixed \(\alpha\).

---

# 7. Relation to \(B_Y\)

If \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) contributes nothing, then it does not enlarge \(B_Y\).

If it contributes a graph-like direction

\[
(1,\rho_Y),
\]

then it is safe and may modify the formal graph slope.

If it contributes a vertical direction

\[
(0,1),
\]

then

\[
(0,1)\in B_Y
\]

and the \(Y12\)-vertical channel dies.

If it contributes a second graph slope

\[
(1,\rho')
\quad
\text{with}\quad
\rho'\neq \rho_Y,
\]

then

\[
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=2
\]

and the local \(Y12\)-fiber is fully absorbed.

So the output of the slot-rank audit should be classified as:

\[
0,\quad
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),\quad
\mathbf C(0,1),\quad
\text{or second slope}.
\]

---

# 8. Strong form of the Agent 2 theorem

The strongest useful theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal T_{\par}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}+\mathcal N_{\sing})=0.
}
\]

A more flexible theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}(\mathcal T_{\par}+\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}+\mathcal N_{\sing})
\subseteq
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
}
\]

The first keeps the path open to rank-two D4 if lower connection also vanishes.

The second still permits the rank-one moving-wall route.

The fatal alternatives are vertical or rank two actual-source model.

---

# 9. What should be computed next

For each allowed parameter \(\theta\), compute:

\[
P_1v_\theta,\quad P_2v_\theta,\quad P_Wv_\theta.
\]

Form matrices:

\[
M_1=[P_1v_\theta]_\theta,
\]

\[
M_2=[P_2v_\theta]_\theta,
\]

\[
M_W=[P_Wv_\theta]_\theta.
\]

Then test:

\[
\xi_1\in\operatorname{ran}M_1?
\]

\[
\xi_2\in\operatorname{ran}M_2?
\]

\[
a_Y\in\operatorname{ran}M_W?
\]

If any answer is no, actual second curvature cannot absorb \(Y12\).

If all are yes, perform the coupled bilinear test:

\[
a_Y\otimes\xi_1\otimes\xi_2
\in
\operatorname{span}
\{P_Wv_i\otimes P_1v_j\otimes P_2v_k\}
\]

with the same parameter-pair restrictions relevant for second derivatives.

This is the concrete finite audit.

---

# Final Agent 2 status

The \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) question is now a finite slot-rank problem.

Expected outcome:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0
\]

for fixed-\(\alpha\) actual-source parameters, because \(m,\lambda,\) chart, phase, and background variations are source-linear/diagonal and should not span the primitive mixed \(12\) source-Hessian slots plus same-point whitening slot.

The only serious risk is \(\alpha\) if it is treated as a genuine source deformation rather than a proof-thickening or AFE weight parameter.

Thus the \(\alpha\)-compatibility test is:

\[
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\in
DW_{\same}[P_WV_\alpha]D^2N_0[P_1V_\alpha,P_2V_\alpha]
\quad ?
\]

If no, \(\alpha\) is safe. If yes, \(\alpha\)-admission may kill \(Y12\).

## Agent 2 — continued attack: diagonal-variation lemma for \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\)

I continue from the slot-rank reduction:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0
\]

unless actual first-variation directions span the two primitive \(Y12\) source slots and the same-point whitening slot. The latest prompt’s architecture still treats this as part of the \(B_Y\)-classification fork. 

The next useful step is to isolate a general lemma:

\[
\boxed{
\text{diagonal actual-source variations cannot generate primitive mixed }Y12.
}
\]

This would dispose of \(m,\lambda,\) phase, normalization, background, and most chart parameters in one stroke.

---

# 1. Diagonal-variation model

Let the actual-source first variations have the form

\[
v_\theta = M_\theta \xi,
\]

where \(M_\theta\) is a scalar, diagonal, or branch-preserving operator acting on the same source packet \(\xi\).

Examples:

\[
\partial_m \xi \sim (\log n)\xi,
\]

\[
\partial_\lambda \xi \sim (3+x\partial_x)\xi,
\]

\[
\partial_\alpha \xi \sim \chi_\alpha(n)\xi
\]

if \(\alpha\) is only an AFE weight/phase tilt.

Then

\[
D^2N_0[v_{\theta_i},v_{\theta_j}]
=
D^2N_0[M_i\xi,M_j\xi].
\]

This is quadratic, but it is quadratic in the **same coherent source packet**. It is not automatically the promoted mixed-block carrier

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\]

unless the two \(Y12\) slots \(\xi_1,\xi_2\) are themselves contained in the branch-preserving span of \(\xi\).

So define the diagonal variation span:

\[
V_{\diag}:=\{M\xi:M\in\mathcal A_{\diag}\}.
\]

Then the safe condition is:

\[
\boxed{
(\xi_1,\xi_2)\notin P_1V_{\diag}\times P_2V_{\diag}.
}
\]

If this holds, every diagonal actual-source curvature vanishes in the primitive \(Y12\)-fiber.

---

# 2. Primitive mixed-block separation

The promoted \(Y12\) carrier came from

\[
Y_{12}^{(2)}=D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2],
\]

where the two inputs are mixed-block source-loss directions.

The important structural distinction is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{diagonal variation: }D^2N_0[M_i\xi,M_j\xi];
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{primitive }Y12\text{: }D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]\text{ with independently labeled mixed-block slots.}
}
\]

A diagonal variation can mimic \(Y12\) only if the actual-source packet \(\xi\) already contains independently accessible components in both mixed slots and the allowed operators \(M_i,M_j\) can isolate them.

Thus the precise no-go condition for diagonal safety is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{the allowed diagonal multipliers cannot separately project onto the two }Y12\text{ input slots.}
}
\]

This is a finite projection/rank condition.

Let

\[
\mathcal M_{\diag}
\]

be the allowed diagonal multiplier algebra generated by

\[
1,\log n,x\partial_x,\chi_\alpha,\text{ phase/weight multipliers}.
\]

Then the dangerous condition is:

\[
\xi_1\in P_1(\mathcal M_{\diag}\xi),
\qquad
\xi_2\in P_2(\mathcal M_{\diag}\xi).
\]

If either fails, diagonal actual curvature cannot absorb \(Y12\).

---

# 3. Stronger parity/branch-label argument

There is likely an even cleaner proof by branch labels.

If \(Y12\) has a branch-polarized label

\[
\mathrm{mixed}\ 12
\]

and diagonal actual variations preserve branch label, then

\[
D^2N_0[M_i\xi,M_j\xi]
\]

lies in the symmetrized diagonal branch sector generated by the actual packet, not the primitive ordered mixed \(12\) sector.

The sufficient theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\mathrm{mixed}\ 12}
D^2N_0[\mathcal M_{\diag}\xi,\mathcal M_{\diag}\xi]=0.
}
\]

or, if there is a mixed part,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\Sigma(Y12)}
D^2N_0[\mathcal M_{\diag}\xi,\mathcal M_{\diag}\xi]=0
}
\]

because it lacks the same-point whitening slot or source-grade/reflection parity.

This would bypass detailed rank computations.

So two possible proof routes:

1. **Rank route:** show \(\xi_1,\xi_2\) are not in the diagonal variation slot spans.
2. **Signature route:** show diagonal variations cannot enter the \(\Sigma(Y12)\) sector.

The signature route is preferable if the branch/parity labels are rigorously available.

---

# 4. Same-point whitening slot

Even if source-slot danger remains, the same-point whitening slot gives another independent blocker.

Actual parameter variations may affect whitening through

\[
DW[a_\theta].
\]

But the \(Y12\) carrier requires the specific same-point slot

\[
DW_{\same}[a_Y].
\]

Thus actual curvature contributes to \(Y12\) only if

\[
a_Y\in V_W.
\]

For \(m,\lambda,\) and phase variations, the whitening variation is usually induced by smooth global changes of the Gram data, not the same-point mixed source-loss slot.

So a likely theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
a_Y\notin V_W(m,\lambda,\mathrm{phase},\mathrm{background}).
}
\]

If true, all those parameter curvatures vanish in \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}\), independent of source-slot issues.

This makes the same-point whitening slot the easiest kill criterion.

---

# 5. Refined \(\alpha\) audit

\(\alpha\) now has three possible statuses.

## Status A: pure proof-thickening / branch weight

Then

\[
v_\alpha=M_\alpha\xi
\]

for a branch weight multiplier.

If \(M_\alpha\) is diagonal and does not vary \(N_0\) or \(DW_{\same}\), then it is covered by the diagonal-variation lemma.

Conclusion:

\[
\boxed{\Pi_{\fib,Y}\partial_\alpha^2u=0.}
\]

## Status B: branch splitter

If \(\alpha\) genuinely varies plus/dual branch components independently, then

\[
V_\alpha
\]

may contain one branch contrast vector.

But one contrast vector still does not necessarily span both mixed slots.

Danger condition:

\[
(\xi_1,\xi_2)\in
P_1\operatorname{span}\{v_\alpha,v_m,v_\lambda\}
\times
P_2\operatorname{span}\{v_\alpha,v_m,v_\lambda\}.
\]

If false, \(\alpha\) is safe.

## Status C: nonlinear source deformation

If \(\alpha\) changes the source variables inside \(N_0\), then \(\partial_\alpha\xi\) may be a genuine \(Y12\)-slot generator.

Then \(\alpha\) is dangerous and must be included in \(B_Y\).

If it gives vertical freedom, \(Y12\) dies. If it gives the same graph line \(\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\), it is safe but modifies \(\rho_Y\). If it gives a second graph slope, \(B_Y\) becomes rank two and the current vertical channel dies.

---

# 6. The key finite matrices

For the actual allowed parameter set \(\Theta\), define

\[
M_1=[P_1 v_\theta]_{\theta\in\Theta},
\]

\[
M_2=[P_2 v_\theta]_{\theta\in\Theta},
\]

\[
M_W=[P_W v_\theta]_{\theta\in\Theta}.
\]

The fast sufficient test is:

\[
\operatorname{rank}[M_1\ \xi_1]>\operatorname{rank}M_1
\]

or

\[
\operatorname{rank}[M_2\ \xi_2]>\operatorname{rank}M_2
\]

or

\[
\operatorname{rank}[M_W\ a_Y]>\operatorname{rank}M_W.
\]

If any holds, then

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0
\]

up to graph-safe terms.

If all fail, compute the coupled bilinear image:

\[
\mathcal B_{\arith}
=
DW_{\same}[V_W]D^2N_0[V_1,V_2],
\]

and test whether the image is:

\[
0,\quad \mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),\quad \mathbf C(0,1),\quad \text{or rank two in the fiber.}
\]

---

My current expectation:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0
}
\]

for fixed-\(\alpha\) actual-source parameters, because \(m,\lambda,\) phase, background, and chart parameters are source-linear/diagonal and should not span the primitive mixed \(12\) inputs plus same-point whitening.

For \(\alpha\), the expected outcome depends on whether the proof treats \(\alpha\) as:

\[

\text{a proof-thickening branch weight}
}
\]

or

\[

}
\]

In the first interpretation, \(\alpha\) is likely safe. In the second, it requires a real slot-rank computation and may kill \(Y12\).

---

# 8. Updated Agent 2 theorem

A concise theorem:

**DiagonalVariationExclusion\(_{Y12}\).** Suppose all allowed actual-source first variations satisfy

\[
v_\theta\in \mathcal M_{\diag}\xi
\]

and the diagonal multiplier algebra \(\mathcal M_{\diag}\) does not separately generate the two primitive mixed \(Y12\) source slots or the same-point whitening slot. Then

\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
\]

If an allowed parameter, especially \(\alpha\), escapes the diagonal class, then classify its contribution by the slot-rank test. It is safe if it is zero or graph-like, fatal if vertical or a second graph slope.

---

# 9. Consequence for \(B_Y\)

=

and the actual parameter curvature does not participate.

---

# Final status

The \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\) contribution can likely be eliminated by a diagonal-variation exclusion lemma.

The main remaining risk is \(\alpha\). The exact question is:

# 1. External \(\alpha\): proof-thickening / branch-weight deformation

In the safe case, \(\alpha\) changes only AFE weights, phases, or branch sampling:

\[
P_2^\alpha,\qquad D_2^\alpha,\qquad \Theta^\alpha,
\]

but it does not change the nonlinear source map

\[
N_0
\]

or the same-point whitening source geometry.

Then \(\alpha\)-variation has the form

\[
\partial_\alpha \xi = M_\alpha \xi
\]

for some branch-weight/log-frequency multiplier

\[
M_\alpha.
\]

Likewise,

\[
\partial_\alpha^2\xi = M_{\alpha,2}\xi.
\]

These are still source-linear deformations.

The second derivative of the composed source is

\[
\partial_\alpha^2 N_0(\xi(\alpha))
=
D^2N_0[M_\alpha\xi,M_\alpha\xi]
+
DN_0[M_{\alpha,2}\xi].
\]

The second term is \(DN_0\)-origin and is killed by \(\Pi_{\fib,Y}\).

The first term is \(D^2N_0\)-origin, but it is diagonal in the \(\alpha\)-variation direction. It can absorb \(Y12\) only if

\[
(\xi_1,\xi_2)
\in
P_1\mathbf C(M_\alpha\xi)
\times
P_2\mathbf C(M_\alpha\xi)

and

\[
a_Y\in P_W\mathbf C(M_\alpha\xi).

Thus, for external \(\alpha\), a simple sufficient condition is:

\[
\boxed{
(\xi_1,\xi_2,a_Y)
\notin
P_1\mathbf C(M_\alpha\xi)
\times
P_2\mathbf C(M_\alpha\xi)
\times
P_W\mathbf C(M_\alpha\xi).
}
\]

Given that \(Y12\) is a mixed two-slot same-point whitening carrier, while \(M_\alpha\xi\) is a single coherent branch-weight deformation, this condition is plausible.

So external \(\alpha\) should be compatible with \(Y12\).

---

# 2. Internal \(\alpha\): actual deformation of source variables

The dangerous case is when \(\alpha\) changes the source variables entering \(N_0\):

\[
\xi=\xi(m,\lambda,\alpha),
\]

with

\[
\partial_\alpha \xi
\]

not just a branch multiplier, but a real source-slot displacement.

Then actual second curvature contains

\[
D^2N_0[\partial_\alpha\xi,\partial_\alpha\xi],
\]

and mixed derivatives contain

\[
D^2N_0[\partial_m\xi,\partial_\alpha\xi],
\]

\[
D^2N_0[\partial_\lambda\xi,\partial_\alpha\xi].
\]

Define

\[
V_\alpha
=
\operatorname{span}\{
\partial_m\xi,\partial_\lambda\xi,\partial_\alpha\xi,
\partial_{\mathrm{phase}}\xi,
\partial_{\mathrm{chart}}\xi
\}.
\]

Then \(\alpha\) is safe iff

DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\notin
DW_{\same}[P_WV_\alpha]D^2N_0[P_1V_\alpha,P_2V_\alpha].
\]

It is dangerous iff this membership holds.

If it holds, there are three subcases.

## Vertical

## Second slope

and the local \(Y12\)-fiber is absorbed. The current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

\(\alpha\) contributes a vertical or second-slope direction. Then the local \(Y12\) route dies.

## Path B: \(\alpha\)-family downstream

## Agent 2 — actual \(s_7\)-motion and one-slope continuation

for the \(Y12\)-fiber graph slope,

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7+\eta_{\mathrm{fixed}}.

\rho_\Gamma
\]

for the wall ratio after subtracting the \(\rho_Y\)-graph,

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9}.
\]

\[
R_{\fold}(x)

for the O2/O3 fold ratio,

\[
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

I will not use \(r\) for any of these.

The prompt’s key correction is that a formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection does **not** automatically place \(\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\) inside \(B_Y\); it enters \(B_Y\) only if the actual-source/nuisance model has an \(s_7\)-tangent. So this audit is conditional: if no actual \(s_7\)-motion exists, the theorem is vacuous and rank-two D4 remains possible. 

---

# 1. Conditional setup

Assume Agent 1 finds an actual-source/nuisance tangent

\[
u_7^{\arith}\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\]

with

\[
s_7(u_7^{\arith})\neq0.
\]

Using the live dual pair

\[
\ell_7,\qquad \ell_9^Y,
\]

define

\[
s_7(u)=\ell_7(u),
\]

\[
s_9^Y(u)=\ell_9^Y(u).
\]

Then the actual graph slope is

\[
\rho_Y^{\arith}
=
\frac{s_9^Y(u_7^{\arith})}{s_7(u_7^{\arith})}.
\]

The formal lower-connection slope is

\[
\rho_Y^{\form}
=
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal R_{7\to9}^{Y}e_7),
\]

assuming \(e_7\) is normalized by

\[
\ell_7(e_7)=1.
\]

The desired theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\rho_Y^{\arith}=\rho_Y^{\form}.
}
\]

If true, actual \(s_7\)-motion continues uniquely into the graph line

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

---

# 2. The real theorem: actual \(s_7\)-motion factors through the formal connection

The precise statement should be:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}u
=
s_7(u)(1,\rho_Y)
}
\]

for every actual-source/nuisance tangent \(u\) whose fiber image is generated by the \(s_7\)-antecedent path.

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
(\ell_9^Y-\rho_Y\ell_7)(u)=0.
}
\]

This says the graph-killing covector

\[
\ell_Y=\ell_9^Y-\rho_Y\ell_7
\]

annihilates actual \(s_7\)-motion.

It does **not** say that \(s_7\)-motion exists. It only says that if it exists, its \(Y12\)-continuation is one-slope.

---

# 3. Determinant test for multiple actual \(s_7\)-directions

If there are several actual-source tangents

\[
u_i
\]

with nonzero \(s_7\)-projection, then all must have the same slope.

The finite determinant condition is:

\[
\Delta_Y(u_i,u_j)
=
\ell_7(u_i)\ell_9^Y(u_j)
-
\ell_7(u_j)\ell_9^Y(u_i)
=
0
\]

for every pair \(u_i,u_j\).

If this holds and at least one \(\ell_7(u_i)\neq0\), then

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\operatorname{span}\{u_i\}
\subseteq
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

If some pair has

\[
\Delta_Y(u_i,u_j)\neq0,
\]

then \(B_Y\) is rank two locally and the current vertical \(Y12\) channel is absorbed.

---

# 4. Relation to the formal connection

The formal connection coefficient

\[
\rho_Y^{\form}
\]

is usable only if the actual tangent \(u_7^{\arith}\) is the actual realization of the formal \(e_7\)-antecedent.

The required compatibility is:

\[
u_7^{\arith}
=
a\,e_7
+
u_{\mathrm{nonlive}}
+
u_{\mathrm{nuis}}
\]

with

\[
a\neq0
\]

and where

\[
\ell_7(u_{\mathrm{nonlive}})=\ell_9^Y(u_{\mathrm{nonlive}})=0,
\]

\[
\ell_7(u_{\mathrm{nuis}})=\ell_9^Y(u_{\mathrm{nuis}})=0
\]

in the realized fiber.

Then:

\[
\frac{\ell_9^Y(u_7^{\arith})}{\ell_7(u_7^{\arith})}
=
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal R_{7\to9}^{Y}e_7)
=
\rho_Y^{\form}.
\]

If this compatibility fails, then the formal connection does not determine the actual graph slope.

---

# 5. Possible causes of \(\rho_Y^{\arith}\neq\rho_Y^{\form}\)

If the slopes differ, the failure should be routed precisely.

## A. Formal connection is not actual-source connection

The formal carrier lift

\[
e_7\mapsto e_7+\mathcal R_{7\to9}^{Y}e_7
\]

exists in the carrier algebra, but actual-source rows realize a different continuation.

Then \(\rho_Y^{\form}\) should **not** be used in the wall.

The actual slope is

\[
\rho_Y^{\arith}.
\]

But this is dangerous unless \(\rho_Y^{\arith}\) is still low-order and not fitted from actual \(s_9^Y\)-data.

## B. Labelled-realization shifts the slope

A labelled-realization coherent term can modify

\[
s_9^Y
\]

by a graph-like component.

Then

\[
\rho_Y
=
\rho_Y^{\form}
+
\rho_Y^{\lab}
\]

provided \(\rho_Y^{\lab}\) is formal/coherent and low-order.

This is safe only if the correction is not extracted from actual order-nine residual data.

## C. \(E_2^Y\) modifies the graph

If \(E_2^Y\) contributes a graph-like local tangent, then

\[
\rho_Y
=
\rho_Y^{\form}
+
\rho_Y^{E_2}.
\]

Again safe only if \(\rho_Y^{E_2}\) is fixed/coherent and low-order, not locally fitted.

## D. Quotient overcollapse

If the singular quotient identifies \(e_7\) or \(e_Y\) with nuisance in a way that changes the measured fiber coordinates, then the slope may be an artifact.

Then the live dual pair itself must be repaired.

---

# 6. Low-order/honesty conditions for \(\rho_Y\)

If the actual one-slope theorem holds, we still need:

\[
\boxed{
\rho_Y\in\mathcal A_{\low}.
}
\]

Also:

\[
\boxed{
\partial_{\Gamma_{G,2}}\rho_Y=0,
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\partial_{q^{(4)}}\rho_Y=0,
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\partial_{s_9^Y}\rho_Y=0.
}
\]

This is the no-tautology condition.

A safe \(\rho_Y\) is computed from formal lower connection data:

\[
\rho_Y=\ell_9^Y(\mathcal R_{7\to9}^{Y}e_7)
\]

plus fixed/coherent low-order corrections.

An unsafe \(\rho_Y\) is fitted as

\[
\rho_Y^{\bad}
=
\frac{s_9^Y(q''_{\can})-\eta_{\fixed}}{s_7(q''_{\can})}.
\]

That would make the wall tautological.

---

# 7. Consequence for D4

If the one-slope theorem holds, then

\[
B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

from this actual \(s_7\)-motion contribution, unless no other contributors exist and \(u_7^{\arith}\) was absent.

D4 gives:

\[
s_7^{D4}=Hc_7\Gamma_G,
\]

\[
s_9^{Y,D4}
=
H(\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

Applying

\[
\ell_Y=\ell_9^Y-\rho_Y\ell_7
\]

gives:

\[
\ell_Y(D4)
=
H\left((\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right)
=
H\alpha_9(\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G).
\]

Thus the rank-one moving wall is forced.

If no actual \(s_7\)-tangent exists, this graph subtraction should not be applied, and the rank-two D4 route remains available.

---

# 8. Failure classification

## Vacuous case

\[
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0.
\]

Then this theorem is vacuous.

Do **not** introduce \(\rho_Y\) into \(B_Y\).

Proceed to rank-two D4 audit.

## Good one-slope case

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\subseteq
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

with \(\rho_Y\in\mathcal A_{\low}\).

Proceed to rank-one moving wall.

## Vertical failure

\[
(0,1)\in \Pi_{\fib,Y}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Then \(Y12\) is actual-source vertical motion. The current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

## Second-slope failure

There exist \(u,v\) with

\[
\Delta_Y(u,v)\neq0.
\]

Then

\[
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=2.
\]

The local \(Y12\)-fiber is absorbed by actual-source coherence.

## Tautological slope failure

A slope exists but is fitted from actual order-nine data:

\[
\rho_Y=\frac{s_9^Y}{s_7}.
\]

Then the wall is not honest. The route fails unless a formal/low-order expression for \(\rho_Y\) is recovered.

---

# 9. Agent 2 theorem

**ActualS7OneSlope.** Suppose there is an actual-source tangent \(u_7^{\arith}\) with

\[
\ell_7(u_7^{\arith})\neq0.
\]

Assume:

1. every actual-source tangent with nonzero \(s_7\)-coordinate factors through the same formal lower \(C_7\)-antecedent path;

2. the labelled realization and singular quotient preserve the live pair \((e_7,e_Y)\);

3. no lower or parameter correction contributes an independent \(Y12\)-fiber slope;

4. \(\rho_Y=\ell_9^Y(\mathcal R_{7\to9}^{Y}e_7)\in\mathcal A_{\low}\).

Then:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\subseteq
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

and the D4 normal wall is:

\[
H\left((\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right).
\]

---

# Final status

The one-slope theorem is conditional on actual \(s_7\)-motion.

The formal lower connection

\[
e_7\mapsto e_7+\mathcal R_{7\to9}^{Y}e_7
\]

does **not** itself put

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)
\]

inside \(B_Y\).

The decisive prior audit remains:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0
\quad\text{or not?}
}
\]

If zero, rank-two D4 may survive.

If nonzero and one-slope, the rank-one moving wall is the correct route.

## Agent 2 — continued attack: deciding whether \(s_7\) is actual-source motion

The prior result was conditional:

\[
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\neq0
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\text{test one-slope continuation }s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7.
\]

But the formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection does **not** itself put \((1,\rho_Y)\) inside \(B_Y\). It enters \(B_Y\) only if \(s_7\) is an actual-source/nuisance direction, not merely a D4 carrier. This is the key fork emphasized in the latest coordinator synthesis. 

So the next audit is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0\quad\text{or not?}
}
\]

---

# 1. Define the actual \(s_7\)-tangent test

Let

\[
\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\]

be the actual-source model projected to the primitive \(Y12\) fiber. Its tangent/local model includes only directions genuinely available to actual rows:

\[
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
=
\mathcal T_{\par}
+
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\mathrm{lab}}^{\local}
+
\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\actual}.
\]

It should **not** include D4-only formal carrier directions.

The test is:

\[
\boxed{
\exists u\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
\quad\text{such that}\quad
s_7(u)=\ell_7(u)\neq0?
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\neq0?
}
\]

If no, then the formal \(C_7\) carrier is D4-only for the actual-source model, and the graph line \(\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\) must **not** be quotiented.

---

# 2. Candidate contributors to actual \(s_7\)-motion

The possible sources of actual \(s_7\)-motion are:

\[
\mathcal T_{\par},
\quad
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9},
\quad
\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local},
\quad
\mathcal T_{\mathrm{lab}}^{\local},
\quad
\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\actual}.
\]

Audit each one.

---

## 2.1 First-order actual parameters \(\mathcal T_{\par}\)

For \(m,\lambda,\) phase, normalization, chart, and background parameters, the earlier diagonal/source-linear argument says these should not enter the primitive \(Y12\) sector at all.

In particular, they should satisfy

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\par}=0.
\]

Therefore

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}\mathcal T_{\par}=0
}
\]

unless some parameter explicitly moves the order-seven carrier coordinate in the primitive \(Y12\)-fiber.

Expected result:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{\par}\text{ does not generate actual }s_7\text{-motion.}
}
\]

Exception:

If a chart/normalization parameter is defined exactly as an order-seven carrier gauge, then it may generate \(s_7\). But then it is a gauge/nuisance direction, and we must ask whether it is actually needed by global rows or merely formal representative freedom.

---

## 2.2 Actual second curvature \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\)

Second curvature mostly targets order-nine data. It may create \(s_9^Y\), but it does not naturally create a free \(s_7\) coordinate unless the same second curvature also changes the lower carrier.

So the test is:

\[
\Pi_{s_7}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0?
\]

Using the slot-rank framework, actual second curvature contributes to \(s_7\) only if its source-slot/polarization has an order-seven component in the live pair.

Expected:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0
}
\]

for fixed-\(\alpha\) actual-source parameters.

If it is nonzero, then ask whether the induced pair is graph-like:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
\subseteq \mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

or vertical/second slope.

---

## 2.3 Local \(E_2^Y\) freedoms

\(E_2^Y\) can contribute to the fiber only if it is local at the extraction scale. Fixed or finite-dimensional coherent \(E_2^Y\) belongs to the amplitude model \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\), not to local \(B_Y\).

So define

\[
\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}
=
\text{extraction-local }E_2^Y\text{ tangent directions}.
\]

Then test:

\[
\Pi_{s_7}\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}\neq0?
\]

There are four cases.

### Case A: absent/fixed/residual

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}=0.
\]

Safe.

### Case B: graph-like local \(E_2^Y\)

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}
\subseteq \mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

This forces rank-one, but does not kill \(Y12\).

### Case C: vertical local \(E_2^Y\)

\[
(0,1)\in \Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}.
\]

Fatal locally.

### Case D: second slope

\[
(1,\rho_E),\quad \rho_E\neq\rho_Y.
\]

Then \(B_Y\) becomes rank two and the current vertical \(Y12\) route dies locally.

Thus \(E_2^Y\) only creates \(s_7\)-motion if it is locally free or graph-like in the fiber. Finite coherent amplitude \(E_2^Y\) does not count as \(B_Y\).

---

## 2.4 Labelled-realization local mismatch

Similarly, labelled-realization residuals enter \(B_Y\) only if they are local at the fiber level.

Let

\[
\mathcal T_{\mathrm{lab}}^{\local}
\]

be the locally free part of

\[
\Pi_YR_{\lab}.
\]

If labelled consistency gives only fixed/finite coherent/residual-small mismatch, then

\[
\mathcal T_{\mathrm{lab}}^{\local}=0.
\]

So:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}\mathcal T_{\mathrm{lab}}^{\local}=0
}
\]

under LabelledRealizationConsistency\(_{Y12}\).

If nonzero and local, classify it by the same route:

\[
0,\quad \mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),\quad \mathbf C(0,1),\quad \text{second slope}.
\]

---

## 2.5 Singular nuisance \(\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\actual}\)

A nuisance direction belongs to \(B_Y\) only if the quotient treats it as an allowed local representative freedom for actual rows.

If

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\]

kills \(e_7\), then \(s_7\) may be quotient-nuisance. But this is a quotient design issue, not necessarily actual-source coherence.

Two possibilities:

### Safe nuisance

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\actual}=0.
\]

Then \(e_7,e_Y\) remain live.

### Overcollapse

\[
e_7\in\mathcal N_{\sing}
\quad\text{or}\quad
e_Y\in\mathcal N_{\sing}.
\]

Then the live dual pair fails or the \(Y12\)-route is killed by the quotient.

So nuisance does not usually create a graph line; it either vanishes or over-collapses.

---

# 3. Operational criterion for \(s_7\)-actual motion

The actual \(s_7\)-motion test becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}
\left(
\mathcal T_{\par}
+
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\mathrm{lab}}^{\local}
+
\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\actual}
\right)
\neq0?
}
\]

Under the expected vanishings:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\par}=0,
\]

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0,
\]

\[
\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}=0,
\]

\[
\mathcal T_{\mathrm{lab}}^{\local}=0,
\]

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\actual}=0,
\]

we get:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
B_Y=0
}
\]

unless there is another local contributor. Rank-two D4 becomes available.

This is now a plausible best-case branch.

---

# 4. If \(s_7\)-motion exists

Suppose some contributor gives

\[
u_7^{\arith}
\]

with

\[
s_7(u_7^{\arith})\neq0.
\]

Then the next theorem is the one-slope continuation:

\[
s_9^Y(u_7^{\arith})=\rho_Ys_7(u_7^{\arith}).
\]

But \(\rho_Y\) must be formal/coherent, not fitted:

\[
\rho_Y
=
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal R_{7\to9}^Ye_7)
\]

plus any fixed graph-like correction from \(E_2^Y\) or \(R_{\lab}\).

Then \(B_Y\) contains

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

If all actual \(s_7\)-directions share that slope, rank-one wall.

If not, route dies locally.

---

# 5. Rank-two branch if \(B_Y=0\)

If

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

the D4 live matrix is

\[
M_{\D4}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7 & 0\\
\beta_9 & \alpha_9
\end{pmatrix},
\qquad
c_7\alpha_9\neq0.
\]

Therefore D4 gives two independent fiber directions:

\[
H(c_7\Gamma_G,\ \beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

This is polynomially equivalent to

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2})
\]

provided

\[
|c_7|\ge Q^{-A},\quad |\alpha_9|\ge Q^{-A}
\]

and coefficients are \(Q^A\)-bounded.

So the wall theorem becomes joint anisotropy/sublevel for

\[
(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}),
\]

not moving-line finite type for

\[
\Gamma_{G,2}+\rho_\Gamma\Gamma_G.
\]

This would bypass much of the rank-one apparatus.

Thus the rank-two branch is genuinely high value.

---

# 6. Rank-one branch if \(B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\)

If

\[
B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

then the quotient by actual-source graph coherence leaves the covector

\[
\ell_Y=\ell_9^Y-\rho_Y\ell_7.
\]

D4 becomes:

\[
\ell_Y(D4)
=
H\left((\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7)\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}\right).
\]

Then the rank-one tower is needed:

1. WallHonesty\(_{Y12}\):

   \[
   \rho_\Gamma\in\mathcal A_{\low}
   \]

   and no dependence on

   \[
   \Gamma_{G,2},q^{(4)},s_9^Y.
   \]

2. Amplitude Schur if \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\neq0\).

3. Wall finite type or trap Schur.

---

# 7. Updated best theorem

A useful near-term theorem is:

**NoActualS7Motion\(_Y\).** Under fixed-\(\alpha\) downstream, labelled-realization consistency, \(E_2^Y\) nonlocal/fixed/residual, and origin-faithful singular quotient,

\[
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0.
\]

Therefore:

\[
B_Y=0
\]

unless lower actual-source or local \(E_2^Y\)/labelled residual contributors are explicitly exhibited.

This theorem would open the rank-two D4 route.

If it fails, the exhibited \(s_7\)-tangent becomes the input to ActualS7OneSlope.

---

# 8. What to compute immediately

The concrete finite outputs needed are:

\[
\Pi_{s_7}\mathcal T_{\par},
\]

\[
\Pi_{s_7}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9},
\]

\[
\Pi_{s_7}\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local},
\]

\[
\Pi_{s_7}\mathcal T_{\mathrm{lab}}^{\local},
\]

\[
\Pi_{s_7}\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\actual}.
\]

Each should be classified as:

\[
0,
\quad
\text{graph-like},
\quad
\text{vertical/second slope},
\quad
\text{overcollapse}.
\]

The key target is to prove all are zero.

---

# Final Agent 2 status

The formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection is conditional. It only matters if actual-source rows can move in \(s_7\).

The decisive first fork is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0?
}
\]

If yes, rank-two D4 likely survives.

If no, then one-slope continuation determines whether the route becomes rank one or dies locally.

Given the existing diagonal/source-linearity and fixed-\(\alpha\) assumptions, the best-case theorem

\[
\Pi_{s_7}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0
\]

is plausible and should be attacked before committing to the rank-one wall hierarchy.

for the lower \(Y12\)-fiber graph slope,

for the effective wall ratio, and

for the O2/O3 fold ratio. I will not use \(r\) for any of these.

\Pi_{s_9^Y}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0
\]

or whether some allowed direction produces \((0,1)\) in the local fiber. This is part of the rank-two fork: rank two requires \(B_Y=0\), so both the \(s_7\)- and \(s_9^Y\)-coordinates must be absent from the actual-source/nuisance/local model. 

---

# Verdict

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_9^Y}T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}=0
\text{ is plausible for fixed-\(\alpha\) downstream.}
}
\]

More precisely:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{\par},\ \mathcal{II}_{\arith,9},\ \mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
\text{ should have no vertical primitive }Y12\text{ component}
}
\]

under the fixed-\(\alpha\), labelled-origin-faithful setup.

The remaining possible vertical contaminants are:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
}
\]

through either a locally free \(E_2^Y\)-amplitude or a locally free labelled-realization commutator.

Thus the target theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_9^Y}
\left(
\mathcal T_{\par}
+
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
+
\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\right)=0.
}
\]

If true together with the \(s_7\)-vanishing audit, then

\[
B_Y=0
\]

and rank-two D4 becomes the preferred route.

---

# 1. The live \(s_9^Y\)-projection

The projection

\[
\Pi_{s_9^Y}(v)=\ell_9^Y(v)
\]

detects the primitive \(Y12\)-signature order-nine coordinate.

It does **not** detect arbitrary order-nine mass. It detects only the live primitive carrier class

\[
\Sigma(Y12)
=
(2,D^2N_0,DW_{\same},\mathrm{mixed}\ 12,\mathrm{same},1,\pi_Y,9,[\mathsf C_{Y12}]).
\]

Therefore a term can have:

\[
\text{order }9,\quad \text{degree }2,\quad \text{one whitening slot}
\]

and still satisfy

\[
\Pi_{s_9^Y}=0
\]

if its origin is, for example,

\[
DW[\xi]E_2(\eta),
\quad
D^2W[\xi_1,\xi_2]N_0,
\quad
\partial_\alpha^2(\mathrm{EF\ atom}),
\]

rather than

\[
DW_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]].
\]

---

# 2. First tangents \(\mathcal T_{\par}\)

For fixed-\(\alpha\) downstream, the admissible first parameters are:

\[
m,\quad \lambda,\quad \text{phase},\quad \text{normalization/chart},\quad \text{background/gamma}.
\]

Their variations are source-linear, chart-origin, or background-origin. Schematically:

\[
\partial_m\xi = M_m\xi,
\]

\[
\partial_\lambda\xi=M_\lambda\xi,
\]

\[
\partial_{\mathrm{phase}}\xi=M_{\mathrm{phase}}\xi,
\]

with \(M_\theta\) branch-preserving or source-linear.

None of these creates the primitive source-Hessian carrier

\[
D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\]

coupled to the same-point whitening slot.

So the expected vanishing is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\par})=0.
}
\]

The only dangerous exceptions are:

1. a chart/normalization parameter that explicitly introduces the live \(Y12\) carrier;
2. an admitted \(\alpha\)-parameter treated as a genuine internal source deformation;
3. a nuisance convention that identifies source-linear order-nine data with the primitive \(Y12\)-sector.

Under fixed-\(\alpha\), origin-faithful labelled transition, these should not occur.

---

# 3. Actual second curvature \(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}\)

The dangerous condition is the slot-rank membership

\[
DW_{\same}[a_Y]D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\in
DW_{\same}[V_W]D^2N_0[V_1,V_2],
\]

where

\[
V_1=P_1V_{\arith},\qquad V_2=P_2V_{\arith},\qquad V_W=P_WV_{\arith}.
\]

If any one slot is missing,

\[
\xi_1\notin V_1,
\]

or

\[
\xi_2\notin V_2,
\]

or

\[
a_Y\notin V_W,
\]

then

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_9^Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
}
\]

For fixed-\(\alpha\) actual parameters, \(V_{\arith}\) is generated by diagonal/source-linear deformations of the same EF packet. These do not independently span the two primitive mixed \(12\) source slots and the same-point whitening slot.

Thus the fixed-\(\alpha\) expectation is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_9^Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}=0.
}
\]

If \(\alpha\) is admitted as an internal source deformation, this must be rechecked with \(v_\alpha\) included in \(V_{\arith}\). In that case, the vertical no-go is:

\[
(0,1)\in \Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}.
\]

If \(\alpha\) contributes only through a graph line \(\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y)\), it forces rank one but is not locally fatal. If it contributes a second slope, it kills the current \(Y12\)-route locally.

---

# 4. Lower actual directions below seven

The prompt asks specifically for lower \(<7\) actual directions.

The target is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_9^Y}\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}=0.
}
\]

The reason is signature filtration: lower actual directions below seven should not contain the first live primitive \(Y12\) carrier

\[
(D^2N_0,DW_{\same},\mathrm{mixed}\ 12,\mathrm{same},w=1,\rho=9).
\]

The Bottleneck-C “first live primitive \(Y12\)” ledger supports the statement:

\[
\boxed{
\text{No lower actual direction below seven gives an independent primitive }Y12\text{ fiber column.}
}
\]

This must be used carefully. It does **not** prove \(C_7\) is actual-source motion. It only helps rule out earlier independent \(Y12\)-antecedents.

So the desired result is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

If a lower \(<7\) direction has nonzero \(s_9^Y\)-coordinate, classify it:

\[
(0,1)
\]

is vertical and fatal;

\[
(1,\rho')\quad \rho'\neq \rho_Y
\]

is a second slope and fatal;

\[
(1,\rho_Y)
\]

is graph-like and rank-one, not rank-two.

But the expected output is zero.

---

# 5. \(E_2^Y\) local vertical freedom

The \(E_2^Y\) audit is not just “does \(E_2\) have \(Y12\)-signature?”

Define a coefficient map:

\[
c_E(\theta;p)
=
\frac{\Pi_YE_{\model}(\theta;p)}{y_Y(p)}
\]

where meaningful.

For local fiber-level impact, look at its local derivatives:

\[
Dc_E,\qquad D^2c_E.
\]

Possible outputs:

## Safe

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0.
\]

Then \(E_2^Y\) is absent, fixed, finite coherent, or residual-controlled.

## Rank-one only

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
\subseteq
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Then rank two collapses to rank one, but the \(Y12\) route survives.

## Fatal vertical

\[
(0,1)\in
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}.
\]

Then the primitive \(Y12\)-amplitude is locally free and the current route dies.

## Fatal second slope

\[
(1,\rho_E),\qquad \rho_E\neq\rho_Y.
\]

Then \(B_Y\) is rank two as an actual-source model and the vertical \(Y12\)-channel is absorbed.

For rank-two D4, the required outcome is the strongest one:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

Finite coherent \(E_2^Y\) belongs to \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\), not to \(B_Y\).

---

# 6. Labelled commutator vertical freedom

The labelled-realization commutator decomposes as:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^W.
\]

We audit the local fiber component.

## \(\mathfrak C_Y^L\)

If the source transition \(L\) is affine, then

\[
D^2L=0
\]

and

\[
\mathfrak C_Y^L=0.
\]

If not affine, we need:

\[
\Pi_YDN_0[D^2L]=0
\]

by origin filtering.

## \(\mathfrak C_Y^N\)

This is the normal-form naturality defect:

\[
\Pi_Y\left(
D^2N_{0,\sing}[L\xi_1,L\xi_2]
-
S D^2N_{0,\tr}[\xi_1,\xi_2]
\right).
\]

Expected safe output:

\[
0
\]

or finite coherent amplitude.

For rank two, local fiber output must be zero.

## \(\mathfrak C_Y^W\)

This is the whitening transport defect. Moving-whitening artifacts must be separated from

\[
DW_{\same}[D^2N_0[\xi_1,\xi_2]].
\]

Expected:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathfrak C_Y^W=0
\]

if the origin-pair quotient is faithful.

Thus the rank-two condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}=0.
}
\]

If the labelled commutator gives a graph-compatible local direction, rank two collapses to rank one. If vertical or second slope, local \(Y12\) dies.

---

# 7. Singular nuisance

The singular nuisance module should be filtered near \(\Sigma(Y12)\).

Rank-two requires:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}\mathcal N_{\sing}=0.
}
\]

If

\[
e_Y\in\mathcal N_{\sing},
\]

then \(Y12\) is killed.

If

\[
e_7\in\mathcal N_{\sing},
\]

then rank-two may fail because the \(s_7\) live coordinate is not available.

This is quotient overcollapse unless justified by genuine actual-source coherence.

---

# 8. Rank-two condition summarized

Rank two requires the zero result:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left(
\mathcal T_{\par}
+
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
+
\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\right)=0
}
\]

and separately:

\[
\boxed{
\Pi_{s_7}\mathcal T_7^{\arith}=0
}
\]

from Agent 1’s \(s_7\)-actualness audit.

If both hold, then:

\[
B_Y=0,
\]

and rank-two D4 survives.

---

# 9. Final classification

Agent 2’s second-coordinate audit yields:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{\par})=0
}
\]

expected by source-linearity;

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9})=0
}

expected by fixed-\(\alpha\) slot-rank exclusion;

\[
\boxed{
\ell_9^Y(\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith})=0
}
\]

expected by first-live \(Y12\) filtration;

required by quotient faithfulness.

is plausible under the fixed-\(\alpha\), origin-faithful, non-local-free \(E_2^Y\), labelled-consistent setup.

## Agent 2 — continued attack: quantitative \(s_9^Y\)-vanishing tests

The previous audit concluded that rank-two D4 requires

\[
\Pi_{s_9^Y}
\left(
\mathcal T_{\par}
+
\mathcal{II}_{\arith,9}
+

+

+
\mathcal N_{\sing}
\right)=0.
\]

I now turn that into finite tests. The goal is to distinguish:

0,
\qquad
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),
\qquad
\mathbf C(0,1),
\qquad
\mathbb C^2,
\]

because for rank-two we need the strongest outcome:

\[

\]

Graph-like contamination is safe for rank-one but **not** for rank-two.

---

# 1. Use the live dual pair as the measurement device

Assume the live dual pair exists:

\[
\ell_7,\qquad \ell_9^Y,
\]

with

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]

For any candidate contaminant \(u\), define its fiber vector

\[
\mathbf b_Y(u)
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(u)\\
\ell_9^Y(u)
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Then:

* \(u\) is invisible iff

  \[
  \ell_7(u)=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(u)=0.
  \]

* \(u\) is vertical iff

  \[
  \ell_7(u)=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(u)\neq0.
  \]

* \(u\) is graph-compatible iff

  \[
  \ell_9^Y(u)=\rho_Y\ell_7(u).
  \]

* two candidates \(u,v\) create rank two iff

  \[
  \Delta_Y(u,v)
  :=
  \ell_7(u)\ell_9^Y(v)
  -
  \ell_7(v)\ell_9^Y(u)
  \neq0.
  \]

So every audit reduces to applying \((\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)\) to a finite list of actual-source/local/nuisance generators.

---

# 2. First tangents \(\mathcal T_{\par}\): zero test

For a first parameter tangent

\[
u_\theta=\partial_\theta J_{\sing}(q''_{\can}),
\]

the finite test is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(u_\theta)=0,\qquad \ell_9^Y(u_\theta)=0.
}
\]

Expected proof method:

* \(u_\theta\) has EF-linear, chart, background, or gamma origin;
* \(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y\) detect live \(C_7\) and primitive \(D^2N_0\)-origin \(Y12\);
* origin labels are disjoint.

Thus the theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Origin}(u_\theta)\notin \{C_7,\Sigma(Y12)\}
\Longrightarrow
\mathbf b_Y(u_\theta)=0.
}
\]

If a chart/normalization parameter has live \(C_7\)-origin, then Agent 1’s \(s_7\)-actualness branch becomes nonzero. If it has primitive \(Y12\)-origin, rank-two fails.

---

# 3. Actual second curvature: bilinear fiber matrix

For actual second curvature directions,

\[
u_{ij}
=
\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}J_{\sing}(q''_{\can}),
\]

the dangerous part is

\[
DW_{\same}[a]D^2N_0[v_i,v_j].
\]

Define the bilinear fiber map

\[
B_{\II}(i,j)
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(u_{ij})\\
\ell_9^Y(u_{ij})
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

The rank-two route needs:

\[
\boxed{
B_{\II}(i,j)=0
\quad\text{for all allowed }i,j.
}
\]

The finite slot test says \(B_{\II}(i,j)=0\) if at least one of the relevant slots is missing:

\[
P_1v_i,P_1v_j
\text{ cannot produce }\xi_1,
\]

or

\[
P_2v_i,P_2v_j
\text{ cannot produce }\xi_2,
\]

or

\[
P_Wv_i,P_Wv_j
\text{ cannot produce }a_Y.

\]

If

\[
B_{\II}(i,j)\parallel(0,1)
\]

or produces an independent slope, local route dies.

For fixed-\(\alpha\), expected:

}
\]

# 4. Lower \(<7\) actual directions: first-live test

For every lower actual candidate

\[
u_j\in\mathcal T_{<7}^{\arith},

compute

(\ell_7(u_j),\ell_9^Y(u_j)).

unless \(u_j\) factors through the \(C_7\)-path.

But for rank-two we also need:

\quad\text{for all actual lower }u_j<7.
}

If

for some actual lower direction, rank-two collapses to rank-one.

The hidden issue remains actualness: formal lower carriers are not included unless they are actual-source/nuisance directions.

---

# 5. Local \(E_2^Y\): coefficient derivative test

For \(E_2^Y\), use

\[
c_E(\theta;p)=\frac{\Pi_YE_{\model}(\theta;p)}{y_Y(p)}

where meaningful.

But for the full fiber, we need two coefficient components:

\[
c_{E,7}(\theta;p)=\ell_7(E_{\model}(\theta;p)),
\]

\[
c_{E,9}(\theta;p)=\ell_9^Y(E_{\model}(\theta;p)).

Local fiber tangent is detected by derivatives:

\[
D c_E,\qquad D^2 c_E.

Rank-two requires:

\[
\boxed{
D(c_{E,7},c_{E,9})=0,
\qquad
D^2(c_{E,7},c_{E,9})=0
}
\]

at extraction-local level.

If derivatives vanish but \(c_E\) is nonzero fixed, it is a basepoint/amplitude issue, not \(B_Y\).

If derivatives span a finite-dimensional nonlocal family, it belongs to \(\mathcal C_Y^{\arith}\).

If derivatives are local and graph-compatible, rank-two collapses to rank-one.

If derivatives are vertical or second-slope, local \(Y12\) route dies.

# 6. Labelled commutator: componentwise fiber test

The labelled commutator decomposes:

\[
\mathfrak C_Y
=
\mathfrak C_Y^L+\mathfrak C_Y^N+\mathfrak C_Y^W.

For each piece define

\[

=

\ell_7(\mathfrak C_Y^\bullet)\\
\ell_9^Y(\mathfrak C_Y^\bullet)
\end{pmatrix}.

Rank-two requires:

\mathbf b_Y(\mathfrak C_Y^L)=
\mathbf b_Y(\mathfrak C_Y^N)=
\mathbf b_Y(\mathfrak C_Y^W)=0.
}
\]

Expected:

* \(\mathfrak C_Y^L=0\) if \(L\) is affine or wrong-origin.
* \(\mathfrak C_Y^N=0\) if \(D^2N_0\) is naturally transported.
* \(\mathfrak C_Y^W=0\) if whitening origin labels distinguish moving-whitening artifacts from \(DW_{\same}[D^2N_0]\).

If any piece is finite coherent but not local, it enters amplitude Schur, not \(B_Y\). If local graph-like, rank-one. If local vertical/second-slope, death.

---

# 7. Singular nuisance: live-pair survival

For \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\), rank-two needs both live vectors to survive:

\[
e_7\notin\mathcal N_{\sing},
\qquad
e_Y\notin\mathcal N_{\sing},
\]

and no nuisance generator has nonzero projection to the live fiber.

Equivalently:

If \(\mathcal N_{\sing}\) kills only one direction:

* if it kills \(e_Y\), \(Y12\) dies;
* if it kills \(e_7\), rank-two fails and the remaining \(Y12\) channel may still be rank-one depending on the quotient;
* if it identifies \(e_Y\) with \(e_7\), quotient overcollapse.

So live-pair Gram and nuisance filtering must be checked before rank selection.

---

# 8. The rank-two certificate

Collect all local actual/nuisance contaminant candidates:

\[
\{u_j\}.
\]

Form the \(2\times M\) matrix

\[
B_Y^{\mathrm{matrix}}
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\ell_7(u_1)&\cdots&\ell_7(u_M)\\
\ell_9^Y(u_1)&\cdots&\ell_9^Y(u_M)
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

\[

and the live D4 matrix is conditioned:

\[

|c_7\alpha_9|\ge Q^{-A},
\qquad
|c_7|,|\alpha_9|,|\beta_9|\le Q^A.
}
\]

Then

\[
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}
\]

is polynomially equivalent to

\[
H
\begin{pmatrix}
\Gamma_G\\
\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

This yields vector wall D4.

---

## One graph line

\[
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=1,
\qquad
\operatorname{ran}B_Y=\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Rank-one moving wall.

## Vertical

\[
(0,1)\in\operatorname{ran}B_Y.

The vertical \(Y12\) direction is actual-source/local freedom. Current \(Y12\) route dies.

## Rank two

\[
\operatorname{rank}B_Y=2.
\]

The local fiber is fully absorbed. Current \(Y12\) route dies.

## Nonvertical line not tied to \(s_7\)

If

\[
\operatorname{ran}B_Y=\mathbf C(a,b)
\]

with \(a\neq0\), rewrite as

\[
\mathbf C(1,b/a).
\]

This is rank-one with slope

\[
\rho_Y=b/a
\]

provided the slope is formal/coherent and low-order. If the slope is fitted/local, route is suspect.

---

The rank-two audit reduces to a finite zero matrix test:

B_Y^{\mathrm{matrix}}=0.
}

Agent 2’s contribution is the \(s_9^Y\)-coordinate test:

\]

for all actual-source/nuisance/local candidate generators \(u_j\).

\[

under fixed-\(\alpha\) slot-rank exclusion,

by first-live primitive \(Y12\),

if \(E_2^Y\) is absent/fixed/finite coherent/residual,
