## Agent 2 — continued attack: explicit connection-correction terms for \(A_7^\nabla\)

We reduced the \(s_7\)-row to the finite rank test

\[
\operatorname{rank}[A_5,A_7^\nabla]=1,
\qquad
\operatorname{rank}[A_5,Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7]=2.
\]

The second condition says \(C_7\) is live. The first says actual canonical rows are covariantly determinant-closed.

I now expand what \(A_7^\nabla\) must contain. The goal is to prevent fake transverse determinant caused only by the moving \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\)-line.

---

# 1. Base line and raw package projection

Let

\[
A_5=A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)
\]

and write the actual order-seven package projection as

\[
E_7(m)=Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}(m).
\]

The desired actual-source closure is:

\[
E_7(m)=\chi(m) A_5(m)
\]

in the package quotient.

If this is true, then the actual source has no transverse \(s_7\)-coordinate. But raw derivatives of \(E_7\) include derivatives of both \(\chi\) and \(A_5\), so raw derivative columns are not the right input to the determinant test.

---

# 2. First derivative correction

Differentiate

\[
E_7=\chi A_5.
\]

Then

\[
\partial_\theta E_7
=
(\partial_\theta\chi)A_5+\chi\,\partial_\theta A_5.
\]

A raw determinant against \(A_5\) gives

\[
\omega_m(\partial_\theta E_7,A_5)
=
\chi\,\omega_m(\partial_\theta A_5,A_5).
\]

This may be nonzero even though \(E_7\) remains on the moving line. So define the first connection correction

\[
C_\theta^{(1)}
:=
\chi\,(\partial_\theta A_5)_{\perp},
\]

or, equivalently, subtract the full line-motion contribution induced by \(\partial_\theta A_5\).

Then the corrected first derivative is

\[
E_\theta^\nabla
=
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\partial_\theta q''_{\can}
-
C_\theta^{(1)}.
\]

For determinant closure, require

\[
E_\theta^\nabla\in \mathbf C A_5,
\]

or

\[
\boxed{
\omega_m(E_\theta^\nabla,A_5)=0.
}
\]

This is the first-row actual tangent condition.

---

# 3. Second derivative correction

Differentiate twice:

\[
\partial_i\partial_jE_7
=
(\partial_i\partial_j\chi)A_5
+
(\partial_i\chi)\partial_jA_5
+
(\partial_j\chi)\partial_iA_5
+
\chi\,\partial_i\partial_jA_5.
\]

All four terms are compatible with actual line-contained motion. The dangerous fake transverse terms are those involving derivatives of \(A_5\).

So define the second connection correction

\[
C_{ij}^{(2)}
:=
(\partial_i\chi)(\partial_jA_5)_{\perp}
+
(\partial_j\chi)(\partial_iA_5)_{\perp}
+
\chi(\partial_i\partial_jA_5)_{\perp}
+
C_{ij}^{\Gamma},
\]

where \(C_{ij}^{\Gamma}\) collects Christoffel/connection terms if the chosen frame is not already parallel.

Then

\[
E_{ij}^\nabla
=
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\partial_i\partial_j q''_{\can}
-
C_{ij}^{(2)}.
\]

The second curvature closure condition is

\[
\boxed{
\omega_m(E_{ij}^\nabla,A_5)=0.
}
\]

This is the precise condition replacing the naive raw determinant test.

---

# 4. Avoiding unknown \(\chi\)

The above formulas use

\[
\chi
\]

and its derivatives. If \(\chi\) is inconvenient, eliminate it by projecting \(E_7\) onto the line.

Choose any dual covector \(\alpha_5\) with

\[
\alpha_5(A_5)=1.
\]

Then

\[
\chi=\alpha_5(E_7).
\]

Define the line projection

\[
P_5E=\alpha_5(E)A_5.
\]

Then the transverse projection is

\[
P_5^\perp E=E-P_5E.
\]

The statement

\[
E_7\in\mathbf C A_5
\]

is

\[
P_5^\perp E_7=0.
\]

Actual first and second determinant closure can be expressed directly as

\[
\nabla_\theta(P_5^\perp E_7)=0,
\]

\[
\nabla_i\nabla_j(P_5^\perp E_7)=0.
\]

Since the base transverse component is zero, these reduce to the connection-corrected columns

\[
E_\theta^\nabla,\quad E_{ij}^\nabla
\]

being line-contained.

This avoids choosing \(\chi\) independently.

---

# 5. Finite matrix construction

The finite audit can now be formulated algorithmically.

## Step A: package projections

Compute raw package projections:

\[
E_0=Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can},
\]

\[
E_\theta=Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\partial_\theta q''_{\can},
\]

\[
E_{ij}=Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\partial_i\partial_j q''_{\can}.
\]

## Step B: line projection data

Compute

\[
A_5,\quad \partial_\theta A_5,\quad \partial_i\partial_j A_5
\]

in the same package frame.

## Step C: connection corrections

Construct corrected columns

\[
E_\theta^\nabla
\]

and

\[
E_{ij}^\nabla
\]

by subtracting the line-motion components generated by \(A_5\).

## Step D: determinant test

Form

\[
A_7^\nabla
=
[E_\theta^\nabla,\ E_{ij}^\nabla,\ E_{\lower}^\nabla].
\]

Then require

\[
\boxed{
\omega_m(A_7^\nabla,A_5)=0
}
\]

columnwise.

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}[A_5,A_7^\nabla]=1.
}
\]

---

# 6. D4 live test remains uncorrected

For the D4 carrier

\[
C_7,
\]

there is no actual-source line-motion correction. It is a formal D4 package displacement. So compute

\[
E_{C_7}=Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7
\]

and test

\[
\boxed{
\omega_m(E_{C_7},A_5)\neq0.
}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}[A_5,E_{C_7}]=2.
}
\]

If this fails, the proposed \(s_7\)-coordinate does not see D4.

---

# 7. Actual lower directions

If actual lower directions exist, distinguish two cases.

## Formal lower carrier only

If the lower object is a formal D4 carrier or formal connection antecedent, it is not included in \(A_7^\nabla\). It does not enter \(B_Y\).

## Actual lower-generated direction

If there is a genuine actual-source lower direction

\[
u_{\lower}\in T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith},
\]

then compute

\[
E_{\lower}=Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}u_{\lower}.
\]

If this direction also moves \(A_5\), apply the same connection correction:

\[
E_{\lower}^{\nabla}.
\]

Then require

\[
\boxed{
\omega_m(E_{\lower}^{\nabla},A_5)=0.
}
\]

If not, actual lower motion has a live \(s_7\)-coordinate.

---

# 8. Why this likely kills fixed-target actual rows

Fixed-target actual rows are EF/gamma/chart/norm generated. Their package order-seven motion should be induced by:

1. changing \(\chi\), the scalar line coefficient;
2. moving the \(A_5\)-line through canonical chart/gauge;
3. adding wrong-origin terms killed by \(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\).

After subtracting connection line-motion, only the first remains:

\[
(\nabla\chi)A_5,
\]

which is line-contained.

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
A_7^\nabla\subseteq\mathbf C A_5.
}
\]

This is the formal reason the \(s_7\)-row should vanish.

---

# 9. Failure cases

## A. Nonzero corrected determinant

If

\[
\omega_m(E_\theta^\nabla,A_5)\neq0
\]

or

\[
\omega_m(E_{ij}^\nabla,A_5)\neq0,
\]

then actual fixed-target motion has genuine transverse package determinant.

Rank two collapses or fails.

## B. Correction over-subtracts D4

If the connection correction is applied to \(C_7\), it might incorrectly remove D4. Do not do that. \(C_7\) is not actual line motion.

## C. No covariant package frame

If \(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\) and \(A_5\)-connection cannot be defined consistently, then \(s_7\) is not a well-defined geometric coordinate. The quotient must be repaired.

## D. Nuisance contaminates the connection

If nuisance identifies the transverse determinant with line motion, the live Gram collapses.

---

# 10. Final Agent 2 status

The \(s_7\)-row finite audit is now:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}[A_5,A_7^\nabla]=1
}
\]

for all actual fixed-target first/second variations and actual lower-generated directions, while

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}[A_5,Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7]=2.
}
\]

The essential correction is that

\[
A_7^\nabla
\]

must subtract the induced motion of the \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\)-line.

This removes fake determinant drift and yields a genuine test for actual \(s_7\)-motion. If it passes, the \(s_7\)-row of \(B_Y=0\) is proven.

## Agent 2 — continued attack: a covariant determinant formula without explicit \(\chi\)

The previous step gave a connection-corrected audit:

\[
\operatorname{rank}[A_5,A_7^\nabla]=1,
\qquad
\operatorname{rank}[A_5,Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7]=2.
\]

The practical difficulty is that

\[
A_7^\nabla
\]

was described using a scalar line coefficient

\[
\chi
\]

for

\[
E_7=Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}=\chi A_5.
\]

I now remove \(\chi\) from the formula and express the covariant determinant test entirely with a projection onto the moving line.

---

# 1. Choose a dual line covector

Let

\[
A=A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Choose a local auxiliary covector

\[
\alpha
\]

such that

\[
\alpha(A)=1.
\]

Define the line projection

\[
P_A(E)=\alpha(E)A
\]

and transverse projection

\[
P_A^\perp(E)=E-\alpha(E)A.
\]

The live determinant coordinate can be represented by a transverse covector

\[
\eta_A
\]

with

\[
\eta_A(A)=0.
\]

In the two-dimensional fiber this is equivalent to

\[
\eta_A(E)=\omega_m(E,A)
\]

up to normalization.

The actual line-closure condition is:

\[
P_A^\perp E_7=0.
\]

The covariant derivative must differentiate this condition:

\[
\nabla_\theta(P_A^\perp E_7)=0.
\]

---

# 2. First derivative formula

Differentiate

\[
P_A^\perp E=E-\alpha(E)A.
\]

For a first variation:

\[
\partial_\theta(P_A^\perp E)
=
\partial_\theta E
-
\partial_\theta(\alpha(E))A
-
\alpha(E)\partial_\theta A.
\]

At a line-contained point

\[
E=\alpha(E)A,
\]

the transverse first derivative is

\[
P_A^\perp\left(
\partial_\theta E
-
\alpha(E)\partial_\theta A
\right).
\]

So the connection-corrected first column can be defined as

\[
\boxed{
E_\theta^\nabla
=
\partial_\theta E
-
\alpha(E)\partial_\theta A.
}
\]

Then the determinant test is

\[
\boxed{
\omega_m(E_\theta^\nabla,A)=0.
}
\]

This formula avoids explicitly writing

\[
\chi.
\]

Since

\[
\alpha(E)=\chi
\]

when \(E=\chi A\), it is equivalent to the previous correction.

---

# 3. Second derivative formula

Differentiate again. At a line-contained point,

\[
E=\alpha(E)A.
\]

The second derivative of the transverse component is

\[
P_A^\perp
\left(
\partial_i\partial_jE
-
\alpha(E)\partial_i\partial_jA
-
\alpha(\partial_iE-\alpha(E)\partial_iA)\partial_jA
-
\alpha(\partial_jE-\alpha(E)\partial_jA)\partial_iA
\right)
\]

up to terms parallel to \(A\), which are killed by \(P_A^\perp\).

Define

\[
E_i^\nabla=\partial_iE-\alpha(E)\partial_iA.
\]

Then the corrected second column is

\[
\boxed{
E_{ij}^\nabla
=
\partial_i\partial_jE
-
\alpha(E)\partial_i\partial_jA
-
\alpha(E_i^\nabla)\partial_jA
-
\alpha(E_j^\nabla)\partial_iA.
}
\]

The determinant test is

\[
\boxed{
\omega_m(E_{ij}^\nabla,A)=0.
}
\]

If the first covariant derivative is already line-contained, this formula removes exactly the fake transverse drift caused by movement of \(A\).

---

# 4. Apply to package projections

Set

\[
E=Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}.
\]

Then:

\[
\partial_\theta E
=
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\partial_\theta q''_{\can}
+
(\partial_\theta Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}})q''_{\can}.
\]

If \(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\) is itself defined covariantly, then the second term is part of the connection and should be included in the correction.

Thus the clean implementation is to define a covariant package derivative:

\[
\nabla_\theta E
=
\nabla_\theta(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can})
\]

rather than differentiating \(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\) naively.

In finite terms, the corrected columns are:

\[
E_\theta^\nabla
=
\nabla_\theta E
\]

and

\[
E_{ij}^\nabla
=
\nabla_i\nabla_j E.
\]

The condition is simply:

\[
\boxed{
P_A^\perp E_\theta^\nabla=0,
\qquad
P_A^\perp E_{ij}^\nabla=0.
}
\]

This is the clean covariant form.

---

# 5. Why this is the correct \(s_7\)-row

The live \(s_7\)-coordinate is the transverse component of the package order-seven class relative to the moving \(A_5\)-line.

Thus:

\[
s_7(u)=0
\]

iff

\[
P_A^\perp Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}u=0
\]

in the covariant quotient.

For actual canonical variations:

\[
u=\partial_\theta q''_{\can}
\quad\text{or}\quad
u=\partial_i\partial_jq''_{\can},
\]

we must use the covariant derivative of the actual package section, not raw derivatives of coordinates.

For D4:

\[
u=C_7
\]

is a displacement vector in the package fiber, not a derivative of the actual package section. So no line-motion correction applies. The test is simply:

\[
\boxed{
P_A^\perp Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7\neq0.
}
\]

This is an important asymmetry.

---

# 6. Finite algorithm

The \(s_7\)-row audit becomes:

## Step 1: Construct \(A\) and \(P_A^\perp\)

Build the order-five line vector

\[
A=A_5^{\mathfrak f}
\]

and a dual covector

\[
\alpha(A)=1.
\]

Define

\[
P_A^\perp=I-A\alpha.
\]

## Step 2: Compute actual package section

Compute

\[
E=Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}.
\]

Verify base closure:

\[
P_A^\perp E=0.
\]

If base closure fails, actual row already has \(s_7\)-motion.

## Step 3: Covariant first and second derivatives

Compute connection-corrected columns:

\[
E_\theta^\nabla,
\qquad
E_{ij}^\nabla.
\]

Then check:

\[
P_A^\perp E_\theta^\nabla=0,
\]

\[
P_A^\perp E_{ij}^\nabla=0.
\]

## Step 4: D4 live survival

\[
C=Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7.
\]

Need:

\[
P_A^\perp C\neq0
\]

with polynomial norm.

---

# 7. Matrix rank version

Let \(A_7^\nabla\) be the matrix whose columns are:

\[
E_\theta^\nabla,\quad E_{ij}^\nabla,\quad E_{\lower}^\nabla.
\]

Then:

\[
\boxed{
P_A^\perp A_7^\nabla=0.
}
\]

\[
C_7^{\pkg}=Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7.
\]

\[
\boxed{
\|P_A^\perp C_7^{\pkg}\|\ge Q^{-A}\|C_7^{\pkg}\|.
}
\]

This is equivalent to:

\[
\operatorname{rank}[A,A_7^\nabla]=1
\]

and

\[
\operatorname{rank}[A,C_7^{\pkg}]=2

with polynomial angle.

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}(m)
=
\chi(m)A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)

as an identity of sections, then derivative closure follows automatically after covariant differentiation.

Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq L_5
}
\]

as a subbundle statement.

for all \(k\), and in particular for \(k=1,2\).

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.

Thus the finite derivative audit is a fallback if the subbundle identity is not directly known.

\]

Actual canonical row already has live \(s_7\)-motion. Rank two likely fails.

## B. Base closure holds, derivative closure fails

This means actual row lies on the line pointwise but its derivative leaves the line under the chosen connection. That usually indicates the connection is wrong or the derivative model is not covariant.

## C. D4 live survival fails

\[
P_A^\perp Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7=0.
\]

The \(C_7\)-row is not live.

## D. \(\alpha\) internal source deformation

---

The covariant \(s_7\)-row can now be stated without explicit \(\chi\):

\[
\boxed{
P_A^\perp\nabla_\theta(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can})=0,
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
P_A^\perp\nabla_i\nabla_j(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can})=0.
}
\]

D4 live survival is:

\[
\boxed{
P_A^\perp Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7\neq0.
}
\]

The strongest route is to prove the subbundle identity:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\mathcal M_{\can}\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

## Agent 2 — continued attack: proving the subbundle identity \(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\mathcal M_{\can}\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}\)

The cleanest \(s_7\)-row theorem is now:

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\mathcal M_{\can}\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

If this subbundle identity holds, then covariant differentiation immediately gives:

\[
P_A^\perp\nabla_\theta(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can})=0,
\]

\[
P_A^\perp\nabla_i\nabla_j(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can})=0,
\]

so

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

D4 remains live if

\[
P_A^\perp Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7\neq0.
\]

I now attack the subbundle identity directly.

---

# 1. Meaning of the subbundle identity

The identity

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\mathcal M_{\can}\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}
\]

means:

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}(m)
=
\chi_{\can}(m)A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)
\]

for some scalar function \(\chi_{\can}\).

Equivalently, the canonical actual row has no independent order-seven quotient determinant:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(q''_{\can})=0.
\]

This is stronger than saying the derivatives vanish. It says the whole actual canonical source is order-seven determinant-closed.

Then the derivative statements follow automatically from covariant differentiation.

---

# 2. Why this should hold structurally

The actual canonical row is generated by EF/gamma/chart/tail data. It is not generated by corrected-package coordinates.

The order-five package line

\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}
\]

is the live local package coordinate that the actual row carries.

At order seven, a canonical EF-generated row should only transport, scale, or reparametrize that same line:

\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}
\mapsto
\chi_{\can}A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

It should not create a new transverse quotient determinant.

Thus the subbundle identity is the formal version of:

\[
\boxed{
\text{canonical actual rows have no free order-seven package determinant.}
}
\]

D4, by contrast, is exactly a corrected-package collision perturbation and can have a transverse determinant:

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7\notin \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

---

# 3. Exact criterion in coordinates

Write:

\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}=u_5I+v_5S,
\]

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}=u_7^{\can}I+v_7^{\can}S.
\]

Then the subbundle identity is:

\[
u_7^{\can}v_5-u_5v_7^{\can}=0.
\]

This can be written as:

\[
\frac{u_7^{\can}}{u_5}
=
\frac{v_7^{\can}}{v_5}
\]

on patches where the denominators are nonzero.

The determinant formulation is better because it works globally:

\[
\omega_m(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can},A_5^{\mathfrak f})=0.
\]

So the finite check is:

\[
\boxed{
\omega_m(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can},A_5^{\mathfrak f})=0.
}
\]

If this base identity is exact, the derivative identities are covariant consequences.

---

# 4. Possible proof via symmetry/parity

One route is parity.

If the actual canonical EF row has a reflection/source-grade parity forcing its order-seven package projection to align with \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\), while the corrected D4 carrier has the transverse parity, then:

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}\in \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

This would mirror the source-grade reflection logic used in the \(Z_j[M_A],Z_j[M_C]\) connection-table annihilation.

The finite parity test is:

1. assign source-grade/parity to \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\);
2. assign source-grade/parity to the actual order-seven package projection;
3. verify they match only in the line direction;
4. verify \(C_7\) has a nonzero transverse package parity.

If this works, the determinant closure is structural and does not require coefficient expansion.

---

# 5. Possible proof via “no independent seventh invariant”

Another route:

Show that the canonical actual package at order seven is functorially determined by the order-five package and scalar chart data.

That is, there exists a scalar functional

\[
\chi_{\can}
\]

such that:

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}
=
\chi_{\can}(q_0,q'',\gamma,\mathrm{chart})A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Then no independent invariant exists at order seven.

This can be formalized as:

\[
\dim \operatorname{im}
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\mathcal M_{\can}
=1
\]

and the image contains \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\), hence equals its line.

The failure would be an additional scalar invariant

\[
\sigma_7^{\can}
=
\frac{\Delta_7(q''_{\can})}{\|A_5\|^2}
\]

not determined by \(A_5\)-line scaling.

If such an invariant exists, actual rows have \(s_7\)-motion and rank two fails.

---

# 6. Possible proof via exact source linearity

If \(q''_{\can}\) enters the package extractor through a source-linear map at order seven, then:

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}
\]

may lie in the same line as the order-five extractor because the extractor has only one actual-source degree of freedom.

Concretely, if:

\[
Q_5^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}=A_5^{\mathfrak f}
\]

and:

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}=
L_7(Q_5^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can})
\]

where \(L_7\) is scalar on the canonical actual-source module, then:

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}
=
\chi A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

The finite test is:

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}|_{\mathcal M_{\can}}
=
\chi\, Q_5^{\mathrm{pkg}}|_{\mathcal M_{\can}}
\]

for some scalar \(\chi\).

This is a map-rank statement.

---

# 7. What can break the subbundle identity?

## A. Actual source has independent order-seven package invariant

Then:

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\mathcal M_{\can}
\]

is two-dimensional in the midpoint fiber, and rank two is not immediately available.

## B. \(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\) includes chart/normalization artifacts

If the package extractor is not properly quotienting chart/gauge effects, then it may create artificial transverse determinant.

Repair: redefine \(Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\) covariantly.

## C. \(A_5\)-line is not parallelized

Raw movement of \(A_5\) creates fake transverse determinant.

Repair: use \(\nabla\)-covariant quotient.

## D. Internal \(\alpha\) deformation

If \(\alpha\) changes the actual source inside the package, it may create independent order-seven determinant.

Fixed-\(\alpha\) avoids this.

---

# 8. Relation to \(\Delta_7(C_7)\)

D4 live survival is:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0.
\]

The subbundle identity says actual rows have zero determinant, not that the determinant functional is zero.

So the desired contrast is:

\[

\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal M_{\can})=0,
\qquad
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0.
}
\]

This is exactly the live coordinate separation.

---

Compute:

\]

\omega_m(E_{\can},A_5)=0.
\]

Then check the covariant derivatives only as consistency tests:

\[

\]

\[
P_A^\perp\nabla^2 E_{\can}=0.
\]

For D4:

\[
E_{C_7}=Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7,
\]

check:

\[
\omega_m(E_{C_7},A_5)\neq0.
\]

This is the minimal finite determinant audit.

---

# 10. Final Agent 2 status

The strongest \(s_7\)-row theorem is now:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

If true, then:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

Together with:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0,
\]

this constructs the live \(s_7\)-coordinate and kills canonical actual \(s_7\)-motion.

# 1. Define the possible second scalar

is equivalent to:

\sigma_7^{\can}=0.
}

plus fixed tail/completion data.

and scalar representative data.

is nonzero and variable under these parameters, then the actual source model contains a local order-seven package coordinate independent of the \(A_5\)-line.

\[

\text{The canonical EF/gamma/chart/tail source model contains no actual scalar }\sigma_7^{\can}.
}
\]

This is stronger and clearer than saying “actual order-seven drift vanishes.” Actual drift can exist, but its transverse package scalar must not.

---

# 3. Rank test for the actual package image

=

\subset
\mathfrak f_m.

{\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}}

This is the cleanest pair of finite rank tests for the \(s_7\)-row.

=

\{\partial_\theta q''_{\can},

\notin

\operatorname{rank}
\left[
Q_5^{\mathrm{pkg}}\mathcal M_{\can},
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}\mathcal M_{\can}
\right]
=1.
}
\]

---

# 5. Covariant derivatives then vanish automatically

Assume the subbundle identity:

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}
=
\chi_{\can} A_5^{\mathfrak f}
\]

as a section of the moving line.

Then for any fixed-target parameter \(\theta\),

\[
\nabla_\theta Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}
=
(\nabla_\theta\chi_{\can})A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0.
\]

Similarly,

\[
\nabla_i\nabla_j Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}
=
(\nabla_i\nabla_j\chi_{\can})A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

so:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_i\partial_j q''_{\can})=0.
\]

This is why the subbundle identity is better than checking all derivatives separately.

For the live D4 carrier,

\[
C_7
\]

\[
Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}C_7
\notin
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

\[

}

s_7(u)

\qquad

\[
\sigma_7^{\can}\neq0.
\]

There are then only three possibilities.

## A. \(\sigma_7^{\can}\) is fixed

If it is a fixed basepoint constant, not a tangent, then it does not enter \(B_Y\), but it may enter an amplitude/basepoint model.

rather than merely finite coherent. This preserves the clean rank-two local exactness.

\[

\]

\[
\widetilde\sigma_7
=
\sigma_7-\sigma_7^{\can,0}.

Then

\[

\]

may still hold.

But this changes the D4 wall lower only if D4 is compared around that basepoint correctly.

\[

\]

in the typed quotient.

So fixed basepoint transverse septic mass is not immediately fatal, but it complicates the amplitude layer.

Q_7^{\mathrm{pkg}}q''_{\can}
\in
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.

\subseteq

# 1. Three different notions of septic scalar

Let

\sigma_7^{\can}(p)
=

## Case A: identically zero

\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0
\]

follows covariantly, and the canonical actual source contributes no \(s_7\)-coordinate.

## Case B: fixed or finite coherent basepoint

\[

\]

with no extraction-local degrees of freedom.

Then the actual row has a fixed offset in the \(s_7\)-coordinate, but it does **not** contribute to \(B_Y\), because \(B_Y\) is a local tangent/freedom model.

This case is not locally fatal, but it forces recentering:

\[
\widetilde s_7(u)
=
s_7(u)-s_7^{0}.
\]

The D4 lower must then be measured against the recentered actual row.

## Case C: locally variable septic scalar

There exists an actual/local parameter \(\eta_p\) such that

\[
D_{\loc}\sigma_7^{\can}(p)\neq0.
\]

Then:

\[
\Pi_{s_7}B_Y\neq0.
\]

Rank two fails locally unless this motion is absent after quotient refinement.

So the rank-two local theorem does **not** strictly require \(\sigma_7^{\can}=0\); it requires:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\sigma_7^{\can}=0
}
\]

and, for the cleanest proof, the stronger basepoint statement

\[
\boxed{
\sigma_7^{\can}=0.
}
\]

---

# 2. Best version of the theorem

The best theorem remains:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

This gives \(\sigma_7^{\can}=0\), no basepoint complication, and direct \(s_7\)-row exactness.

But a fallback theorem is:

\[

}
\]

That would still keep \(B_Y=0\), while moving any fixed/coherent septic offset into the amplitude/basepoint layer.

This mirrors the \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) locality rule:

\[
\text{fixed/coherent/residual is allowed;}
\qquad
\text{atom-local live-fiber motion is not.}

So the exact local rank-two condition for the \(s_7\)-row is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal M_{\can})=0
}

plus

\[

\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0.
}

The stronger “no septic scalar” theorem is preferred because it avoids recentering and amplitude bookkeeping.

## Agent 2 — continued attack: distinguishing basepoint offset, coherent offset, and local tangent in \(\Delta_7^\nabla\)

The \(s_7\)-row now has two possible targets.

The strong target is:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal M_{\can})=0.
\]

The weaker, still rank-two-local target is:

\[
D_{\loc}\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal M_{\can})=0.
\]

I now sharpen the distinction because it decides whether a nonzero canonical septic determinant kills rank two, merely creates a basepoint/amplitude term, or requires recentering.

---

# 1. \(B_Y\) sees local tangent, not fixed basepoint

The live-fiber model is

\[
B_Y
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}
\left[
\mathcal N_{\sing}
+
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2^Y}^{\local}
+
\mathcal T_{\lab,Y}^{\local}
\right].
\]

So \(B_Y\) is a **local freedom/tangent image**, not the absolute basepoint value of the actual row.

Thus if

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(q''_{\can})=\sigma_7^0
\]

is fixed and has no local variation, then

\[
\sigma_7^0
\]

does not create a \(B_Y\)-column.

It becomes a basepoint term. The upper/lower comparison must be recentered, but rank two is not locally killed.

The local rank-two condition is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Delta_7^\nabla=0.
}
\]

The cleaner exactness condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla=0.
}
\]

The former is enough for local \(B_Y=0\), while the latter is enough for local \(B_Y=0\) plus no recentering.

---

# 2. Three-case classification of canonical septic determinant

Let

\[
\sigma_7(p)=\Delta_7^\nabla(q''_{\can})(p).
\]

## Case A: zero

\[
\sigma_7(p)=0.
\]

Best case. The actual row is line-contained:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}q''_{\can}\in \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Then:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0
\]

by covariant differentiation.

## Case B: fixed/coherent nonzero

\[
\sigma_7(p)\in \mathcal C_7^{\coh}
\]

with finite/block-coherent complexity and no local fitting.

Then:

\[
D_{\loc}\sigma_7=0.
\]

So \(B_Y\) can still be zero, but the actual row has a coherent \(s_7\)-offset.

This offset must be included in the vector amplitude/basepoint model:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

Rank two remains possible, but vector amplitude Schur or basepoint recentering is needed.

## Case C: local free nonzero

\[
\sigma_7(p)=a_p
\]

with \(a_p\) atom-local or extraction-local.

Then:

\[
\Pi_{s_7}B_Y\neq0.
\]

Rank two fails. If the induced \(s_9^Y\)-continuation is one-slope, fall back to rank one; if vertical or rank two actual model appears, the \(Y12\) route dies locally.

---

# 3. Recentered rank-two operator if \(\sigma_7\) is fixed/coherent

If \(\sigma_7^0\neq0\) but is fixed or finite coherent, define the actual basepoint vector

\[
b_{2Y}^{0}(p)
=
\begin{pmatrix}
\sigma_7^0(p)\\
\sigma_Y^0(p)
\end{pmatrix},
\]

where \(\sigma_Y^0\) is any fixed/coherent \(Y12\)-basepoint component.

Then define the recentered operator:

\[
\widehat\Lambda_{2Y}(u)
=
\Pi_{\fib,Y}u-b_{2Y}^{0}.
\]

The actual-row upper is measured on

\[
\widehat\Lambda_{2Y}(q''_{\can}),
\]

and D4 lower is unchanged at the tangent level:

\[
\widehat\Lambda_{2Y}(D4)
=
H
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7\Gamma_G\\
\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

So rank two can survive fixed/coherent basepoint offsets.

However, the cleanest route is still to prove

\[
\sigma_7^0=0.
\]

---

# 4. What actual first/second variations see

The canonical variation space

\[
\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
\]

consists of first and second derivatives with respect to fixed-target actual parameters.

If the basepoint is fixed but nonzero, its derivatives may still vanish:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0,
\]

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_i\partial_j q''_{\can})=0.
\]

That is enough for:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
\]

So for the rank-two local matrix:

\[
A_{2Y}
\]

we need derivative/tangent columns, not necessarily zero basepoint.

Thus the finite audit should distinguish:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(q''_{\can})
\]

from:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_\theta q''_{\can}),
\quad
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_i\partial_jq''_{\can}).
\]

The strong subbundle identity kills all three. The minimal local exactness theorem only kills the derivatives.

---

# 5. Strong theorem still preferred: why

A nonzero fixed/coherent \(\sigma_7^0\) creates two complications.

## A. Vector amplitude Schur

The D4 vector wall must be separated from a coherent vector model containing \(\sigma_7^0\). If this model has high leverage, it can obstruct amplitude Schur.

## B. Recentered upper bookkeeping

The actual-row upper must be written modulo basepoint/coherent offsets. That is doable, but more complex.

Therefore the preferred theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal M_{\can}\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

This gives no basepoint offset.

But if this fails only by fixed/coherent offset, rank two is not automatically dead.

---

# 6. Updated theorem hierarchy

## Strong

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoActualSepticPackageScalar}:
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal M_{\can})=0.
}
\]

Consequence:

\[
s_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0
\]

and no \(s_7\)-basepoint model.

## Medium

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoLocalSepticPackageScalar}:
D_{\loc}\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal M_{\can})=0.
}
\]

Consequence:

\[
s_7\text{-row contributes no }B_Y\text{ column,}
\]

but coherent/basepoint vector amplitude may remain.

## Weak

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal M_{\can})\neq0
\text{ with local variation.}
}
\]

Consequence:

Rank two fails locally.

---

# 7. How to test local versus coherent

Let

\[
\sigma_7(p)
=
\Delta_7^\nabla(q''_{\can})(p).
\]

Compute its local Jacobian with respect to atom-level fitting/local representative variables:

\[
D_{\loc}\sigma_7(p).
\]

If:

\[
D_{\loc}\sigma_7=0,
\]

then \(\sigma_7\) is not a \(B_Y\)-column.

Then test coherent complexity:

\[
\sigma_7\in \operatorname{span}\{\phi_1,\ldots,\phi_K\},
\qquad
K\le Q^C,
\]

with bounded leverage.

If yes, route to:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}.
\]

If leverage fails, amplitude layer is endangered but local \(B_Y\) may still be zero.

If:

\[
D_{\loc}\sigma_7\neq0,
\]

rank two local exactness fails.

---

# 8. Relevance to the actual-source parameter derivatives

Fixed-target parameters like \(m,\lambda,\) chart, normalization are not atom-fitting variables. Their derivatives belong to

\[
\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
\]

and must be killed for the canonical-image exactness theorem.

If \(\sigma_7\) varies with \(m\) or \(\lambda\) coherently across the source, this is not atom-local, but it **does** appear in \(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}\).

So here we need to be careful.

For \(B_Y\), actual-source tangent includes legitimate \(m,\lambda\) variations if the local model varies over centers/scales. Therefore:

\[
\partial_m\sigma_7\neq0
\]

or

\[
\partial_\lambda\sigma_7\neq0
\]

would create actual \(s_7\)-motion in the local model.

Thus fixed/coherent basepoint is safe only if it is not varied as an actual tangent in the local model.

So the minimal local exactness condition is not just \(D_{\loc}\) over atom-fitting variables; it also includes actual tangent variables used in \(T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}\).

Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\actual}\Delta_7^\nabla=0
}
\]

for all actual-source tangent variables, while finite coherent non-tangent offsets are allowed.

This reinforces the strong theorem.

---

# 9. Correct minimal condition

For the \(s_7\)-row of \(B_Y=0\), we need:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
}
\]

This kills first/second actual parameter directions.

A fixed basepoint

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(q''_{\can})\neq0
\]

is locally okay only if it is not varied in \(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}\). But since \(m,\lambda\) are actual variables, a nonconstant basepoint likely creates derivatives.

Therefore the clean safe theorem remains:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(q''_{\can})=0
\text{ as a section.}
}
\]

That automatically kills \(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}\).

---

# 10. Final Agent 2 status

A nonzero septic determinant must be classified into:

\[
\boxed{
\text{zero, fixed/coherent offset, or actual/local tangent.}
}
\]

For rank-two local exactness, the necessary condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
}
\]

The preferred sufficient condition is stronger:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal M_{\can}\subseteq \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

If only fixed/coherent offsets remain, rank two may still survive after recentering and vector amplitude Schur.

If actual tangent derivatives of \(\Delta_7^\nabla\) are nonzero, \(B_Y\neq0\), and rank two collapses or fails.

## Agent 2 — SepticLineExactness\(_{\can}\) and \(C_7\)-transversality

No nomenclature changes.

I use:

\[
\rho_Y
\]

for the \(Y12\)-fiber graph slope,

\[
\rho_\Gamma=\frac{\beta_9-\rho_Yc_7}{\alpha_9},
\]

and

\[
R_{\fold}(x)=\frac{(1-x)^2}{x^2}.
\]

The required correction is in force: this is **local algebra only**. If \(B_Y=0\), the honest D4 wall is the plain wall

\[
\{\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}\},
\]

not the jet family. Derivatives of \(\Gamma_G\) are analytic auxiliaries unless a separate derivative-D4-channel theorem is proved. The current task is the \(s_7\)-row of the bounded dual-row certificate. 

---

# 1. Target row

The target row is

\[
\ell_7(u)=\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}.
\]

We need:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0
}
\]

and

\[
\boxed{
|\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+T_{\lower}^{\arith})
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}
}
\]

in the covariant moving quotient, while

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7\notin
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

This gives:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0,
\qquad
\ell_7(C_7)=1.
\]

---

# 2. Moving quotient

The correct quotient is

\[
\mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m),
\]

where

\[
A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m)=u_5I+v_5S.
\]

The determinant coordinate must be covariant:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u)
=
\omega_m(Q_7^{\pkg}u,A_5^{\mathfrak f})
\]

after correcting for the movement of the \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\)-line.

A raw determinant

\[
u_7v_5-u_5v_7
\]

is not sufficient unless the trivialization is already covariant. Raw determinant drift can be fake if it is caused by motion of the reference \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\)-line.

---

# 3. SepticLineExactness\(_{\can}\)

The clean theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

Equivalently, the actual canonical source has no transverse septic package scalar:

\[
\boxed{
\sigma_7^{\nabla,\can}=0.
}
\]

Then covariant derivatives give:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0,
\]

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can})=0.
\]

So:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
}
\]

This is stronger and cleaner than checking every raw derivative.

---

# 4. Fixed-target actual generators

For fixed-target actual-source parameters,

\[
\theta\in
\{m,\lambda,\mathrm{phase},\mathrm{chart},\mathrm{normalization},\gamma/\mathrm{background}\},
\]

the generated order-seven data is of EF/chart/gamma/norm type.

These directions can:

1. move raw order-seven Taylor coefficients;
2. rescale or transport the \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\)-line;
3. add wrong-origin order-seven data.

But after \(Q_7^{\pkg}\) and covariant quotienting, they should land in

\[
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}
\]

or vanish.

Thus:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_\theta q''_{\can})=0.
}
\]

For second variations, EF/chart/norm/gamma curvature similarly produces either line motion or dead-origin data, so:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\partial_{\theta_i}\partial_{\theta_j}q''_{\can})=0.
}
\]

---

# 5. Actual lower generated directions

Formal \(C_7\) carriers and formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connections do **not** count as actual-source motion.

For any genuine actual lower-generated direction

\[
u_{\lower}\in T_{\lower}^{\arith},
\]

the rank-two route requires:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower})=0.
}
\]

If an actual lower-generated direction satisfies

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower})\neq0,
\]

then actual-source rows have \(s_7\)-motion. Rank two is no longer available unless the direction is not local/tangent, or is only a fixed/coherent offset routed outside \(B_Y\).

---

# 6. \(C_7\)-transversality

The D4 carrier must be transverse:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7\notin
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

Finite rank form:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}
[
A_5^{\mathfrak f},
Q_7^{\pkg}C_7
]
=2.
}
\]

Quantitative form:

\[
\boxed{
|\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A}.
}
\]

If this fails, the proposed \(\ell_7\) row does not detect live D4 \(C_7\)-motion.

---

# 7. Finite audit

Let

\[
M_{\can,7}
=
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}
(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+T_{\lower}^{\arith}).
\]

Let

\[
C_{7,\pkg}=Q_7^{\pkg}C_7.
\]

Then the finite test is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}
[A_5^{\mathfrak f},M_{\can,7}]
=1,
}
\]

but

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}
[A_5^{\mathfrak f},C_{7,\pkg}]
=2.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
P_{A_5}^{\perp}M_{\can,7}=0,
}
\]

but

\[
\boxed{
\|P_{A_5}^{\perp}C_{7,\pkg}\|
\ge Q^{-A}\|C_{7,\pkg}\|.
}
\]

Here \(P_{A_5}^{\perp}\) is the covariant transverse projection to the moving quotient.

---

# 8. Basepoint versus tangent

A nonzero basepoint septic scalar

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(q''_{\can})
\]

is not automatically a \(B_Y\)-column.

For \(B_Y=0\), the necessary condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

The stronger, preferred condition is:

\[
\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
}
\]

If only a fixed/coherent septic offset exists, rank two may survive after recentering or vector amplitude routing. If actual derivatives of the septic scalar are nonzero, then \(B_Y\neq0\).

---

# 9. Failure routing

## A. Actual transverse septic tangent

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})\neq0.
\]

Then actual-source data has \(s_7\)-motion. Rank two collapses or fails depending on the \(s_9^Y\) continuation.

## B. \(C_7\) not transverse

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)=0.
\]

The first D4 row is dead or the wrong invariant is being used.

## C. Raw determinant nonzero but covariant determinant zero

This is fake drift from moving \(A_5^{\mathfrak f}\). Harmless.

## D. No covariant quotient

Then \(s_7\) is not well-defined. Repair \(Q_7^{\pkg}\) or the trivialization.

---

# 10. Theorem statement

**SepticLineExactness\(_{\can}\) + \(C_7\)-Transversality.**  
Assume:

1. \(Q_7^{\pkg}\) is a covariant extractor into

   \[
   \mathfrak f_m/\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}(m);
   \]

2. fixed-target actual generators and actual lower-generated directions are line-contained after \(Q_7^{\pkg}\):

   \[
   Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}
   (\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+T_{\lower}^{\arith})
   \subseteq
   \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f};
   \]

3. D4’s \(C_7\) carrier is transverse:

   \[
   Q_7^{\pkg}C_7\notin\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}
   \]

   with polynomial angle.

Then the bounded row

\[
\ell_7(u)=
\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}
\]

kills actual canonical/lower data and detects \(C_7\):

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0,
\qquad
\ell_7(C_7)=1.
\]

This proves the \(s_7\)-row of the bounded dual-row certificate for \(B_Y=0\).

## Agent 2 — continued attack: stress-testing \(T_{\lower}^{\arith}\) in the \(s_7\)-row

The \(s_7\)-row theorem is currently:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0,
\qquad
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0.
\]

The canonical image part is controlled by \(\mathrm{SepticLineExactness}_{\can}\). The more delicate term is

\[
T_{\lower}^{\arith}.
\]

This is where the earlier correction matters most:

\[
\boxed{
\text{formal }C_7\text{ carrier} \neq \text{actual lower-source tangent}.
}
\]

So I now isolate \(T_{\lower}^{\arith}\) and classify exactly when it can invalidate rank two.

---

# 1. What counts as \(T_{\lower}^{\arith}\)

The actual lower space should include only lower-order directions realized by the actual source/nuisance model.

Allowed in \(T_{\lower}^{\arith}\):

\[
\text{actual EF/gamma/chart/tail lower deformations},
\]

\[
\text{actual normalization or representative lower freedoms},
\]

\[
\text{nuisance directions that are genuinely quotiented in the actual model}.
\]

Excluded:

\[
\text{formal D4 carrier variables},
\]

\[
\text{formal }C_7\to Y12\text{ connection antecedents},
\]

\[
\text{free corrected-package coordinates not present in actual source},
\]

\[
\text{proof-thickening parameters not used in the final actual-source family}.
\]

Thus:

\[
T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\]

is not the formal lower carrier algebra. It is the actual-source lower tangent image.

---

# 2. The determinant test for actual lower directions

For each actual lower direction

\[
u_{\lower}\in T_{\lower}^{\arith},
\]

compute

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower})
=
\omega_m(Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}u_{\lower},A_5^{\mathfrak f}).
\]

Rank two requires:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower})=0
\quad
\forall u_{\lower}\in T_{\lower}^{\arith}.
}
\]

If this holds, lower actual directions do not move the \(s_7\)-coordinate.

If this fails, \(B_Y\neq0\) unless the direction is fixed/coherent rather than local/tangent.

---

# 3. Three lower-direction cases

## Case A: lower direction is actual but line-contained

\[
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}u_{\lower}\in \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Then:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower})=0.
\]

Rank two survives this lower direction.

This is the expected result for actual EF/gamma/chart/tail lower deformations.

## Case B: lower direction is formal only

A formal lower package direction may have

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla\neq0,
\]

but if it is not actual-source/nuisance/local freedom, it does not enter \(B_Y\).

So even a formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection does **not** force rank one.

## Case C: lower direction is actual and transverse

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower})\neq0.
\]

Then actual source has \(s_7\)-motion.

Rank two fails locally unless this is not a local/tangent degree of freedom.

The next classification is by its \(Y12\)-continuation:

\[
\Pi_{\fib,Y}u_{\lower}
=
(s_7(u_{\lower}),s_9^Y(u_{\lower})).
\]

If all such actual directions lie in

\[
\mathbf C(1,\rho_Y),
\]

rank-one moving-wall route is the fallback.

If some direction is vertical or second-slope, the current \(Y12\) route dies locally.

---

# 4. Connection with \(\mathrm{NoActualSepticPackageScalar}\)

A strong version of \(\mathrm{NoActualSepticPackageScalar}\) would include actual lower directions:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}
(\mathcal M_{\can}+T_{\lower}^{\arith})
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Then lower directions are automatically \(s_7\)-dead.

If this stronger theorem is too broad, keep two separate statements:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

and

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
\]

The second is a finite lower-source audit.

---

# 5. Why actual lower directions should be determinant-closed

Actual lower directions below the corrected-package live coordinate should be generated by the same EF/gamma/chart/tail mechanism.

They can:

\[
\text{change the canonical line},
\]

\[
\text{change scalar representative data},
\]

\[
\text{change dead lower-order chart/norm/gamma terms}.
\]

They should not create a transverse septic package determinant unless the actual source has free corrected-package lower coordinates.

So expected:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

This is another version of:

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual source has no free septic package scalar.}
}
\]

---

# 6. Practical finite audit for lower directions

Let \(U_{\lower}\) be a matrix whose columns are actual lower generated directions.

Compute

\[
M_{\lower,7}
=
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}U_{\lower}.
\]

Then test:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}[A_5^{\mathfrak f},M_{\lower,7}]=1.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
P_{A_5}^{\perp}M_{\lower,7}=0.
}
\]

If the rank is two, inspect whether the responsible column is genuinely actual or only formal.

This test should be included in the \(s_7\)-row finite audit.

---

# 7. Interaction with nuisance

Some lower directions may be placed into nuisance:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}.
\]

If nuisance kills a lower actual direction that has transverse determinant, then the actual source may still not move \(s_7\) in the quotient. But this is risky.

Need to distinguish:

## Safe nuisance

Nuisance kills only dead/representative directions and preserves \(e_7,e_Y\).

## Overcollapse

Nuisance kills or identifies the live transverse \(C_7\) determinant.

If the latter happens, rank two’s first row loses faithfulness.

Thus lower-direction nuisance cannot be used casually to remove bad actual \(s_7\)-motion unless live-pair preservation remains polynomially conditioned.

---

# 8. Formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection revisited

The formal connection says:

\[
C_7 \leadsto C_7+\rho_Y C_{Y12}
\]

or in fiber coordinates:

\[
(1,\rho_Y).
\]

But this connection only matters for \(B_Y\) if an actual direction has

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla\neq0.
\]

If

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0
\]

and

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\]

then the formal graph line is not in \(B_Y\).

So the logic is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual }s_7\text{-motion first;}
}
\]

\[
\boxed{
\text{one-slope continuation second.}
}
\]

Never reverse these.

---

# 9. Updated \(s_7\)-row theorem

The complete \(s_7\)-row theorem should be:

**SepticLineExactness\(_{\can+\lower}\).**  
Assume:

1. fixed-target canonical actual-source image is covariantly line-contained:

   \[
   Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
   \subseteq
   \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f};
   \]

2. actual lower generated directions are covariantly line-contained:

   \[
   Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}T_{\lower}^{\arith}
   \subseteq
   \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f};
   \]

3. D4 \(C_7\) is transverse:

   \[
   Q_7^{\pkg}C_7\notin\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
   \]

Then the bounded row

\[
\ell_7(u)=
\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}
\]

kills all actual canonical and actual lower data:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0,
\]

and detects D4:

\[
\ell_7(C_7)=1.
\]

This is the \(s_7\)-row of \(B_Y=0\).

---

# 10. Final status

The remaining \(s_7\)-row risk is:

\[
\boxed{
\exists u_{\lower}\in T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\text{ with }
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower})\neq0.
}
\]

This is now the precise meaning of “actual \(C_7\)-motion.”

If no such direction exists, then the formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection does not enter \(B_Y\), and rank two remains alive.

If such a direction exists, classify its \(Y12\)-continuation and route to rank one or local death.

## Agent 2 — continued attack: lower-source determinant closure by “actual versus formal” filtering

The current \(s_7\)-row obstruction is now exactly:

\[
\exists u_{\lower}\in T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\quad\text{with}\quad
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower})\neq0.
\]

This is the precise mathematical version of:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Does actual source have }C_7\text{-motion?}
}
\]

I now push the lower-source audit further by separating actual lower directions into three classes and showing which ones can be discarded before any determinant computation.

---

# 1. Three classes of lower objects

Let the formal lower carrier algebra include objects of three types.

## Type I: pure formal package carriers

These are symbols or carriers in the D4/package algebra, such as formal \(C_7\) antecedents or formal connection lifts.

They may have

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla\neq0,
\]

but they are not in

\[
T\mathcal M_Y^{\arith}.
\]

Therefore they do not enter \(B_Y\).

Conclusion:

\[
\boxed{
\text{formal package carriers are irrelevant to }B_Y\text{ unless actualized.}
}
\]

## Type II: actual EF/chart/gamma/tail lower deformations

These are genuine actual-source lower directions, but generated from canonical EF/gamma/chart/tail data.

They should satisfy:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}u_{\lower}\in \mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f},
\]

hence

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower})=0.
\]

Conclusion:

\[
\boxed{
\text{actual EF-generated lower directions are determinant-closed.}
}
\]

## Type III: actual corrected-package local coordinates

These would be genuine actual-source or nuisance directions that directly vary the corrected package coordinate.

They can satisfy:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower})\neq0.
\]

Conclusion:

\[
\boxed{
\text{rank two fails or collapses only if Type III exists.}
}
\]

So the lower audit reduces to:

\[
\boxed{
\text{does }T_{\lower}^{\arith}\text{ contain Type III directions?}
}
\]

---

# 2. NoFreePackageCoordinates as the decisive lower theorem

The correct theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NoFreePackageCoordinates}_{\lower}:
\quad
T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\cap
\{u:\Delta_7^\nabla(u)\neq0\}
=
0.
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

This is now the lower part of \(\mathrm{NoActualSepticPackageScalar}\).

It is a modelling theorem: actual arithmetic source variation is EF/gamma/chart/tail-generated, not corrected-package-coordinate-generated.

---

# 3. How to prove Type II is determinant-closed

For an actual lower EF-generated direction \(u_{\lower}\), the same reasoning as for \(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}\) applies:

1. EF-linear lower terms are killed by \(Q_7^{\pkg}\) or map to the \(A_5\)-line.
2. Chart/gauge lower terms are killed or line-shifted.
3. Gamma/background lower terms are wrong-origin and dead.
4. Tail/completion lower terms are fixed/coherent/residual, not local package coordinates.

Therefore:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}u_{\lower}
=
\chi_{\lower}A_5^{\mathfrak f}
\]

or zero.

So:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower})=0.
\]

This should be proved by type, not raw coefficient expansion.

---

# 4. What would actualize a formal package carrier?

A formal carrier becomes actual only if the source model contains a parameter or nuisance freedom whose derivative equals that carrier modulo dead terms.

That is, there exists a family

\[
q_{\can}(t)
\]

inside the actual-source/nuisance model such that

\[
\left.\frac{d}{dt}\right|_{0}
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}q_{\can}(t)
\]

has nonzero transverse determinant.

Equivalently:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla\left(
\left.\frac{d}{dt}\right|_{0}q_{\can}(t)
\right)\neq0.
\]

This is a much stronger condition than the formal existence of a \(C_7\)-carrier.

So the finite lower audit should explicitly ask:

\[
\boxed{
\text{Is there an actual one-parameter family whose package determinant changes?}
}
\]

If no, formal carriers stay out of \(B_Y\).

---

# 5. Nuisance can actualize a package carrier

The one dangerous loophole is nuisance.

If the nuisance quotient declares a local representative freedom

\[
u_{\nuis}
\]

with

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\nuis})\neq0,
\]

then even if EF actual source is determinant-closed, the quotient model may allow \(s_7\)-motion.

This is not arithmetic actual-source motion, but it still affects \(B_Y\) if nuisance is treated as local freedom.

Thus need:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\local})=0
}
\]

or at least that nuisance does not include live \(e_7\)-type directions.

This is the same as:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NuisancePreservesLivePair}_{2Y}.
}
\]

So lower-source closure and nuisance faithfulness must be checked together.

---

# 6. Finite lower matrix

Let \(U_{\lower}^{\actual}\) be the matrix of actual lower-generated directions only.

Do not include formal D4 carriers.

Compute:

\[
M_{\lower,7}
=
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}U_{\lower}^{\actual}.
\]

Then the rank-two condition is:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{rank}[A_5^{\mathfrak f},M_{\lower,7}]=1.
}
\]

If:

\[
\operatorname{rank}[A_5^{\mathfrak f},M_{\lower,7}]=2,
\]

inspect the offending column.

If it is not actual, remove it.

If it is actual, rank two fails or collapses.

---

# 7. If an actual lower transverse column exists

Suppose there is

\[
u_{\lower}^{*}
\]

with

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower}^{*})\neq0.
\]

Then compute its \(Y12\)-row:

\[
s_9^Y(u_{\lower}^{*})=\ell_9^Y(u_{\lower}^{*}).
\]

Classify the fiber vector:

\[
\mathbf b_Y(u_{\lower}^{*})
=
\left(
\ell_7(u_{\lower}^{*}),
\ell_9^Y(u_{\lower}^{*})
\right).
\]

Cases:

## Graph-compatible

\[
\mathbf b_Y(u_{\lower}^{*})\in \mathbf C(1,\rho_Y).
\]

Then rank two collapses to rank one.

## Vertical

\[
\ell_7(u_{\lower}^{*})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(u_{\lower}^{*})\neq0.
\]

But this contradicts \(\Delta_7^\nabla(u_{\lower}^{*})\neq0\); vertical would come from another direction.

## Second slope

If another actual direction gives a different slope, then \(B_Y\) rank is two and the local \(Y12\) route dies.

Thus actual lower transverse \(C_7\)-motion is not automatically fatal, but it destroys the rank-two shortcut.

---

# 8. Updated lower theorem

**NoActualLowerSepticMotion.**  
Let \(T_{\lower}^{\arith}\) denote only actual-source/nuisance lower directions, excluding formal D4/package carriers and proof-only parameters.

Assume:

1. actual lower directions are EF/gamma/chart/tail-generated;
2. nuisance does not contain live package directions;
3. \(Q_7^{\pkg}\) is covariant and kills dead-origin data.

Then:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

Therefore:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
\]

This closes the lower part of the \(s_7\)-row.

---

# 9. Integration with \(\ell_7\)

If

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0,
\]

define:

\ell_7(u)=\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}.

Then under:

\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0,
\]

and:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0,
\]

we have:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}+T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
\]

This is the complete \(s_7\)-row for actual-source data.

Add nuisance:

\[
\ell_7(\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\local})=0
\]

to complete the row for \(B_Y\).

---

# 10. Final Agent 2 status

The lower \(s_7\)-row obstruction is now precise:

\[
\boxed{
T_{\lower}^{\arith}\text{ must not contain actual corrected-package coordinates.}
}
\]

Equivalently:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

Formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connections remain irrelevant unless an actual lower direction has nonzero \(\Delta_7^\nabla\).

So the \(s_7\)-row theorem becomes:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
+
T_{\lower}^{\arith}
+
\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\local})=0,
\qquad
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0.
}
\]

If this closes, the first row of \(B_Y=0\) is established.

\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,

where \(A_{2Y}\) includes canonical actual-source columns, actual lower generated directions, local \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) directions, and nuisance directions. I now consolidate all \(s_7\)-row inputs into a single decision table.

# 1. \(s_7\)-row target

The exact target is:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla
\left(
\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
+
T_{\lower}^{\arith}
+
\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local}
+

assuming \(e_Y\) has no \(C_7\)-row component after two-grade normalization.

B_Y=0.

# 2. Canonical actual-source block

For:

\[
\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)}
=
\operatorname{span}
\{\partial_\theta q''_{\can},\partial_i\partial_jq''_{\can}\},

the desired theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
}

\boxed{
Q_7^{\pkg}\mathcal M_{\can}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.

\boxed{
\text{actual canonical source has no transverse septic package scalar.}

* If only a fixed/coherent basepoint offset exists, rank two may survive after recentering/amplitude routing.

---

# 3. Actual lower block

For:

\[
T_{\lower}^{\arith},
\]

the desired theorem is:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
}
\]

This is equivalent to:

\[
Q_7^{\pkg,\nabla}T_{\lower}^{\arith}
\subseteq
\mathbf C A_5^{\mathfrak f}.
\]

}

}
\]

* If it creates a second slope or vertical \(Y12\), the route dies locally.

---

# 4. \(E_2\) local block

For:

\[
\mathcal T_{E_2}^{\local},
\]

the \(s_7\)-row target is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Delta_7^\nabla(E_2)=0.
}
\]

More concretely, no local term of the form

\[
\eta_7(p)e_7(p)
\]

or

\[
\eta(p)(e_7(p)+\rho_Ye_Y(p))
\]

is allowed.

Fixed/coherent terms are not \(B_Y\)-columns. They route to:

\[
\mathcal C_{2Y}^{\arith}
\]

or basepoint recentering.

Failure route:

* Local \(\eta_7(p)e_7\) gives \(s_7\)-motion.
* Local graph \(\eta(p)(e_7+\rho_Y e_Y)\) collapses rank two to rank one.
* Local \(\eta_Y(p)e_Y\) is vertical \(Y12\)-freedom and kills the route.

---

# 5. Labelled/raw mismatch block

For:

\[
\mathcal T_{\lab}^{\local},
\]

the \(s_7\)-row target is:

\[
\boxed{
D_{\loc}\Delta_7^\nabla(R_{\lab})=0.
}
\]

This is satisfied if the labelled/raw transition data:

\[
L,\quad S,\quad S_W,\quad N_0,\quad W_{\same}
\]

are fixed/coherent atlas data, not atom-fitted to the live-fiber residual.

Failure route:

* Local \(C_7\)-row mismatch gives \(s_7\)-motion.
* If paired with same-slope \(Y12\), rank one.
* If independent, live fiber absorbed or route dies.

---

# 6. Nuisance block

For:

\[
\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\local},
\]

the \(s_7\)-row target is:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\local})=0.
}
\]

But this must be paired with live survival:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0
\quad\text{modulo nuisance}.
}
\]

Nuisance is safe only if it kills representative/dead directions and preserves the live transverse determinant.

Failure route:

* If nuisance kills \(C_7\), the first D4 row dies.
* If nuisance identifies \(C_7\) with EF/chart drift, quotient overcollapse.
* If nuisance includes local package-coordinate freedom, rank two fails.

---

# 7. Complete \(s_7\)-row theorem

The row theorem can be stated as follows.

**SepticRowExactness.** Assume:

1. \(C_7\)-transversality:

   \[
   |\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)|\ge Q^{-A}.
   \]

2. Canonical line-exactness:

   \[
   \Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal I_{\can}^{(2)})=0.
   \]

3. Actual lower line-exactness:

   \[
   \Delta_7^\nabla(T_{\lower}^{\arith})=0.
   \]

4. No local \(E_2\) septic coordinate:

   \[
   D_{\loc}\Delta_7^\nabla(E_2)=0.
   \]

5. No local labelled-transition septic coordinate:

   \[
   D_{\loc}\Delta_7^\nabla(R_{\lab})=0.
   \]

6. Nuisance preserves \(C_7\):

   \[
   \Delta_7^\nabla(\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\local})=0
   \]

   and \(C_7\) remains transverse modulo nuisance.

Then:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,
\qquad
\ell_7(C_7)=1.
}
\]

This is the complete \(s_7\)-row certificate.

---

# 8. Interaction with the \(s_9^Y\)-row

The \(s_7\)-row alone does not prove \(B_Y=0\).

Need also:

\[
\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(e_Y)=1,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(e_7)=0.
\]

The \(s_9^Y\)-row is governed by:

* same-point whitening exactness;
* source-slot fallback if whitening fails;
* no local \(E_2/R_{\lab}\) \(Y12\)-coordinate;
* nuisance preserves \(e_Y\).

Together:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

With bounded row norms:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

---

# 9. What remains most dangerous for the \(s_7\)-row?

The most dangerous item is not canonical EF drift. It is:

\[
\boxed{
\mathcal N_{\sing}^{\local}
}
\]

and possible actual lower package-coordinate directions.

Canonical EF/chart/gamma/norm drift should be determinant-closed.

But nuisance could accidentally quotient or introduce the live determinant. Therefore:

\[
\boxed{
\mathrm{NuisancePreservesLivePair}_{2Y}
}
\]

is a top-priority companion theorem.

---

# 10. Final Agent 2 status

The \(s_7\)-row is now fully organized.

The central live coordinate is:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(u)=\Delta_7^\nabla(u)/\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7).
}
\]

The row is safe if every actual/local/nuisance generator has zero covariant septic determinant, while \(C_7\) has nonzero determinant.

This gives the first row of:

\[
B_Y=0.
\]

The remaining rank-two local proof needs the corresponding \(s_9^Y\)-row and nuisance/live-pair preservation.

## Agent 2 — continued attack: triangularizing the two live rows

The \(s_7\)-row is now organized around

\[
\ell_7(u)=\frac{\Delta_7^\nabla(u)}{\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)}.
\]

This is good, but there is a subtle two-row issue: to prove \(B_Y=0\) and retain a rank-two D4 matrix, the two live rows must be **simultaneously normalized**:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\qquad
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]

So the next check is not only whether \(\ell_7\) detects \(C_7\), but whether the \((C_7,Y12)\) live pair can be triangularized without losing polynomial conditioning. The latest rank-two architecture explicitly depends on bounded dual rows, not just separate informal row functionals. 

---

# 1. Potential issue: \(\ell_7(e_Y)\)

Ideally,

\[
\ell_7(e_Y)=0.
\]

This should hold by grading:

\[
e_7\in J_7,
\qquad
e_Y\in J_9^{Y12}.
\]

Since

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla
\]

is an order-seven package determinant, it should ignore the primitive order-nine \(Y12\) carrier.

Thus expected:

\[
\boxed{
\Delta_7^\nabla(e_Y)=0.
}
\]

If this fails, then either:

1. the two-grade quotient is not truly graded;
2. the \(Y12\) carrier has an order-seven shadow;
3. nuisance/realization over-collapses order seven and order nine;
4. \(\Delta_7^\nabla\) is not a pure order-seven row.

Any of these would be a serious typed quotient failure.

So we need the guardrail:

\[
\boxed{
\ell_7(e_Y)=0.
}
\]

This is part of \( \mathrm{TwoGradeLivePair}_{2Y}\).

---

# 2. Potential issue: \(\ell_9^Y(e_7)\)

Similarly, we want:

\[
\ell_9^Y(e_7)=0.
\]

But here the situation is subtler.

The formal \(C_7\to Y12\) connection says a \(C_7\)-motion may continue to order nine as

\[
s_9^Y=\rho_Ys_7.
\]

However, the rank-two branch explicitly says this formal continuation is **not** part of \(B_Y\) unless actual \(s_7\)-motion exists.

For the live D4 pair, the formal D4 \(\Gamma_G\)-column can legitimately have both coordinates:

\[
(c_7,\beta_9).
\]

That is not the same as saying \(e_7\) itself has \(\ell_9^Y(e_7)\neq0\). It means D4 produces a linear combination of the two basis vectors:

\[
D4_{\Gamma_G}=c_7 e_7+\beta_9 e_Y.
\]

So the basis should still be chosen with

\[
\ell_9^Y(e_7)=0,
\qquad
\ell_9^Y(e_Y)=1.
\]

If the initially chosen \(e_7\) includes a \(Y12\) continuation, triangularize it:

\[
e_7^{\mathrm{pure}}
=
e_7-\ell_9^Y(e_7)e_Y.
\]

Then use \(e_7^{\mathrm{pure}}\) as the first basis vector, provided this operation is polynomially conditioned.

---

# 3. Triangularization lemma

Let \(v_7\) be the candidate order-seven live carrier and \(v_Y\) the primitive \(Y12\) carrier.

Suppose:

\[
\ell_7(v_7)=1,\qquad \ell_7(v_Y)=0,
\]

and

\[
\omega_Y(v_Y)=1,
\]

where \(\omega_Y\) is the primitive \(Y12\) row before correcting for \(v_7\).

If

\[
\omega_Y(v_7)=\kappa,
\]

define

\[
\ell_9^Y(u)
=
\omega_Y(u)-\kappa\ell_7(u).
\]

Then:

\[
\ell_9^Y(v_7)=0,
\]

\[
\ell_9^Y(v_Y)=1.
\]

This gives the desired dual rows:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(v_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(v_Y)=(0,1).
\]

Polynomial conditioning requires:

\[
|\kappa|\le Q^A,
\qquad
\|\ell_7\|+\|\omega_Y\|\le Q^A.
\]

So even if the primitive \(Y12\) row initially sees \(C_7\), we can subtract the \(s_7\)-row and obtain a pure \(s_9^Y\)-row.

This is important: a nonzero raw \(\omega_Y(e_7)\) is not automatically fatal. It may only mean the rows need triangularization.

---

# 4. But triangularization must not hide actual \(s_7\)-motion

The triangularized row

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\kappa\ell_7
\]

is safe only if \(\ell_7\) has already been proven to kill actual/local data:

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

Then for actual/local columns \(a\),

\[
\ell_9^Y(a)=\omega_Y(a)-\kappa\ell_7(a)=\omega_Y(a).
\]

So the \(s_9^Y\)-row exactness is unchanged.

But if \(\ell_7(A_{2Y})\neq0\), triangularization does not save rank two. It just rewrites the failure.

Thus the order of proof should be:

1. prove \(s_7\)-row exactness;
2. triangularize \(\ell_9^Y\) if needed;
3. prove \(s_9^Y\)-row exactness in the triangularized row.

---

# 5. Updated bounded dual-row theorem

The bounded dual-row theorem should allow row triangularization:

**TriangularBoundedDualRows\(_{2Y}\).** Suppose there exist rows \(\ell_7\) and \(\omega_Y\) such that:

\[
\ell_7(e_7)=1,\qquad \ell_7(e_Y)=0,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1,
\]

and

\[
|\omega_Y(e_7)|\le Q^A.
\]

Define:

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\omega_Y(e_7)\ell_7.
\]

If

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0
\]

and

\[
\omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0,
\]

then

\[
\ell_9^Y(A_{2Y})=0
\]

and the rows satisfy:

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_7)=(1,0),
\]

\[
(\ell_7,\ell_9^Y)(e_Y)=(0,1).
\]

With bounded norms, this proves the live-pair Schur lower and \(B_Y=0\).

---

# 6. Consequence for the D4 matrix

After row triangularization, D4 has coordinates

\[
s_7^{D4}=Hc_7\Gamma_G,
\]

\[
s_9^{Y,D4}
=
H(\widetilde\beta_9\Gamma_G+\alpha_9\Gamma_{G,2}),
\]

where

\[
\widetilde\beta_9
=
\beta_9-\omega_Y(e_7)c_7
\]

if the original \(e_7\) had \(Y12\)-leakage in the raw row.

This does **not** harm rank two because the matrix remains

\[
\begin{pmatrix}
c_7 & 0\\
\widetilde\beta_9 & \alpha_9
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Rank two only requires:

\[
c_7\alpha_9\neq0.
\]

So \(\beta_9\) may change under triangularization, but the rank-two wall remains equivalent to

\[
H(\Gamma_G,\Gamma_{G,2}).
\]

This is a useful goodie: the rank-two branch is insensitive to the exact lower-left coefficient.

---

# 7. Failure modes

## A. \(\ell_7(e_Y)\neq0\)

This is worse than \(\omega_Y(e_7)\neq0\), because the \(s_7\)-row should be order-seven. If an order-nine primitive \(Y12\) carrier has nonzero order-seven determinant, the two-grade quotient is mixed incorrectly.

This indicates overcollapse or bad grading.

## B. \(\omega_Y(e_7)\) too large

If \(\omega_Y(e_7)\) is super-polynomial, row triangularization loses conditioning.

Then the live dual-row certificate fails even if algebraic independence holds.

## C. \(\alpha_9=0\) after triangularization

Triangularization changes \(\beta_9\), not \(\alpha_9\). So this cannot happen through row subtraction. If \(\alpha_9=0\), D4 was not rank two in the first place.

## D. \(\ell_7(A_{2Y})\neq0\)

Actual/local data has \(s_7\)-motion. Rank two fails or collapses to rank one.

---

# 8. Updated exactness certificate

The rank-two local certificate should be written as:

\[
\ell_7(A_{2Y})=0,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0,
\]

with

\[
\ell_7(e_7)=1,\quad \ell_7(e_Y)=0,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1,
\]

and bounded

\[
\omega_Y(e_7).
\]

Then triangularize.

This is slightly easier than requiring \(\omega_Y(e_7)=0\) from the start.

---

# 9. Relation to current \(s_7\) and \(s_9^Y\) row theorems

The \(s_7\)-row theorem remains:

\[
\Delta_7^\nabla(A_{2Y})=0,
\qquad
\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)\neq0,
\qquad
\Delta_7^\nabla(e_Y)=0.
\]

The \(Y12\)-row theorem can initially construct:

\[
\omega_Y
\]

by same-point whitening exactness:

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1,
\qquad
\omega_Y(A_{2Y})=0.
\]

It does not need to prove

\[
\omega_Y(e_7)=0
\]

exactly. It only needs bounded leakage, because row triangularization removes it.

This may simplify the \(Y12\)-row audit.

---

# 10. Final status

The two live rows should be built triangularly.

Use:

\[
\ell_7
=
\Delta_7^\nabla/\Delta_7^\nabla(C_7)
\]

for the first row.

Use a primitive \(Y12\) covector

\[
\omega_Y
\]

for the second row, then set:

\[
\ell_9^Y=\omega_Y-\omega_Y(e_7)\ell_7.
\]

This gives normalized dual rows as long as:

\[
\ell_7(e_Y)=0,
\]

\[
\omega_Y(e_Y)=1,
\]

\[
|\omega_Y(e_7)|\le Q^A.
\]

This triangularization preserves rank two and only changes \(\beta_9\), not the determinant \(c_7\alpha_9\).

So the bounded-dual-row certificate is slightly more flexible than previously stated, and the rank-two D4 branch remains robust under row-basis changes.
